entry.name: IChartProps
entry.description_fallback: Props accepted by <OrigamChart> — the universal chart component supporting eight visualisation primitives (line, area, bar, column, scatter, pie, donut, radar, gauge). The type prop selects the path generator inside useChart. Extends ICommonsComponentProps, IDimensionProps, IMarginProps, IPaddingProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IBgColorProps.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartProps
    extends ICommonsComponentProps,
        IDimensionProps,
        IMarginProps,
        IPaddingProps,
        IRoundedProps,
        IElevationProps,
        IBgColorProps {
    /** Visualisation primitive. Default `'line'`. */
    type?: TChartType
    /** Data series — one or more. Empty array renders the `#empty` slot. */
    series: Array<IChartSeries>
    /**
     * X axis labels (for line/area/bar/column/radar). Length should
     * match the longest series; missing entries fall back to the
     * numeric index.
     */
    categories?: Array<string>
    /** Optional title rendered above the plotting area. */
    title?: string
    /** Optional subtitle rendered below the title. */
    subtitle?: string
    /** Toggle the legend block. Default `true`. */
    showLegend?: boolean
    /** Anchor of the legend block. Default `'bottom'`. */
    legendPosition?: TChartLegendPosition
    /** Toggle the hover tooltip. Default `true`. */
    showTooltip?: boolean
    /**
     * Render grid lines behind the plot. Ignored for pie/donut/radar.
     * Default `true`.
     */
    showGrid?: boolean
    /** Render X / Y axes + tick labels. Ignored for pie/donut. Default `true`. */
    showAxis?: boolean
    /**
     * Animate paths / bars / slices on first paint and on data
     * changes. Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`. Default `true`.
     */
    animated?: boolean
    /** Animation duration in ms. Default `600`. */
    animationDuration?: number
    /** Stack series on `bar` / `column`. Default `false`. */
    stacked?: boolean
    /**
     * Stacking mode applied when `stacked=true`.
     *
     * - `'normal'`  — raw absolute values (default, matches pre-v2
     *                 `stacked=true` behaviour).
     * - `'percent'` — every stack is normalised to 100 %; Y-axis
     *                 fixed at `0 → 100`, tick labels use `${v}%`.
     */
    stacking?: TChartStacking
    /**
     * Inner-radius proportion for `donut`. `0` collapses to a pie,
     * `1` to a ring of zero thickness. Default `0.6` when
     * `type === 'donut'`, ignored otherwise.
     */
    donutHoleSize?: number
    /**
     * Palette used when a series does not pin its own `color`.
     * Pass intent strings (cycled in order) or raw CSS colours.
     * Default cycles through the 8 origam intents.
     */
    colorScheme?: Array<TIntent | string>
    /** Formatter applied to X-axis tick labels. */
    xAxisFormat?: (value: string | number) => string
    /** Formatter applied to Y-axis tick labels. */
    yAxisFormat?: (value: number) => string
    /**
     * CSS `aspect-ratio` shortcut (`'16/9'`, `'4/3'`, `'1/1'`…). When
     * set, overrides `height` and lets the chart breathe with the
     * container width.
     */
    aspectRatio?: string
    /**
     * Smoothing strategy for line/area. Default `'none'` —
     * Cartesian segments between points. `'curve'` uses cubic
     * Bezier with Catmull-Rom tangents.
     */
    smoothing?: TChartSmoothing
    /**
     * Override the auto-computed Y min. Useful when the data
     * starts well above zero and you want the visual baseline.
     */
    yMin?: number
    /** Override the auto-computed Y max. */
    yMax?: number
    /**
     * Lower bound of the gauge range. Ignored unless `type='gauge'`.
     * Default `0`.
     */
    gaugeMin?: number
    /**
     * Upper bound of the gauge range. Ignored unless `type='gauge'`.
     * Default `100`.
     */
    gaugeMax?: number
    /**
     * Optional unit appended to the gauge centre label
     * (e.g. `'%'`, `' km/h'`). Ignored unless `type='gauge'`.
     */
    gaugeUnit?: string
    /**
     * Coloured rectangular zones drawn behind (or above) the chart
     * data. Forwarded to `<OrigamChartCartesian>` only — ignored for
     * polar / radar / gauge families.
     */
    plotBands?: Array<IChartPlotBand>
    /**
     * Threshold lines drawn at a fixed axis value. Forwarded to
     * `<OrigamChartCartesian>` only — ignored for polar / radar / gauge.
     */
    plotLines?: Array<IChartPlotLine>
    /**
     * Overlay annotations drawn on top of the series layer. Forwarded to
     * `<OrigamChartCartesian>` only — ignored for polar / radar / gauge /
     * other non-cartesian families. Annotations are positioned in data
     * coordinates. Four kinds: `'arrow'`, `'label'`, `'circle'`, `'bracket'`.
     */
    annotations?: Array<IChartAnnotation>
    /**
     * Drilldown configuration forwarded to `<OrigamChartCartesian>` and
     * `<OrigamChartPolar>`. Ignored by other families (radar, gauge, …).
     */
    drilldown?: IChartDrilldownProps
    /**
     * Secondary (right-hand) Y axis configuration. Forwarded to
     * `<OrigamChartCartesian>` only — ignored for other families.
     * When absent the engine ignores `series.yAxis === 1` assignments.
     */
    secondaryYAxis?: IChartSecondaryYAxis
}

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: Visualisation primitive. Default "line".

props.name: series
props.description_fallback: Data series — one or more. Empty array renders the #empty slot.

props.name: categories
props.description_fallback: X axis labels (for line/area/bar/column/radar). Length should match the longest series; missing entries fall back to the numeric index.

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Optional title rendered above the plotting area.

props.name: subtitle
props.description_fallback: Optional subtitle rendered below the title.

props.name: showLegend
props.description_fallback: Toggle the legend block. Default true.

props.name: legendPosition
props.description_fallback: Anchor of the legend block. Default "bottom".

props.name: showTooltip
props.description_fallback: Toggle the hover tooltip. Default true.

props.name: showGrid
props.description_fallback: Render grid lines behind the plot. Ignored for pie/donut/radar. Default true.

props.name: showAxis
props.description_fallback: Render X / Y axes + tick labels. Ignored for pie/donut. Default true.

props.name: animated
props.description_fallback: Animate paths / bars / slices on first paint and on data changes. Respects prefers-reduced-motion. Default true.

props.name: animationDuration
props.description_fallback: Animation duration in ms. Default 600.

props.name: stacked
props.description_fallback: Stack series on bar / column. Default false.

props.name: stacking
props.description_fallback: Stacking mode when stacked=true. "normal" = raw absolute values (default); "percent" = every stack normalised to 100%.

props.name: donutHoleSize
props.description_fallback: Inner-radius proportion for donut. 0 collapses to a pie, 1 to a ring of zero thickness. Default 0.6 when type === "donut".

props.name: colorScheme
props.description_fallback: Palette used when a series does not pin its own color. Default cycles through the 8 origam intents.

props.name: xAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to X-axis tick labels.

props.name: yAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to Y-axis tick labels.

props.name: aspectRatio
props.description_fallback: CSS aspect-ratio shortcut ("16/9", "4/3", "1/1"…). When set, overrides height and lets the chart breathe with the container width.

props.name: smoothing
props.description_fallback: Smoothing strategy for line/area. Default "none" — straight segments. "curve" uses cubic Bezier with Catmull-Rom tangents.

props.name: yMin
props.description_fallback: Override the auto-computed Y min.

props.name: yMax
props.description_fallback: Override the auto-computed Y max.

props.name: gaugeMin
props.description_fallback: Lower bound of the gauge range. Ignored unless type="gauge". Default 0.

props.name: gaugeMax
props.description_fallback: Upper bound of the gauge range. Ignored unless type="gauge". Default 100.

props.name: gaugeUnit
props.description_fallback: Optional unit appended to the gauge centre label (e.g. "%", " km/h"). Ignored unless type="gauge".

props.name: plotBands
props.description_fallback: Coloured rectangular zones drawn behind (or above) chart data. Forwarded to cartesian charts only.

props.name: plotLines
props.description_fallback: Threshold lines drawn at a fixed axis value. Forwarded to cartesian charts only.

props.name: annotations
props.description_fallback: Overlay annotations drawn on top of the series layer. Forwarded to cartesian charts only. Four kinds: "arrow", "label", "circle", "bracket".

examples.title_fallback: Universal chart with stacked bars and a plot line
examples.code:
<OrigamChart
    type="bar"
    :series="[
        { name: 'Q1', data: [30, 50, 20] },
        { name: 'Q2', data: [40, 35, 45] }
    ]"
    :categories="['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar']"
    stacked
    stacking="percent"
    :plotLines="[{ axis: 'y', value: 50, color: 'danger', label: 'Target' }]"
    aspectRatio="16/9"
/>

entry.name: IChartPyramidProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamChartPyramid> — the pyramid / funnel family. A single series of N data points is rendered as N horizontal trapezoids stacked vertically. type="funnel" = widest at top (conversion funnel); type="pyramid" = widest at bottom. Extends IChartBaseProps.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartPyramidProps extends IChartBaseProps {
    /**
     * Rendering mode. `'funnel'` = widest at top; `'pyramid'` =
     * widest at bottom. Default `'funnel'`.
     */
    type?: TChartPyramidKind
    /**
     * Gap between consecutive trapezoid slices, in SVG pixels.
     * Default `4`.
     */
    sliceGap?: number
    /**
     * Whether to render the category name + value inside each slice
     * when the slice is large enough, or beside it when it is not.
     * `'inside'` — label always inside the trapezoid.
     * `'outside'` — label always beside the trapezoid.
     * `'auto'`  — inside when the slice height ≥ 28px, outside
     *              otherwise. Default `'auto'`.
     */
    labelPlacement?: 'inside' | 'outside' | 'auto'
    /**
     * X-axis / value formatter applied to the per-slice value label.
     * When omitted the raw number is displayed.
     */
    xAxisFormat?: (value: string | number) => string
    /**
     * Y-axis formatter — alias kept for API symmetry with other
     * families. Same as `xAxisFormat` for this geometry.
     */
    yAxisFormat?: (value: number) => string
}

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: Rendering mode. "funnel" = widest at top; "pyramid" = widest at bottom. Default "funnel".

props.name: sliceGap
props.description_fallback: Gap between consecutive trapezoid slices, in SVG pixels. Default 4.

props.name: labelPlacement
props.description_fallback: Label placement strategy. "inside" — always inside; "outside" — always beside; "auto" — inside when slice height >= 28px, outside otherwise. Default "auto".

props.name: xAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: X-axis / value formatter applied to the per-slice value label. When omitted the raw number is displayed.

props.name: yAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Y-axis formatter — alias kept for API symmetry with other families. Same as xAxisFormat for this geometry.

examples.title_fallback: Conversion funnel chart
examples.code:
<OrigamChartPyramid
    type="funnel"
    :series="[{ name: 'Funnel', data: [1000, 750, 400, 200, 80] }]"
    :categories="['Visits', 'Leads', 'Qualified', 'Proposals', 'Closed']"
    labelPlacement="auto"
    :sliceGap="6"
/>

entry.name: IChartPyramidSlice
entry.description_fallback: Single slice descriptor produced by the pyramid geometry engine. Exposes the pre-computed SVG <path d="…"> for the trapezoid, the label anchor position, and all data needed to render accessibility text and tooltip content.

props.name: index
props.description_fallback: Slice index in the original data array.

props.name: d
props.description_fallback: SVG <path d="…"> for the trapezoid.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Resolved CSS colour string.

props.name: category
props.description_fallback: Category label (from categories[index] or fallback).

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Raw numeric value from series.data[index].

props.name: formatted
props.description_fallback: Pre-formatted value string.

props.name: visible
props.description_fallback: Whether this slice is currently visible (legend toggle).

props.name: labelX
props.description_fallback: Label anchor X — centre of the trapezoid face for inside placement; right-edge midpoint + offset for outside.

props.name: labelY
props.description_fallback: Label anchor Y.

props.name: labelAnchor
props.description_fallback: SVG text-anchor attribute matching labelX placement.

props.name: leaderX
props.description_fallback: Y of the leader-line target (band right edge midpoint).

props.name: labelFitsInside
props.description_fallback: true when the band is wide enough to host the label inside.

examples.title_fallback: Consuming slice descriptors from the pyramid engine

entry.name: IChartPyramidSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot signatures exposed by <OrigamChartPyramid>. Extends IChartBaseSlots with a slice-label slot that replaces the label rendered on or beside each slice, receiving the category name, raw value, formatted value, resolved colour, index and visibility.

props.name: 'slice-label'
props.type_label:
(bindings: {
        category: string
        value: number
        formatted: string
        color: string
        index: number
        visible: boolean
    }) => any
props.description_fallback: Replace the label rendered on / beside each slice. Receives the category name, raw value, formatted value, the resolved slice colour, the slice index and its visibility state.

examples.title_fallback: Custom slice label on a funnel chart
examples.code:
<OrigamChartPyramid :series="series" :categories="cats">
    <template #slice-label="{ category, value, formatted }">
        <tspan font-weight="bold">{{ category }}</tspan>
        <tspan> ({{ formatted }})</tspan>
    </template>
</OrigamChartPyramid>

entry.name: IChartRadarProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamChartRadar> — the radar / spider chart. Tightly-typed wrapper around <OrigamChart type="radar"> with only the options relevant to radar charts. No type prop is exposed — the family is fixed to the radar geometry. Extends IChartBaseProps.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartRadarProps extends IChartBaseProps {

}

examples.title_fallback: Spider chart comparing two profiles
examples.code:
<OrigamChartRadar
    :series="[
        { name: 'Alice', data: [80, 65, 90, 75, 55] },
        { name: 'Bob',   data: [60, 80, 70, 90, 80] }
    ]"
    :categories="['Speed', 'Power', 'Agility', 'Stamina', 'Technique']"
    showLegend
/>

entry.name: IChartRangeSelector
entry.description_fallback: Configuration for the optional band-style range-selector toolbar rendered above <OrigamChartCartesian>. When enabled, displays a set of buttons that let the user select a visible time window over the full dataset.

props.name: enabled
props.description_fallback: Show the range-selector toolbar. Default false.

props.name: buttons
props.description_fallback: Buttons to display. Defaults to a standard set when omitted: 1w / 1m / 3m / 6m / 1y / all.

props.name: selected
props.description_fallback: Zero-based index of the initially selected button.

examples.title_fallback: Enabling the range selector with custom buttons
examples.code:
<OrigamChartCartesian
    :series="series"
    :rangeSelector="{
        enabled: true,
        selected: 1,
        buttons: [
            { label: '7d', count: 7 },
            { label: '1m', count: 30 },
            { label: 'All', fraction: 1 }
        ]
    }"
/>

entry.name: IChartRangeSelectorButton
entry.description_fallback: A single button in the range-selector toolbar. Exactly one of count or fraction must be provided: count = number of categories from the right edge to show; fraction = fraction of the full category range to show (0..1).

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Short label rendered on the button. E.g. "1w", "1m", "all".

props.name: count
props.description_fallback: Number of categories from the trailing edge of the data to include in the visible window. Ignored when fraction is set.

props.name: fraction
props.description_fallback: Fraction of the total category count to show (0 exclusive, 1 inclusive). fraction: 1 is equivalent to "all". Ignored when count is set.

examples.title_fallback: Defining range selector buttons
examples.code:
const buttons: IChartRangeSelectorButton[] = [
    { label: '1w',  count: 7 },
    { label: '1m',  count: 30 },
    { label: '3m',  count: 90 },
    { label: 'All', fraction: 1 },
]

entry.name: IChartSankeyDatum
entry.description_fallback: A single flow datum in the Sankey series. from and to are node name strings — nodes are inferred automatically from the full set of unique names. value must be >= 0. An optional color override accepts a TIntent token or raw CSS colour.

props.name: from
props.description_fallback: Source node name.

props.name: to
props.description_fallback: Target node name.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Flow magnitude. Must be >= 0.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Optional per-link colour override. Accepts a design-system intent token or any raw CSS colour string. When absent the source node's colour is used.

examples.title_fallback: Defining flow data for a Sankey diagram
examples.code:
const data: IChartSankeyDatum[] = [
    { from: 'Marketing', to: 'Leads',    value: 1200 },
    { from: 'Leads',     to: 'Qualified', value: 800 },
    { from: 'Qualified', to: 'Closed',   value: 300, color: 'success' },
]

entry.name: IChartSankeyLink
entry.description_fallback: Internal link descriptor for each IChartSankeyDatum after Sankey layout. Carries the rendered SVG cubic Bezier path d attribute, visual metadata and resolved colour. Produced by the Sankey layout engine — not intended for direct consumer use.

props.name: index
props.description_fallback: Index in the original data array.

props.name: from
props.description_fallback: Source node name.

props.name: to
props.description_fallback: Target node name.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Raw numeric flow value.

props.name: formatted
props.description_fallback: Pre-formatted value string.

props.name: d
props.description_fallback: Rendered SVG cubic Bezier path string.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Resolved CSS stroke colour string (defaults to source node color).

props.name: strokeWidth
props.description_fallback: Stroke width in SVG user units.

examples.title_fallback: Internal Sankey link descriptor

entry.name: IChartSankeyNode
entry.description_fallback: Internal node descriptor produced by the Sankey layout engine. All coordinates are in SVG-user-unit space. Nodes are inferred from the union of all from and to strings in IChartSankeyDatum; column is assigned by topological depth (Kahn's algorithm).

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: Unique node name (inferred from from / to values).

props.name: column
props.description_fallback: Column index (0-based topological depth).

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Total flow value (max of incoming and outgoing sums).

props.name: formatted
props.description_fallback: Pre-formatted value string.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Resolved CSS colour string.

props.name: x
props.description_fallback: SVG x coordinate of the node rect's left edge.

props.name: y
props.description_fallback: SVG y coordinate of the node rect's top edge.

props.name: height
props.description_fallback: Node rect height in SVG user units, proportional to value.

props.name: outgoingOffset
props.description_fallback: Accumulated outgoing y offset (used during link placement).

props.name: incomingOffset
props.description_fallback: Accumulated incoming y offset (used during link placement).

examples.title_fallback: Internal Sankey node descriptor

entry.name: IChartSankeyProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamChartSankey>. The component consumes series[0] only — each item in series[0].data must conform to IChartSankeyDatum ({ from, to, value }). Nodes are inferred automatically; columns are assigned by topological depth. Extends IChartBaseProps.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartSankeyProps extends IChartBaseProps {
    /**
     * Width of each node rectangle in SVG pixels. Default `16`.
     */
    nodeWidth?: number
    /**
     * Minimum vertical gap between sibling nodes within the same column,
     * in SVG pixels. Default `8`.
     */
    nodePadding?: number
    /**
     * Opacity of the flow ribbons (links). Range [0, 1]. Default `0.4`.
     */
    linkOpacity?: number
    /**
     * Whether to render node labels. Left-column nodes are right-aligned
     * outside their rect; right-column nodes are left-aligned outside.
     * Default `true`.
     */
    showLabel?: boolean
    /**
     * X-axis / value formatter applied to node labels and tooltip values.
     * When omitted the raw number is displayed.
     */
    xAxisFormat?: (value: number | string) => string
    /**
     * Y-axis formatter — alias kept for API symmetry with other
     * families. Same as `xAxisFormat` for this geometry.
     */
    yAxisFormat?: (value: number) => string
}

props.name: nodeWidth
props.description_fallback: Width of each node rectangle in SVG pixels. Default 16.

props.name: nodePadding
props.description_fallback: Minimum vertical gap between sibling nodes within the same column, in SVG pixels. Default 8.

props.name: linkOpacity
props.description_fallback: Opacity of the flow ribbons (links). Range [0, 1]. Default 0.4.

props.name: showLabel
props.description_fallback: Whether to render node labels. Left-column nodes are right-aligned outside their rect; right-column nodes are left-aligned outside. Default true.

props.name: xAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: X-axis / value formatter applied to node labels and tooltip values. When omitted the raw number is displayed.

props.name: yAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Y-axis formatter — alias kept for API symmetry with other families. Same as xAxisFormat for this geometry.

examples.title_fallback: Sankey diagram showing energy flows
examples.code:
<OrigamChartSankey
    :series="[{
        name: 'Energy',
        data: [
            { from: 'Solar',   to: 'Electricity', value: 40 },
            { from: 'Wind',    to: 'Electricity', value: 30 },
            { from: 'Electricity', to: 'Homes',   value: 50 },
            { from: 'Electricity', to: 'Industry', value: 20 },
        ]
    }]"
    :nodeWidth="20"
    :nodePadding="12"
    :linkOpacity="0.35"
    showLabel
/>

entry.name: IChartSankeySlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot signatures exposed by <OrigamChartSankey>. Extends IChartBaseSlots with a node-label slot that replaces the label rendered beside each node, receiving the node name, value, formatted value, resolved colour and column index.

props.name: 'node-label'
props.type_label:
(bindings: {
        name: string
        value: number
        formatted: string
        color: string
        column: number
    }) => any
props.description_fallback: Replace the label rendered beside each node. Receives the node name, raw value, formatted value, resolved colour and column index.

examples.title_fallback: Custom node label on a Sankey diagram
examples.code:
<OrigamChartSankey :series="series">
    <template #node-label="{ name, formatted, column }">
        <tspan font-weight="bold">{{ name }}</tspan>
        <tspan> ({{ formatted }})</tspan>
    </template>
</OrigamChartSankey>

entry.name: IChartScales
entry.description_fallback: Pair of pixel-mapping functions produced by useChart from the data range and viewBox size. Pure functions — safe to call from computed refs. x maps a value or category index to an SVG horizontal coordinate; y maps a numeric value to an SVG vertical coordinate.

props.name: x
props.type_label: (value: number | string, dataIndex?: number, categoryCount?: number) => number
props.description_fallback: Maps an X-axis value (numeric or category string) plus an optional data index and category count to an SVG horizontal pixel coordinate.

props.name: y
props.description_fallback: Maps a numeric Y value to an SVG vertical pixel coordinate using the primary left-axis scale.

examples.title_fallback: Using IChartScales to project a data point onto SVG space

entry.name: IChartSecondaryYAxis
entry.description_fallback: Configuration object for the secondary (right-hand) Y axis on a cartesian chart. When passed to secondaryYAxis on OrigamChartCartesian, series with yAxis: 1 are projected against this scale. Omit to use the primary left axis only.

props.name: min
props.description_fallback: Force the minimum value of the secondary Y scale. Auto-computed from data when absent.

props.name: max
props.description_fallback: Force the maximum value of the secondary Y scale. Auto-computed from data when absent.

props.name: format
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to the right-axis tick labels.

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Optional axis title rendered alongside the right axis.

examples.title_fallback: Adding a secondary Y axis with a custom formatter
examples.code:
<OrigamChartCartesian
    :series="series"
    :secondary-y-axis="{ min: 0, max: 100, format: v => v + '%', title: 'Growth' }"
/>

entry.name: IChartSeries
entry.description_fallback: A single data series rendered by OrigamChart. The data field accepts either a flat number array (values aligned to categories by index) or an object array of IChartSeriesPoint for explicit x/y coordinates — required for scatter and sparse data.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartSeries {
    /** Display name in the legend + screen-reader description. */
    name: string

    /**
     * Series data. Numbers are aligned to `categories[index]`.
     * Objects carry their own X coordinate (numeric or category
     * string), useful for scatter and irregular sampling.
     */
    data: Array<number> | Array<IChartSeriesPoint>

    /**
     * Intent token (`primary`, `success`, …) or raw CSS colour
     * (`#ff0080`, `rgb(…)`). When absent, the chart pulls the
     * next colour from `colorScheme` cycling by series index.
     */
    color?: TIntent | string

    /**
     * Whether the series is currently visible. Driven from the
     * legend click (`legend-click` → `series-toggle`). Default
     * `true`.
     */
    visible?: boolean

    /**
     * Override the chart-level `type` for this series only.
     * Enables mix charts (a `column` chart with a `line`
     * overlay on top).
     */
    type?: TChartType

    /**
     * Which Y axis this series is mapped to.
     * `0` (default) = left primary axis, `1` = right secondary axis.
     * Only meaningful for cartesian chart families (line, area, bar,
     * column, scatter, spline, stepped-line). Ignored for pie, donut,
     * radar, and gauge.
     */
    yAxis?: 0 | 1
}

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: Display name shown in the legend and used as the screen-reader description for the series.

props.name: data
props.description_fallback: Series data. Numbers are aligned to categories[index]. Objects carry their own X coordinate — required for scatter and irregular sampling.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Intent token (primary, success, …) or raw CSS colour (#ff0080, rgb(…)). When absent the chart pulls the next colour from colorScheme cycling by series index.

props.name: visible
props.description_fallback: Whether the series is currently visible. Driven from legend-click → series-toggle. Default true.

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: Override the chart-level type for this series only — enables mix charts (a column chart with a line overlay).

props.name: yAxis
props.description_fallback: Which Y axis this series is mapped to. 0 = left primary axis, 1 = right secondary axis. Only meaningful for cartesian family charts.

examples.title_fallback: Defining a line series and a column series on a mix chart
examples.code:
import type { IChartSeries } from 'origam'

const series: IChartSeries[] = [
    { name: 'Revenue', data: [120, 200, 150, 80], color: 'primary' },
    { name: 'Units', data: [40, 70, 55, 30], type: 'column', yAxis: 1 },
]

entry.name: IChartSeriesPoint
entry.description_fallback: Object form of a single data point inside IChartSeries.data. Required for scatter charts where the X axis is not a simple categories[index]. The optional z field turns the point into a bubble by scaling its dot radius.

props.name: x
props.description_fallback: X coordinate — numeric for scatter, category string for categorical axes.

props.name: y
props.description_fallback: Y value mapped to the vertical scale.

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: Optional friendly label shown in the tooltip instead of the raw x value.

props.name: z
props.description_fallback: Third dimension for scatter — turns the chart into a bubble plot where the dot radius scales with z. Omit to keep the default fixed radius.

props.name: drilldown
props.description_fallback: Drilldown reference. When present and the chart has a matching IChartDrilldownDataset, clicking this point replaces the chart view with the sub-dataset.

examples.title_fallback: Scatter series with bubble sizing
examples.code:
import type { IChartSeries, IChartSeriesPoint } from 'origam'

const points: IChartSeriesPoint[] = [
    { x: 10, y: 200, z: 40, name: 'Product A' },
    { x: 25, y: 150, z: 80, name: 'Product B' },
]
const series: IChartSeries[] = [{ name: 'Sales', data: points }]

entry.name: IChartSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot signatures for the universal OrigamChart component. Replace the default tooltip card, a legend entry, the title block, or the empty state without losing the rest of the chart chrome.

props.name: tooltip
props.type_label: (bindings: { point: IChartPoint, series: IChartSeries, category: string | number }) => any
props.description_fallback: Replace the default floating tooltip body. Receives the hovered point, its series and the X-axis category.

props.name: 'legend-item'
props.type_label: (bindings: { series: IChartSeries, index: number, visible: boolean }) => any

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Replace the title + subtitle block rendered above the plotting area.

props.name: empty
props.description_fallback: Rendered when series is an empty array or every series is hidden via the legend.

examples.title_fallback: Custom tooltip and empty state

entry.name: IChartSparklineEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emits surfaced by OrigamChartSparkline. Single event — point-click — fired on mouse click or keyboard activation on a data point, providing the IChartPoint payload and the originating DOM event.

examples.title_fallback: Listening to point-click on a sparkline
examples.code:
<OrigamChartSparkline
    :series="series"
    @point-click="(point, event) => console.log(point.y, event.type)"
/>

entry.name: IChartSparklineKind
entry.description_fallback: Internal marker descriptor for a highlighted circle rendered on the sparkline SVG. Used by the min / max / last indicator rendering logic inside OrigamChartSparkline.

props.name: cx
props.description_fallback: SVG cx coordinate of the marker circle centre.

props.name: cy
props.description_fallback: SVG cy coordinate of the marker circle centre.

props.name: r
props.description_fallback: Circle radius in SVG pixels.

props.name: fill
props.description_fallback: Resolved CSS fill colour string applied to the marker circle.

props.name: role
props.description_fallback: Role of this marker — 'min' highlights the minimum value, 'max' the maximum, 'last' the most recent data point.

props.name: dataIndex
props.description_fallback: Index of the data point this marker corresponds to inside the series data array.

examples.title_fallback: IChartSparklineKind used internally by the sparkline renderer

entry.name: IChartSparklineProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamChartSparkline — the tiny inline-chart family. Sparklines are compact data visualisations for embedding in table cells, KPI cards and dashboards. They have no axes, no legend, no grid by default. Extends IChartBaseProps omitting series, showLegend, legendPosition and colorScheme.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartSparklineProps extends Omit<IChartBaseProps, 'series' | 'showLegend' | 'legendPosition' | 'colorScheme'> {
    /** Single data series. Data must be `Array<number>`. */
    series: Array<IChartSeries>

    /**
     * Rendering mode.
     * `'line'` — polyline connecting data points.
     * `'area'` — line with a translucent fill below.
     * `'column'` — vertical bars.
     * `'bar'` — horizontal bars.
     * Default `'line'`.
     */
    type?: TChartSparklineKind

    /**
     * Stroke / fill intent or raw CSS colour.
     * Default `'primary'`.
     */
    color?: TIntent | string

    /**
     * Stroke width for line and area modes (SVG pixels).
     * Default `1.5`.
     */
    lineWidth?: number

    /**
     * Render a filled circle marker at every data point.
     * Only meaningful for `'line'` and `'area'` modes.
     * Default `false`.
     */
    showMarkers?: boolean

    /**
     * Highlight the minimum value point in red.
     * Default `false`.
     */
    showMin?: boolean

    /**
     * Highlight the maximum value point in green.
     * Default `false`.
     */
    showMax?: boolean

    /**
     * Highlight the last data point in the primary colour.
     * Default `true`.
     */
    showLast?: boolean

    /**
     * Radius of highlighted marker circles (SVG pixels).
     * Default `2.5`.
     */
    markerSize?: number

    /**
     * CSS width of the sparkline container.
     * Accepts a number (px) or a CSS string (`'100%'`, `'8rem'`).
     * Default `120`.
     */
    width?: number | string

    /**
     * CSS height of the sparkline container.
     * Accepts a number (px) or a CSS string (`'100%'`, `'2rem'`).
     * Default `30`.
     */
    height?: number | string
}

props.name: series
props.description_fallback: Single data series. Data must be Array<number> — only one series is rendered.

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: Rendering mode. 'line' — polyline connecting data points. 'area' — line with a translucent fill below. 'column' — vertical bars. 'bar' — horizontal bars.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Stroke / fill intent or raw CSS colour applied uniformly to the sparkline (no palette cycling).

props.name: lineWidth
props.description_fallback: Stroke width for line and area modes in SVG pixels.

props.name: showMarkers
props.description_fallback: Render a filled circle marker at every data point. Only meaningful for line and area modes.

props.name: showMin
props.description_fallback: Highlight the minimum value point in red.

props.name: showMax
props.description_fallback: Highlight the maximum value point in green.

props.name: showLast
props.description_fallback: Highlight the last data point in the primary colour.

props.name: markerSize
props.description_fallback: Radius of highlighted marker circles in SVG pixels.

props.name: width
props.description_fallback: CSS width of the sparkline container. Accepts a number (px) or a CSS string (100%, 8rem).

props.name: height
props.description_fallback: CSS height of the sparkline container. Accepts a number (px) or a CSS string (100%, 2rem).

examples.title_fallback: Embedding a sparkline in a KPI card
examples.code:
<OrigamChartSparkline
    :series="[{ name: 'Sales', data: [10, 45, 30, 80, 65] }]"
    type="area"
    color="success"
    :show-max="true"
    :show-last="true"
    width="160"
    height="40"
/>

entry.name: IChartStreamgraphProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamChartStreamgraph. Multiple series are rendered as continuous flowing ribbons stacked over time on the X axis. The baseline offset algorithm is controlled by offsetMode: wiggle (canonical streamgraph), silhouette, expand, or zero.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartStreamgraphProps extends IChartBaseProps {
    /**
     * Baseline offset algorithm. Default `'wiggle'`.
     */
    offsetMode?: TChartStreamgraphOffset
    /**
     * Path interpolation. `'curve'` applies Catmull-Rom smoothing
     * on each ribbon's top and bottom polylines. `'none'` uses
     * straight linear segments. Default `'curve'`.
     */
    smoothing?: 'none' | 'curve'
    /**
     * Toggle the X / Y axis guides. Default `true`.
     */
    showAxis?: boolean
    /**
     * Toggle background grid lines. Default `false`.
     */
    showGrid?: boolean
    /**
     * Formatter applied to each X-axis category label.
     */
    xAxisFormat?: (value: string | number) => string
    /**
     * Formatter applied to Y-axis tick labels.
     */
    yAxisFormat?: (value: number) => string
}

props.name: offsetMode
props.description_fallback: Baseline offset algorithm. 'wiggle' — Byron-Wattenberg: centred baseline minimising total slope change (canonical). 'silhouette' — centred at half total stack. 'expand' — normalised to 100% height. 'zero' — traditional stacked area.

props.name: smoothing
props.description_fallback: Path interpolation. 'curve' applies Catmull-Rom smoothing on each ribbon's top and bottom polylines. 'none' uses straight linear segments.

props.name: showAxis
props.description_fallback: Toggle the X / Y axis guides.

props.name: showGrid
props.description_fallback: Toggle background grid lines.

props.name: xAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to each X-axis category label.

props.name: yAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to Y-axis tick labels.

examples.title_fallback: Streamgraph with percent-normalised expand mode
examples.code:
<OrigamChartStreamgraph
    :series="series"
    :categories="months"
    offset-mode="expand"
    smoothing="curve"
    :y-axis-format="v => v + '%'"
/>

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: Display name from series[i].name.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Resolved CSS colour string for this ribbon.

props.name: d
props.description_fallback: SVG <path d="…"> — top path forward + bottom path reverse, forming a closed ribbon shape.

props.name: visible
props.description_fallback: Whether this ribbon is currently toggled visible via the legend.

props.name: values
props.description_fallback: Raw data values at each X position, used for tooltip breakdown display.

entry.name: IChartSunburstDatum
entry.description_fallback: A single datum node in the sunburst tree. Internal nodes may omit value — it will be auto-computed as the sum of direct children. The children array forms the next ring outward in the chart.

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: Display name for the node, shown in arc labels and the tooltip.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Numeric value. Optional for internal nodes — if absent it equals the sum of direct children.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Override colour for this specific node. Accepts a design-system intent token or any raw CSS colour string.

props.name: children
props.description_fallback: Child nodes — each child appears in the next ring outward from this node.

examples.title_fallback: Two-level sunburst dataset

entry.name: IChartSunburstNode
entry.description_fallback: Fully-resolved node produced by the sunburst geometry engine. Every field is definite — defaults and inherited values have been applied. Contains SVG arc coordinates, resolved colour and the label rendering mode decision.

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Unique path key built as "Root > Branch > Leaf". Used as the v-for :key.

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: Display name — copy of datum.name.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Resolved numeric value — sum of children when absent in the source datum.

props.name: depth
props.description_fallback: Tree depth; root-level nodes are 0.

props.name: rootIndex
props.description_fallback: Index within the root-level array — used for colour inheritance down the tree.

props.name: startAngle
props.description_fallback: Arc start angle in radians [0, 2π).

props.name: endAngle
props.description_fallback: Arc end angle in radians (0, 2π].

props.name: innerR
props.description_fallback: Inner ring radius for this node in SVG user units.

props.name: outerR
props.description_fallback: Outer ring radius for this node in SVG user units.

props.name: d
props.description_fallback: Pre-computed SVG <path d="…"> for the arc segment.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Resolved CSS colour string — intent variable or raw value.

props.name: visible
props.description_fallback: Whether this node is currently visible (driven by legend toggle on root-level entries).

props.name: path
props.description_fallback: Full ancestor path string "Root > Branch > Leaf" — shown in the tooltip.

props.name: labelMode
props.description_fallback: How the label for this node should be rendered. 'inline' — centred inside the arc. 'rotated' — tangentially along the arc midpoint. 'leader' — outside the chart with a leader line.

entry.name: IChartSunburstProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamChartSunburst — the hierarchical ring chart. Extends IChartBaseProps (omitting categories). Series data must be Array<IChartSunburstDatum> carrying a nested tree. Controls inner-radius hole size, ring padding, arc label rendering and value formatting.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartSunburstProps extends Omit<IChartBaseProps, 'categories'> {
    /**
     * Proportion of the overall radius reserved for the centre hole.
     * `0` = full pie (no hole). `0.4` = deep donut.
     * Default `0.15`.
     */
    innerRadius?: number
    /**
     * Radial gap between consecutive rings in SVG pixels.
     * Default `1`.
     */
    ringPadding?: number
    /**
     * When `true`, arc labels are rendered for nodes whose angular
     * span is ≥ 0.1 rad. Default `true`.
     */
    showLabel?: boolean
    /**
     * Y-axis / tooltip value formatter. Receives the numeric value,
     * returns a display string. When omitted the raw number is used.
     */
    yAxisFormat?: (value: number) => string
}

props.name: innerRadius
props.description_fallback: Proportion of the overall radius reserved for the centre hole. 0 = full pie (no hole). 0.4 = deep donut.

props.name: ringPadding
props.description_fallback: Radial gap between consecutive rings in SVG pixels.

props.name: showLabel
props.description_fallback: When true, arc labels are rendered for nodes whose angular span is ≥ 0.1 rad.

props.name: yAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Y-axis / tooltip value formatter. Receives the numeric value, returns a display string. Raw number used when omitted.

examples.title_fallback: Sunburst with a large centre hole and currency formatter
examples.code:
<OrigamChartSunburst
    :series="series"
    :inner-radius="0.5"
    :ring-padding="2"
    :show-label="true"
    :y-axis-format="v => '$' + v.toLocaleString()"
/>

entry.name: IChartTick

entry.name: IChartTooltipProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamChartTooltip — the floating card that follows the cursor and shows the current data point. Extracted from the legacy OrigamChart so every chart family can share the same rendering and slot API. Position is driven by x/y pixel coordinates; no Popper.js dependency.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartTooltipProps {
    /**
     * Hovered point payload (denormalised). When `null`, the
     * tooltip is hidden entirely.
     */
    point: IChartPoint | null
    /**
     * Hovered series reference. Companion to `point`. Hidden when
     * `null` even if `point` is set.
     */
    series: IChartSeries | null
    /**
     * Friendly category for the X axis — string when the X axis
     * is categorical, number otherwise.
     */
    category: string | number
    /** Mouse X position relative to the chart body, in pixels. */
    x: number
    /** Mouse Y position relative to the chart body, in pixels. */
    y: number
    /**
     * Formatter applied to the X value shown in the tooltip body.
     * Defaults to `String(value)`.
     */
    xAxisFormat?: (value: string | number) => string
    /**
     * Formatter applied to the Y value shown in the tooltip body.
     * Defaults to `String(value)`.
     */
    yAxisFormat?: (value: number) => string
}

props.name: point
props.description_fallback: Hovered point payload (denormalised). When null the tooltip is hidden entirely.

props.name: series
props.description_fallback: Hovered series reference. Companion to point. Hidden when null even if point is set.

props.name: category
props.description_fallback: Friendly category for the X axis — string for categorical axes, number otherwise.

props.name: x
props.description_fallback: Mouse X position relative to the chart body, in pixels.

props.name: y
props.description_fallback: Mouse Y position relative to the chart body, in pixels.

props.name: xAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to the X value shown in the tooltip body. Defaults to String(value).

props.name: yAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to the Y value shown in the tooltip body. Defaults to String(value).

examples.title_fallback: Standalone OrigamChartTooltip usage
examples.code:
<OrigamChartTooltip
    :point="hoveredPoint"
    :series="hoveredSeries"
    :category="hoveredCategory"
    :x="mouseX"
    :y="mouseY"
    :y-axis-format="v => '$' + v"
/>

entry.name: IChartTreemapDatum
entry.description_fallback: A single data item for the treemap series. Consumed from series[0].data — each item becomes one tile sized proportionally to its value. The optional color overrides the palette entry for that specific tile. children is reserved for future nested drilldown support and is ignored in v1.

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: Tile display label shown inside the tile and in the tooltip.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Numeric magnitude that drives the tile area. Must be ≥ 0.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Optional per-tile colour override. Accepts a design-system intent token or any raw CSS colour string.

props.name: children
props.description_fallback: Reserved for future nested / drilldown support. Currently unused — v1 renders a flat single-level treemap only.

examples.title_fallback: Flat treemap dataset with per-tile colour overrides
examples.code:
import type { IChartTreemapDatum } from 'origam'

const data: IChartTreemapDatum[] = [
    { name: 'Vue', value: 400, color: 'success' },
    { name: 'React', value: 300 },
    { name: 'Angular', value: 200, color: 'danger' },
    { name: 'Svelte', value: 100 },

entry.name: IChartTreemapProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamChartTreemap. Consumes series[0] only — each item in series[0].data becomes one tile sized proportionally to its value. v1 renders a flat single-level treemap; nested children are accepted for forward compatibility but not rendered. Extends IChartBaseProps.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartTreemapProps extends IChartBaseProps {
    /**
     * Layout algorithm.
     * - `'squarified'` — minimises tile aspect ratio (default).
     * - `'slice-dice'` — alternates horizontal / vertical slicing.
     * Default `'squarified'`.
     */
    algorithm?: TChartTreemapAlgorithm
    /**
     * Whether to render the `name` + `value` text inside each tile when
     * the tile is at least 60 × 24 SVG-px. Default `true`.
     */
    showLabel?: boolean
    /**
     * Formatter applied to the numeric value in tile labels and
     * the tooltip. When absent the raw number is stringified.
     */
    yAxisFormat?: (value: number) => string
}

props.name: algorithm
props.description_fallback: Layout algorithm. 'squarified' — minimises tile aspect ratio (default). 'slice-dice' — alternates horizontal / vertical slicing.

props.name: showLabel
props.description_fallback: Whether to render the name + value text inside each tile when the tile is at least 60 × 24 SVG-px.

props.name: yAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to the numeric value in tile labels and the tooltip. Raw number stringified when absent.

examples.title_fallback: Squarified treemap with a K-suffix formatter
examples.code:
<OrigamChartTreemap
    :series="[{ name: 'Budget', data: budgetItems }]"
    algorithm="squarified"
    :show-label="true"
    :y-axis-format="v => v >= 1000 ? (v / 1000).toFixed(1) + 'k' : String(v)"
/>

entry.name: IChartTreemapSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot signatures exposed by OrigamChartTreemap. Extends IChartBaseSlots with a tile-label slot that replaces the inline name + value text inside each tile, receiving the full tile descriptor for complete customisation.

props.name: 'tile-label'
props.type_label:
(bindings: {
        name: string
        value: number
        formatted: string
        color: string
        index: number
        visible: boolean

examples.title_fallback: Custom tile label with percentage share
examples.code:
<OrigamChartTreemap :series="series" :total="total">
    <template #tile-label="{ name, formatted, value }">
        <tspan class="tile-name">{{ name }}</tspan>
        <tspan class="tile-value">{{ formatted }}</tspan>
    </template>
</OrigamChartTreemap>

entry.name: IChartVariwideDatum
entry.description_fallback: A single datum in the variwide series. Encodes two dimensions simultaneously: value → bar height (primary metric, maps to Y axis) and width → bar width (secondary metric, proportional share of plot width).
entry.definition:
export interface IChartVariwideDatum {
    /** Category label shown on the X axis tick and in the tooltip. */
    category: string
    /**
     * Primary value — drives the bar HEIGHT via the Y scale.
     * e.g. GDP in trillions, revenue in M€, response time in ms.
     */
    value: number
    /**
     * Secondary value — drives the bar WIDTH proportionally
     * (`barPixels = plotWidth * width / Σwidths`).
     * e.g. population in M, number of requests, market share %.
     */
    width: number
    /**
     * Optional per-datum colour override. Accepts a `TIntent` token
     * or any raw CSS colour string. When omitted the chart cycles
     * through `colorScheme`.
     */
    color?: TIntent | string
}

props.name: category
props.description_fallback: Category label shown on the X axis tick and in the tooltip.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Primary value — drives the bar height via the Y scale (e.g. GDP in trillions, revenue in M€).

props.name: width
props.description_fallback: Secondary value — drives the bar width proportionally (barPixels = plotWidth * width / Σwidths). e.g. population in M, market share %.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Optional per-datum colour override. Accepts a TIntent token or any raw CSS colour string. When omitted the chart cycles through colorScheme.

examples.title_fallback: GDP × population variwide series
examples.code:
import type { IChartVariwideDatum } from 'origam'

const data: IChartVariwideDatum[] = [
    { category: 'USA',   value: 25.46, width: 335 },
    { category: 'China', value: 17.96, width: 1412 },
    { category: 'EU',    value: 16.64, width: 448, color: 'primary' },
]

entry.name: IChartVariwideProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamChartVariwide>. A variwide chart encodes two metrics per category: height for the primary value (Y axis) and width for the secondary value (proportional share of the plot width). Typical uses: GDP × population, revenue × market share, response time × request count.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartVariwideProps extends IChartBaseProps {
    /**
     * Gap in SVG pixels between adjacent columns.
     * Default `2`.
     */
    barGap?: number
    /**
     * Show the value label above each column. Default `true`.
     */
    showLabel?: boolean
    /**
     * Render X (category) and Y (value) axes. Default `true`.
     */
    showAxis?: boolean
    /**
     * Render horizontal grid lines aligned with Y-axis ticks.
     * Default `true`.
     */
    showGrid?: boolean
    /**
     * Formatter applied to X-axis category tick labels.
     * When omitted the category string is shown as-is.
     */
    xAxisFormat?: (value: string | number) => string
    /**
     * Formatter applied to Y-axis value tick labels and to the
     * primary-value display in tooltip and above-bar label.
     * When omitted the raw number is used.
     */
    yAxisFormat?: (value: number) => string
    /**
     * Override the auto-computed Y minimum.
     * Useful when the data starts well above zero.
     */
    yMin?: number
    /**
     * Override the auto-computed Y maximum.
     */
    yMax?: number
}

props.name: barGap
props.description_fallback: Gap in SVG pixels between adjacent columns.

props.name: showLabel
props.description_fallback: Show the value label above each column.

props.name: showAxis
props.description_fallback: Render X (category) and Y (value) axes.

props.name: showGrid
props.description_fallback: Render horizontal grid lines aligned with Y-axis ticks.

props.name: xAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to X-axis category tick labels. When omitted the category string is shown as-is.

props.name: yAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Formatter applied to Y-axis value tick labels and to the primary-value display in tooltip and above-bar label.

props.name: yMin
props.description_fallback: Override the auto-computed Y minimum. Useful when the data starts well above zero.

entry.name: IChartWordCloudProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamChartWordCloud>. Reads series[0]; each datum must be a { text, value, color? } object. Controls font sizes, rotation strategy, font family/weight, and optional value formatters.
entry.definition:
export interface IChartWordCloudProps extends Omit<IChartBaseProps, 'fontWeight'> {
    /**
     * Minimum font size in px applied to the word with the lowest value.
     * Default `12`.
     */
    minFontSize?: number
    /**
     * Maximum font size in px applied to the word with the highest value.
     * Default `64`.
     */
    maxFontSize?: number
    /**
     * Word rotation strategy.
     * - `'none'`       — all words horizontal (default).
     * - `'random'`     — uniform random in [-30°, 30°].
     * - `'orthogonal'` — 0° or 90° alternating by index parity.
     */
    rotation?: TChartWordCloudRotation
    /**
     * Font family applied to all words. Defaults to `'inherit'` so it
     * picks up the host page's typeface.
     */
    fontFamily?: string
    /**
     * Font weight applied to all words. Default `600`.
     */
    fontWeight?: number | string
    /**
     * X-axis / value formatter. Unused for word clouds; kept for API
     * symmetry with other chart families.
     */
    xAxisFormat?: (value: number | string) => string
    /**
     * Y-axis formatter applied to the value shown in the tooltip.
     * When omitted the raw number is displayed.
     */
    yAxisFormat?: (value: number) => string
}

props.name: minFontSize
props.description_fallback: Minimum font size in px applied to the word with the lowest value.

props.name: maxFontSize
props.description_fallback: Maximum font size in px applied to the word with the highest value.

props.name: rotation
props.description_fallback: Word rotation strategy: 'none' (all horizontal), 'random' (uniform random in [-30°, 30°]), or 'orthogonal' (0° or 90° alternating by index parity).

props.name: fontFamily
props.description_fallback: Font family applied to all words. Defaults to inherit so it picks up the host page typeface.

props.name: fontWeight
props.description_fallback: Font weight applied to all words.

props.name: xAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: X-axis / value formatter. Unused for word clouds; kept for API symmetry with other chart families.

props.name: yAxisFormat
props.description_fallback: Y-axis formatter applied to the value shown in the tooltip. When omitted the raw number is displayed.

examples.title_fallback: Word cloud with orthogonal rotation and custom fonts
examples.code:
<OrigamChartWordCloud
    :series="[{ name: 'Terms', data: words }]"
    :min-font-size="14"
    :max-font-size="72"
    rotation="orthogonal"
    font-family="'Inter', sans-serif"
    :font-weight="700"
    :y-axis-format="v => v + ' hits'"
/>

entry.name: IClipboardProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamClipboard> — a chrome-less copy-to-clipboard wrapper. Owns the copy pipeline (navigator.clipboard.writeText + execCommand fallback) and the auto-resetting copied flag. The built-in trigger is a single button; consumers can pass a #default scoped slot for full control.
entry.definition:
export interface IClipboardProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IBorderProps, IRoundedProps, IMarginProps, IPaddingProps, ITypographyProps {
    /**
     * Text payload written to the clipboard on `copy()`. Required.
     * Re-read each time the trigger fires, so a parent that mutates
     * the value between mounts still copies the up-to-date string.
     */
    value: string
    /**
     * Duration (ms) the `copied` flag stays true after a successful
     * write. The state auto-resets after this window — there is no
     * need to clear it from the consumer side.
     *
     * @default 2000
     */
    feedbackDuration?: number
    /**
     * Label rendered inside the built-in feedback overlay (and the
     * auto-rendered button label when no slot is provided). Consumers
     * wrap with `t()` if they need full i18n.
     *
     * @default 'Copied!'
     */
    feedbackText?: string
    /**
     * Alias for `feedbackText`. Takes precedence when both are passed.
     * Provided for callers who prefer the "success" framing in their
     * codebase.
     */
    successText?: string
    /**
     * Disables the copy action. The default trigger becomes
     * non-interactive (`disabled` attribute) and `copy()` becomes a
     * no-op. The scoped slot still receives the `copy` function so
     * consumers can decide what to render — but it will short-circuit.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    disabled?: boolean
}

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Text payload written to the clipboard on copy(). Re-read on each trigger so mutated values are always current.

props.name: feedbackDuration
props.description_fallback: Duration (ms) the copied flag stays true after a successful write. Auto-resets — no manual clearing needed.

props.name: feedbackText
props.description_fallback: Label rendered inside the built-in feedback overlay and the auto-rendered button label when no slot is provided.

props.name: successText
props.description_fallback: Alias for feedbackText. Takes precedence when both are passed.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables the copy action. The default trigger becomes non-interactive and copy() becomes a no-op.

examples.title_fallback: Basic copy button with custom feedback text
examples.code:
<OrigamClipboard
    :value="codeToCopy"
    feedback-text="Copied to clipboard!"
    :feedback-duration="3000"
    color="primary"
/>

props.name: copy
props.description_fallback: Triggers the copy pipeline. Promise resolves true on success, false on failure.

props.name: copied
props.description_fallback: True for feedbackDuration ms after a successful copy. Automatically resets to false.

props.name: error
props.description_fallback: Set to the thrown Error when the last copy attempt failed; null otherwise.

examples.title_fallback: Custom trigger using scoped slot bindings

entry.name: IClipboardSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot signatures for <OrigamClipboard>. The default slot receives copy/copied/error bindings for a fully custom trigger. The feedback slot replaces the success indicator shown after a successful copy.
entry.definition:
export interface IClipboardSlots {
    /**
     * Custom trigger. Scoped — receives `{ copy, copied, error }`.
     * When omitted, the component renders a default icon button with
     * `mdi:mdi-content-copy`.
     */
    default?: (bindings: IClipboardScopedSlotBindings) => any
    /**
     * Custom feedback marker rendered when `showFeedback` is true and
     * `copied` is true. Scoped — receives the boolean for symmetry
     * with the default slot.
     */
    feedback?: (bindings: { copied: boolean }) => any
}

props.name: default
props.description_fallback: Custom trigger. Scoped — receives { copy, copied, error }. When omitted the component renders a default icon button with mdi:mdi-content-copy.

props.name: feedback
props.description_fallback: Custom feedback marker rendered when copied is true. Scoped — receives the boolean for symmetry with the default slot.

examples.title_fallback: Custom trigger and feedback slots

entry.name: ICodeProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamCode — a shiki-powered code block with line numbers, line highlighting, copy button, compact pill mode and theme awareness.
entry.definition:
export interface ICodeProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, IBorderProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IDimensionProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, ITypographyProps {
    /**
     * The code to highlight. When omitted, the default slot is used as the
     * source — useful for multi-line snippets that are nicer to read in
     * the template than as a JS string literal. When both are provided,
     * the prop wins.
     */
    code?: string
    /**
     * Language grammar to apply. One of the values in `SUPPORTED_LANGS`.
     * Unknown values fall back to `plaintext` (with a one-shot console
     * warning).
     */
    lang?: TCodeLang
    /**
     * Show a left gutter with line numbers. Pure CSS — uses a CSS counter
     * incremented per `.origam-code__row`, no JS layout work.
     */
    lineNumbers?: boolean
    /**
     * Lines to highlight. Accepts either a pre-computed array of 1-based
     * line numbers (`[2, 5, 6, 7]`) or a comma/range string (`'2,5-7'`)
     * that the component parses via `parseHighlightLines()`. `null` /
     * empty disables the feature.
     */
    highlightLines?: number[] | string | null
    /**
     * Show the copy-to-clipboard button in the top-right corner. Defaults
     * to `true` — most code blocks benefit from it.
     */
    copyable?: boolean
    /**
     * Auto-format the code before highlighting. Reserved for v3 — prettier
     * is intentionally NOT bundled at runtime to keep the tarball small.
     * Currently a no-op; passing `true` warns once in dev and pre-formats
     * via a best-effort whitespace trim only.
     */
    format?: boolean
    /**
     * Wrap long lines instead of scrolling horizontally. Off by default so
     * the visual column matches the source file.
     */
    wrap?: boolean
    /**
     * Optional filename rendered in the header bar (e.g. `'App.vue'`).
     * When provided AND `copyable` is true, the header also hosts the copy
     * button; otherwise the copy button is absolutely positioned in the
     * top-right corner of the code surface.
     */
    filename?: string
    /**
     * Render the block as a single-line **pill** instead of a multi-line
     * surface — ideal for a copyable install command (`npm install origam`).
     * In compact mode the header, filename and line-numbers are suppressed,
     * vertical padding shrinks to one line, the surface keeps its themed
     * tokens, and the copy control collapses to a small icon button anchored
     * inline at the end of the row (not a floating text "Copy"). The `<figure>
     * <pre><code>` semantics are preserved.
     */
    compact?: boolean
    /**
     * Decorative prompt prefix rendered BEFORE the code (e.g. `'$'` for a
     * shell command). Purely visual: it is NOT part of the highlighted code
     * and is NEVER included in the clipboard copy (only `code` is copied).
     * Most useful paired with `compact` for an install-command pill.
     */
    prompt?: string
}

props.name: code
props.description_fallback: The code to highlight. When omitted the default slot is used as the source. When both are provided the prop wins.

props.name: lang
props.description_fallback: Language grammar to apply (e.g. "typescript", "vue"). Unknown values fall back to "plaintext" with a one-shot console warning.

props.name: lineNumbers
props.description_fallback: Show a left gutter with line numbers. Pure CSS — uses a CSS counter incremented per .origam-code__row.

props.name: highlightLines
props.description_fallback: Lines to highlight. Accepts a pre-computed array of 1-based line numbers or a comma/range string ("2,5-7"). null / empty disables the feature.

props.name: copyable
props.description_fallback: Show the copy-to-clipboard button in the top-right corner. Defaults to true.

props.name: format
props.description_fallback: Auto-format the code before highlighting. Reserved for v3 — currently a no-op that warns once in dev.

props.name: wrap
props.description_fallback: Wrap long lines instead of scrolling horizontally. Off by default so the visual column matches the source file.

props.name: filename
props.description_fallback: Optional filename rendered in the header bar (e.g. "App.vue"). When provided and copyable is true, the header also hosts the copy button.

props.name: compact
props.description_fallback: Render the block as a single-line pill — ideal for a copyable install command. Suppresses header, filename and line-numbers.

props.name: prompt
props.description_fallback: Decorative prompt prefix rendered before the code (e.g. "$" for a shell command). Purely visual — never included in the clipboard copy.

examples.title_fallback: Basic code block with language and line numbers
examples.code:
<OrigamCode lang="typescript" :line-numbers="true" :copyable="true">
const x: number = 42
</OrigamCode>

examples.title_fallback: Compact install-command pill
examples.code:
<OrigamCode lang="bash" :compact="true" prompt="$">
npm install origam
</OrigamCode>

entry.name: ICommand
entry.description_fallback: A single command/action exposed by OrigamCommandPalette — holds the stable id, label, optional description, icon, keyboard shortcut hint, group, extra search keywords and the perform handler.
entry.definition:
export interface ICommand {
    /**
     * Stable identifier. Used as the v-for key, the ARIA
     * `aria-activedescendant` target and as the dedup key in the
     * registry — registering twice with the same `id` overwrites
     * the previous entry instead of duplicating it.
     */
    id: string
    /** Visible primary label. Drives the default fuzzy-match input. */
    label: string
    /** Secondary line shown under the label (optional). */
    description?: string
    /** Optional prepend icon. */
    icon?: TIcon
    /**
     * Keyboard shortcut hint rendered through `<OrigamKbd>` next to
     * the action. Display-only — it does NOT bind a global listener.
     * (To bind a shortcut globally, use the `hotkey` prop on the
     * palette OR the `useHotkey` composable on the call site.)
     */
    kbd?: ReadonlyArray<string>
    /**
     * Group label (e.g. "Navigation", "Settings"). Commands sharing
     * the same group render under the same subheader. Commands
     * without a group fall back to a default bucket rendered first.
     */
    group?: string
    /**
     * Extra search terms that should match the command (e.g.
     * `['preferences', 'config']` for a "Settings" entry).
     */
    keywords?: ReadonlyArray<string>
    /**
     * Handler invoked when the command is selected. May be async — the
     * palette awaits the returned promise before closing (when
     * `closeOnSelect` is `true`).
     */
    perform: () => void | Promise<void>
    /** Disabled commands stay visible but cannot be selected. */
    disabled?: boolean
}

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Stable identifier used as the v-for key, the ARIA aria-activedescendant target and the dedup key in the registry.

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Visible primary label. Drives the default fuzzy-match input.

props.name: description
props.description_fallback: Secondary line shown under the label.

props.name: icon
props.description_fallback: Optional prepend icon.

props.name: kbd
props.description_fallback: Keyboard shortcut hint rendered through OrigamKbd next to the action. Display-only — does NOT bind a global listener.

props.name: group
props.description_fallback: Group label (e.g. "Navigation", "Settings"). Commands sharing the same group render under the same subheader.

props.name: keywords
props.description_fallback: Extra search terms that should match the command (e.g. ["preferences", "config"] for a "Settings" entry).

props.name: perform
props.description_fallback: Handler invoked when the command is selected. May be async — the palette awaits the returned promise before closing when closeOnSelect is true.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disabled commands stay visible but cannot be selected.

examples.title_fallback: Registering a command via the composable
Declaring commands inline on the palette
examples.code:
<OrigamCommandPalette
    v-model="open"
    :commands="[
        { id: 'dark', label: 'Toggle dark mode', icon: 'mdi-theme-light-dark', perform: toggleTheme },
    ]"
/>

entry.name: ICommandPaletteProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamCommandPalette — a global ⌘K command palette that fuzzy-searches registered commands, supports custom hotkeys, grouping, async loading and slot customisation.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: Whether the palette is open. Used with v-model.

props.name: hotkey
props.description_fallback: Global hotkey(s) that toggle the palette. Defaults to [["meta","k"],["ctrl","k"]]. Pass null to disable.

props.name: commands
props.description_fallback: Static command list. When omitted the palette uses commands registered via the useCommand() global registry.

props.name: placeholder
props.description_fallback: Placeholder of the search input (already-translated string).

props.name: emptyText
props.description_fallback: Empty-state message shown when no command matches the query.

props.name: maxHeight
props.description_fallback: Max height of the result list. Number → px.

props.name: width
props.description_fallback: Width of the palette dialog. Number → px.

props.name: loading
props.description_fallback: Display a loader inside the result list (use during async command fetches).

props.name: closeOnSelect
props.description_fallback: Close the palette automatically when a command is selected.

props.name: closeOnEscape
props.description_fallback: Close when the user presses Escape.

props.name: closeOnBackdrop
props.description_fallback: Close when the user clicks the backdrop.

examples.title_fallback: Minimal palette with default hotkey
examples.code: <OrigamCommandPalette v-model="open" />

examples.title_fallback: Custom hotkey and width with async loading
examples.code:
<OrigamCommandPalette
    v-model="open"
    :hotkey="[['ctrl', 'p']]"
    :loading="fetching"
    :width="560"
    :close-on-select="true"
    @query="fetchCommands"
/>

entry.name: IConfirmWrapperProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamConfirmWrapper> — a "type-it-twice" form helper that pairs a primary input with a confirmation input and lights up only when both values match. Inherits layout, color, density, rounded, elevation, variant and focus mixins.
entry.definition:
export interface IConfirmWrapperProps extends ICommonsComponentProps,
    IAdjacentProps, IDirectionProps, IColorProps, IDensityProps,
    IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IVariantProps, IFocusProps {
    modelValue?: any
    confirm?: any
    field?: string
    defaults?: Record<string, any>
    confirmLabel?: string
    disabled?: boolean
    readonly?: boolean
    required?: boolean
    error?: boolean
    errorMessages?: string | string[]
    hideDetails?: boolean | 'auto'
    messages?: string | string[]
    hint?: string
    persistentHint?: boolean
    centerAffix?: boolean
    label?: string
}

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: Value bound via v-model — the primary input value.

props.name: confirm
props.description_fallback: Value of the confirmation input. The wrapper is "confirmed" when modelValue === confirm.

props.name: field
props.description_fallback: Field name forwarded to the internal inputs for form libraries (e.g. VeeValidate).

props.name: defaults
props.description_fallback: Default prop values injected into the internal input slot components.

props.name: confirmLabel
props.description_fallback: Label text rendered on the confirmation input.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables both inputs and prevents interaction.

props.name: readonly
props.description_fallback: Makes both inputs read-only.

props.name: required
props.description_fallback: Marks both inputs as required (triggers native form validation).

props.name: error
props.description_fallback: Forces the error state on the wrapper.

props.name: errorMessages
props.description_fallback: One or more error messages displayed below the inputs.

props.name: hideDetails
props.description_fallback: Hides the details/messages row. Set to "auto" to hide it only when there are no messages.

props.name: messages
props.description_fallback: Informational messages displayed in the details area.

props.name: hint
props.description_fallback: Hint text shown below the inputs.

props.name: persistentHint
props.description_fallback: Forces the hint to always be visible (not only on focus).

props.name: centerAffix
props.description_fallback: Vertically centres the prepend/append affixes instead of aligning them to the top.

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Label text for the primary input.

examples.title_fallback: Password confirmation field
examples.code:
<OrigamConfirmWrapper
    v-model="password"
    v-model:confirm="passwordConfirm"
    label="Password"
    confirm-label="Confirm password"
    :error="!!error"
    :error-messages="error"
/>

entry.name: IConfirmWrapperSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot contract for <OrigamConfirmWrapper> — exposes the primary input slot, the confirmation input slot, a header slot, title/messages/message/details helpers and the full adjacent-slots surface.
entry.definition:
export interface IConfirmWrapperSlots extends IAdjacentSlots {
    default?: () => any
    confirm?: () => any
    header?: () => any
    title?: (props: any) => any
    messages?: (data: { hasMessages: boolean, messages: string[], messagesId: string }) => any
    message?: (data: { message: string }) => any
    details?: (data: { id: string, messagesId: string, isDirty: boolean, isDisabled: boolean, isReadonly: boolean }) => any
}

props.name: default
props.description_fallback: Primary input slot — the first field the user fills in.

props.name: confirm
props.description_fallback: Confirmation input slot — the second field that must match the first.

props.name: header
props.description_fallback: Optional header rendered above both inputs.

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Title content area above the input group.

props.name: messages
props.type_label: (data: { hasMessages: boolean, messages: string[], messagesId: string }) => any
props.description_fallback: Override the default messages area; receives hasMessages flag, message strings and the messagesId for aria linking.

props.name: message
props.description_fallback: Template for a single message item.

props.name: details
props.type_label: (data: { id: string, messagesId: string, isDirty: boolean, isDisabled: boolean, isReadonly: boolean }) => any
props.description_fallback: Details/hint area below the inputs — receives the field state flags.

examples.title_fallback: Custom messages slot on ConfirmWrapper
