examples.code: Content stretches full width.

entry.name: IContextualMenuProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamContextualMenu> — a right-click / long-press floating menu. Inherits the full IMenuProps surface (activator, positioning, transitions, dimensions) and the commons component contract.
entry.definition:
export interface IContextualMenuProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IMenuProps {

}

examples.title_fallback: Right-click contextual menu
examples.code:
<OrigamContextualMenu activator="parent" openOnContextMenu>
    <OrigamList>
        <OrigamListItem title="Copy" />
        <OrigamListItem title="Paste" />
    </OrigamList>
</OrigamContextualMenu>

entry.name: IContrastOptions
entry.description_fallback: Runtime text-contrast guard configuration consumed by the v-contrast directive. enabled toggles the feature; threshold sets the minimum WCAG contrast ratio (default 4.5 — AA for normal text).

props.name: enabled
props.description_fallback: Whether the contrast guard is active. Defaults to true.

props.name: threshold
props.description_fallback: Minimum acceptable WCAG contrast ratio. Defaults to 4.5 (AA for normal text).

examples.title_fallback: Configuring the contrast guard at plugin install
examples.code:
import { createOrigam } from 'origam'
import type { IContrastOptions } from 'origam'

const contrastOptions: IContrastOptions = { enabled: true, threshold: 3 }

createOrigam({ contrast: contrastOptions })

entry.name: IControlProps
entry.description_fallback: Minimal prop shape for standalone icon-button controls (clear, visibility-toggle, counter actions). Requires an icon, a class string, and an accessible aria-label.

props.name: icon
props.description_fallback: Icon identifier — MDI name, URL, or component — shown inside the control button.

props.name: class
props.description_fallback: CSS class applied to the control root element.

examples.title_fallback: Passing IControlProps to a field clear button

entry.name: ICounterProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamCounter> — a numeric badge that clamps to a max value, with the full design surface (color, border, spacing, elevation, density, rounded, transition).

props.name: active
props.description_fallback: When true the counter is visible. When false it is hidden.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables the counter, muting its interactive appearance.

props.name: max
props.description_fallback: Maximum displayable value. When `value` exceeds `max`, the counter displays `{max}+`.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Numeric value to display inside the counter badge.

examples.title_fallback: Counter with a max cap
examples.code: <OrigamCounter :value="42" :max="99" color="primary" active />

entry.name: IDataItem
entry.description_fallback: Shape of a single item when <OrigamDataList> runs in its default "avatar" mode — a title descriptor (IDataTitleProps) and an array or keyed map of text rows (IDataTextProps), both optional, plus all IAdjacentProps for prepend/append slots.

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Title descriptor rendered as a <dt> cell above the text rows.

props.name: text
props.description_fallback: One or more text row descriptors rendered as <dd> cells below the title.

examples.title_fallback: Passing items in avatar mode
examples.code:
<OrigamDataList :items="[
    { title: { text: 'User' }, text: [{ text: 'alice@example.com' }] }
]" />

entry.name: IDataListKVItem
entry.description_fallback: Shape of a single key/value row when <OrigamDataList> runs in mode="kv". The key is the visible label in the <dt> cell; the value fills the <dd> cell as plain text, a VNode, or an IDataListKVItemValueComponent descriptor.

props.name: key
props.description_fallback: Visible label rendered in the <dt> cell. Also kebab-cased to build the row data-cy selector.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Value rendered in the <dd> cell. Plain text or number is rendered as-is; VNode is rendered directly; IDataListKVItemValueComponent is resolved via <component :is>.

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Optional stable row id. Falls back to key when omitted — supply your own when keys may repeat across instances.

examples.title_fallback: Key/value list with a chip as value
examples.code:
<OrigamDataList mode="kv" :items="[
    { key: 'Status', value: { component: 'origam-chip', props: { color: 'success' }, children: 'Active' } },
    { key: 'Plan', value: 'Pro' }
]" />

entry.name: IDataListKVItemValueComponent
entry.description_fallback: Dynamic-component descriptor for a KV row value. Used when a consumer wants the value cell of <OrigamDataList mode="kv"> to render a registered Origam component (chip, avatar, link…) without writing a slot.

props.name: component
props.description_fallback: The Vue component itself (imported) or a registered global tag name (e.g. "origam-chip").

props.name: props
props.description_fallback: Props forwarded as-is to the resolved component.

props.name: children
props.description_fallback: Content rendered inside the component default slot — handy for components that take their text as a slot rather than a prop.

examples.title_fallback: Rendering a chip in a KV value cell

entry.name: IDataListProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamDataList> — a versatile list component that supports two layout modes: "avatar" (classic dl stack) and "kv" (PDF-aligned key/value table), with the full design surface.

props.name: mode
props.description_fallback: Layout selector: "avatar" (default, dl layout with title+text rows) or "kv" (PDF-aligned key/value table).

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Source list. Shape depends on mode: IDataItem for "avatar", IDataListKVItem for "kv".

examples.title_fallback: DataList in kv mode
examples.code:
<OrigamDataList
    mode="kv"
    :items="[{ key: 'Name', value: 'Alice' }, { key: 'Plan', value: 'Pro' }]"
    rounded="md"
    border
/>

entry.name: IDataTableColumnProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for a single column cell in <OrigamDataTable> — alignment, sticky offset, nowrap, plus dimension and padding from the shared Commons interfaces.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableColumnProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, IDimensionProps, IPaddingProps {
    align?: ALIGN.START | ALIGN.END | ALIGN.CENTER
    fixed?: boolean
    fixedOffset?: number | string
    lastFixed?: boolean
    nowrap?: boolean
}

props.name: align
props.description_fallback: Horizontal alignment of the cell content — start, end, or center.

props.name: fixed
props.description_fallback: Pins the column to the left side of the table when the user scrolls horizontally.

props.name: fixedOffset
props.description_fallback: Left offset (px or CSS length) used to stack multiple fixed columns side by side.

props.name: lastFixed
props.description_fallback: Marks this as the last fixed column, triggering a box-shadow divider on its right edge.

props.name: nowrap
props.description_fallback: Prevents text wrapping inside the cell.

examples.title_fallback: Fixed left column with center alignment
examples.code: <OrigamDataTableColumn fixed :fixedOffset="0" align="center" nowrap />

entry.name: IDataTableEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emits contract for <OrigamDataTable> — pagination, sorting, grouping, expansion, selection and the v-model:options aggregate event.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableEmits extends ICommonsComponentEmits {
    (e: 'update:page', value: number): void
    (e: 'update:itemsPerPage', value: number): void
    (e: 'update:sortBy', value: UnwrapRef<IDataTableProvideSort['sortBy']>): void
    (e: 'update:options', value: Record<string, unknown>): void
    (e: 'update:groupBy', value: UnwrapRef<IDataTableProvideGroup['groupBy']>): void
    (e: 'update:expanded', value: ReadonlySet<unknown>): void
    (e: 'update:currentItems', value: Array<IDataTableItem>): void
}

examples.title_fallback: Listening to pagination and sort events
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    v-model:page="page"
    v-model:sortBy="sortBy"
    @update:options="handleOptions"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableExpandProps
entry.description_fallback: Props controlling row expansion in <OrigamDataTable> — whether rows expand on click, whether the expand column is shown, and the list of currently expanded row keys.

props.name: expandOnClick
props.description_fallback: Expand a row by clicking anywhere on it, not just the expand icon.

props.name: showExpand
props.description_fallback: Show the expand toggle column in the header and each data row.

props.name: expanded
props.description_fallback: Array of row keys that are currently expanded. Use with v-model:expanded.

examples.title_fallback: Expandable rows with v-model
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    show-expand
    expand-on-click
    v-model:expanded="expanded"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableFooterProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamDataTableFooter> — pagination icons, per-page options, accessible page labels, and the full color/padding/alignment design surface.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableFooterProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IPaddingProps, IAlignProps {
    prevIcon?: TIcon
    nextIcon?: TIcon
    firstIcon?: TIcon
    lastIcon?: TIcon
    itemsPerPageText?: string
    pageText?: string
    firstPageLabel?: string
    prevPageLabel?: string
    nextPageLabel?: string
    lastPageLabel?: string
    itemsPerPageOptions?: Array<number | { title: string, value: number }>
    showCurrentPage?: boolean
}

props.name: prevIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon for the previous-page button.

props.name: nextIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon for the next-page button.

props.name: firstIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon for the first-page button.

props.name: lastIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon for the last-page button.

props.name: itemsPerPageText
props.description_fallback: Label rendered before the items-per-page selector.

props.name: pageText
props.description_fallback: Label template for the current page range (e.g. "1-10 of 42").

props.name: firstPageLabel
props.description_fallback: Accessible label for the first-page button.

props.name: prevPageLabel
props.description_fallback: Accessible label for the previous-page button.

props.name: nextPageLabel
props.description_fallback: Accessible label for the next-page button.

props.name: lastPageLabel
props.description_fallback: Accessible label for the last-page button.

props.name: itemsPerPageOptions
props.description_fallback: Options for the items-per-page selector. Can be plain numbers or objects with a display title.

props.name: showCurrentPage
props.description_fallback: Display the current page number in the footer.

examples.title_fallback: Custom items-per-page options with labels
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTableFooter
    :itemsPerPageOptions="[10, 25, { title: 'All', value: -1 }]"
    itemsPerPageText="Rows per page:"
    show-current-page
/>

entry.name: IDataTableGroup
entry.description_fallback: Internal representation of a group node in a grouped <OrigamDataTable>. Carries the group key, value, nesting depth, and the flattened list of child items or nested sub-groups.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableGroup<T = any> {
    type: 'group'
    depth: number
    id: string
    key: string
    value: any
    items: Array<(T | IDataTableGroup<T>)>
}

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: Discriminant literal — always "group". Used to distinguish group nodes from item nodes at runtime.

props.name: depth
props.description_fallback: Nesting depth of this group (0 = top-level, 1 = nested, …).

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Stable unique id for this group node, used as the key in the opened set.

props.name: key
props.description_fallback: Column key this group was built from (matches the groupBy sortItem key).

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: The shared value of this group (e.g. the department name when grouping by department).

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Children of this group — either data items or nested IDataTableGroup nodes when groupBy has multiple levels.

examples.title_fallback: Grouping rows by department
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :groupBy="[{ key: 'department' }]"
    :items="employees"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableGroupHeaderRowProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the group header row component in <OrigamDataTable> — carries the group item, its resolved internal item, column definitions, and handlers for expand/select/group-toggle interactions.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableGroupHeaderRowProps<T = IDataTableGroup> extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IPaddingProps {
    index: number
    item: T
    internalItem: IDataTableItem<T>
    columns: IInternalDataTableHeader[]
    isExpanded: (item: IDataTableItem) => boolean
    toggleExpand: (item: IDataTableItem) => void
    isSelected: (items: IDataTableSelectableItem | Array<IDataTableSelectableItem>) => boolean
    toggleSelect: (item: IDataTableSelectableItem) => void
    toggleGroup: (group: IDataTableGroup) => void
}

props.name: index
props.description_fallback: Position of this group header in the rendered row list.

props.name: item
props.description_fallback: The raw group descriptor (IDataTableGroup by default).

props.name: internalItem
props.description_fallback: The resolved internal item wrapping the group for selection/expansion APIs.

props.name: columns
props.description_fallback: Flattened column definitions, used to render the correct number of cells.

props.name: isExpanded
props.description_fallback: Returns true when the given row is currently expanded.

props.name: toggleExpand
props.description_fallback: Toggles the expanded state of the given row.

props.name: isSelected
props.description_fallback: Returns true when all given items are currently selected.

props.name: toggleSelect
props.description_fallback: Toggles the selected state of the given item.

props.name: toggleGroup
props.description_fallback: Opens or closes the given group.

examples.title_fallback: Custom group header using slot props

entry.name: IDataTableGroupHeaderSlot
entry.description_fallback: Slot props injected into the #group-header slot of <OrigamDataTable> — extends IDataTableItemBase with the group item, column list, and group-open/select/expand handlers.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableGroupHeaderSlot<T = IDataTableGroup> extends IDataTableItemBase<T> {
    index: number
    item: T
    columns: IInternalDataTableHeader[]
    isExpanded: (item: IDataTableItem) => boolean
    toggleExpand: (item: IDataTableItem) => void
    isSelected: (items: IDataTableSelectableItem | Array<IDataTableSelectableItem>) => boolean
    toggleSelect: (item: IDataTableSelectableItem) => void
    toggleGroup: (group: IDataTableGroup) => void
    isGroupOpen: (group: IDataTableGroup) => boolean
}

props.name: index
props.description_fallback: Position of this group header in the rendered list.

props.name: item
props.description_fallback: The group descriptor (IDataTableGroup by default).

props.name: columns
props.description_fallback: Flattened column definitions.

props.name: isExpanded
props.description_fallback: Returns true when the given row is expanded.

props.name: toggleExpand
props.description_fallback: Toggles row expansion.

props.name: isSelected
props.description_fallback: Returns true when all given items are selected.

props.name: toggleSelect
props.description_fallback: Toggles row selection.

props.name: toggleGroup
props.description_fallback: Opens or closes the group.

props.name: isGroupOpen
props.description_fallback: Returns true when the group is currently open (expanded).

examples.title_fallback: Rendering a custom group header row

entry.name: IDataTableGroupProps
entry.description_fallback: Props controlling row grouping in <OrigamDataTable> — accepts an array of sort-item descriptors that define which columns to group by.

props.name: groupBy
props.description_fallback: Columns to group rows by. Each entry is an IDataTableSortItem with a key and optional order. Use v-model:groupBy for two-way binding.

examples.title_fallback: Group by a single column
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :groupBy="[{ key: 'department', order: 'asc' }]"
    :items="employees"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableGroupableItem
entry.description_fallback: Minimal discriminated-union marker for a data row node in a grouped DataTable tree. Carries a type="item" literal to distinguish row nodes from IDataTableGroup group nodes.

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: Discriminant literal — always "item". Distinguishes data row nodes from group nodes (type: "group") in the mixed tree.

props.name: raw
props.description_fallback: The original data object for this row.

examples.title_fallback: Type-narrowing in a custom cell renderer

entry.name: IDataTableHeader
entry.description_fallback: Definition of a single column header in <OrigamDataTable> — key, value accessor, title, alignment, fixed pinning, width/minWidth/maxWidth, sorting callbacks, filter function, mobile visibility, and nested children for multi-row headers.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableHeader<T = any> {
    key?: 'data-table-group' | 'data-table-select' | 'data-table-expand' | (string & {})
    value?: TSelectItemKey<T>
    title?: string

    fixed?: boolean
    align?: ALIGN.START | ALIGN.END | ALIGN.CENTER

    width?: number | string
    minWidth?: string
    maxWidth?: string
    nowrap?: boolean

    headerProps?: any
    cellProps?: TDataTableHeaderCell

    sortable?: boolean
    sort?: TDataTableCompareFunction
    sortRaw?: TDataTableCompareFunction
    filter?: TFilterFunction

    mobile?: boolean

    children?: Array<IDataTableHeader<T>>
}

props.name: key
props.description_fallback: Unique column identifier. Reserved values trigger built-in columns (group toggle, row select checkbox, row expand toggle).

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Accessor for the cell value from the row object. Can be a dot-path string, a function, or an array path.

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Display label in the column header cell.

props.name: fixed
props.description_fallback: Pins the column to the left edge on horizontal scroll.

props.name: align
props.description_fallback: Horizontal text alignment of all cells in this column.

props.name: width
props.description_fallback: Fixed width for the column (number = px, string = any CSS length).

props.name: minWidth
props.description_fallback: Minimum CSS width for the column.

props.name: maxWidth
props.description_fallback: Maximum CSS width for the column.

props.name: nowrap
props.description_fallback: Prevents cell text from wrapping.

props.name: headerProps
props.description_fallback: Extra props/attributes spread onto the <th> element.

props.name: cellProps
props.description_fallback: Extra props/attributes spread onto each <td> cell in this column.

props.name: sortable
props.description_fallback: Enables sorting on this column. Defaults to true when value is defined.

props.name: sort
props.description_fallback: Custom sort comparator applied to resolved cell values.

props.name: sortRaw
props.description_fallback: Custom sort comparator applied to raw row objects.

props.name: filter
props.description_fallback: Custom filter function for this column.

props.name: mobile
props.description_fallback: Whether this column appears in the mobile stacked layout.

props.name: children
props.description_fallback: Nested sub-columns for multi-row header spans.

examples.title_fallback: Headers with a fixed column and custom sort
examples.code:
const headers: IDataTableHeader[] = [
    { key: 'name', title: 'Name', fixed: true, sortable: true },
    { key: 'email', title: 'Email', align: 'start' },
    { key: 'status', title: 'Status', sortable: false },
]

entry.name: IDataTableHeaderCellColumnSlot
entry.description_fallback: Slot props injected into per-column header cell slots of <OrigamDataTable> — provides the column definition plus sort helpers (isSorted, toggleSort, getSortIcon) and selection helpers (selectAll, someSelected, allSelected).
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableHeaderCellColumnSlot {
    column: IInternalDataTableHeader
    selectAll: (value: boolean) => void
    isSorted: (column: IInternalDataTableHeader) => boolean
    toggleSort: (column: IInternalDataTableHeader) => void
    sortBy: Ref<Array<IDataTableSortItem>>
    someSelected: ComputedRef<boolean>
    allSelected: ComputedRef<boolean>
    getSortIcon: (column: IInternalDataTableHeader) => TIcon
}

props.name: column
props.description_fallback: The resolved internal column definition for this header cell.

props.name: selectAll
props.description_fallback: Selects or deselects all rows.

props.name: isSorted
props.description_fallback: Returns true when the column is part of the active sort.

props.name: toggleSort
props.description_fallback: Cycles the sort direction for the column (none → asc → desc).

props.name: sortBy
props.description_fallback: Reactive list of active sort columns.

props.name: someSelected
props.description_fallback: True when at least one row is selected (used for the indeterminate checkbox state).

props.name: allSelected
props.description_fallback: True when every visible row is selected.

props.name: getSortIcon
props.description_fallback: Returns the correct sort indicator icon for the column.

examples.title_fallback: Custom header cell with sort indicator

entry.name: IDataTableHeaderCellProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the header cell wrapper component in <OrigamDataTable> — the full headers matrix plus the shared IHeaderCellProps and IColorProps surfaces.

examples.title_fallback: Passing a resolved headers matrix

entry.name: IDataTableHeaderProps
entry.description_fallback: Top-level header configuration props accepted by <OrigamDataTable> — the array of column header definitions and the initial items array used to infer column keys.

props.name: headers
props.description_fallback: Column header definitions. Each entry configures key, title, alignment, sorting, filtering and more.

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Data rows. Passed here so the header can infer column keys when no explicit headers are provided.

examples.title_fallback: Defining headers on OrigamDataTable
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :headers="[{ key: 'name', title: 'Name' }, { key: 'age', title: 'Age' }]"
    :items="users"
/>

entry.description_fallback: Props for the mobile-only merged header cell of a data table, grouping all visible columns into a single collapsible cell with an optional colspan.

props.name: columns
props.description_fallback: List of resolved internal header descriptors to render inside the mobile cell.

props.name: colspan
props.description_fallback: Number of columns this cell should span horizontally.

entry.name: IDataTableHeadersCellProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the headers cell component (single <th>) in <OrigamDataTable> — the full two-dimensional headers matrix plus the shared ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, and IHeaderCellProps surfaces.

props.name: headers

entry.name: IDataTableHeadersProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for the DataTable headers row — extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IDisplayProps, ILoaderProps, and IHeaderCellProps to control appearance, sorting icons, stickiness and loading state of the header area.

export interface IDataTableHeadersProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IDisplayProps, ILoaderProps, IHeaderCellProps {

entry.name: IDataTableHeadersSlotProps
entry.description_fallback: Slot props injected into the DataTable headers slot — provides column definitions, sort state, selection state, and helpers to toggle sort, select-all, and retrieve the sort icon.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableHeadersSlotProps {
    headers: Array<Array<IInternalDataTableHeader>>
    columns: Array<IInternalDataTableHeader>
    sortBy: UnwrapRef<Ref<Array<IDataTableSortItem>>>
    someSelected: UnwrapRef<ComputedRef<boolean>>
    allSelected: UnwrapRef<ComputedRef<boolean>>
    toggleSort: (column: IInternalDataTableHeader) => void
    selectAll: (value: boolean) => void
    getSortIcon: (column: IInternalDataTableHeader) => TIcon | undefined
    isSorted: (column: IInternalDataTableHeader) => boolean
}

props.name: headers
props.description_fallback: Resolved header rows (supports multi-row/span headers).

props.name: columns
props.description_fallback: Flat list of leaf columns, used to iterate cells.

props.name: sortBy
props.description_fallback: Current sort configuration (key + direction).

props.name: someSelected
props.description_fallback: True when at least one row is selected (indeterminate state).

props.name: allSelected
props.description_fallback: True when every selectable row is selected.

props.name: toggleSort
props.description_fallback: Cycles sort direction for the given column.

props.name: selectAll
props.description_fallback: Selects or deselects all rows.

props.name: getSortIcon
props.description_fallback: Returns the icon name matching the column sort direction.

props.name: isSorted
props.description_fallback: Returns true if the column is part of the active sort.

examples.title_fallback: Custom header slot leveraging IDataTableHeadersSlotProps

entry.name: IDataTableItem
entry.description_fallback: Internal representation of a single data-table row — extends IInternalItem, IDataTableGroupableItem, and IDataTableSelectableItem with a unique key, row index, and a pre-computed column values map.

props.name: key
props.description_fallback: Unique key derived from itemValue; used to track selection and expansion state.

props.name: index
props.description_fallback: Zero-based position of the item in the current (possibly filtered/sorted) dataset.

props.name: columns
props.description_fallback: Pre-resolved cell values keyed by column key.

examples.title_fallback: Reading internal item values in a custom cell slot

entry.name: IDataTableItemBase
entry.description_fallback: Base shape shared by all row slot contexts in <OrigamDataTable> — provides the row index, raw item, its resolved internal item, and handlers for expand and select.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableItemBase<T = any> {
    index: number
    item: T
    internalItem: IDataTableItem<T>
    isExpanded: (item: IDataTableItem) => boolean
    toggleExpand: (item: IDataTableItem) => void
    isSelected: (items: IDataTableSelectableItem | Array<IDataTableSelectableItem>) => boolean
    toggleSelect: (item: IDataTableSelectableItem) => void
}

props.name: index
props.description_fallback: Zero-based position of this row in the rendered items list.

props.name: item
props.description_fallback: The raw data object for this row.

props.name: internalItem
props.description_fallback: The resolved internal item wrapping the raw object with computed key, columns map, and selection/expansion flags.

props.name: isExpanded
props.description_fallback: Returns true when the given row is currently expanded.

props.name: toggleExpand
props.description_fallback: Toggles the expanded state of the given row.

props.name: isSelected
props.description_fallback: Returns true when all given items are currently selected.

props.name: toggleSelect
props.description_fallback: Toggles the selected state of the given item.

examples.title_fallback: Row slot using IDataTableItemBase handlers

entry.name: IDataTableItemBaseSlot
entry.description_fallback: Slot props for row-level slots in <OrigamDataTable> — extends IDataTableItemBase with the flat columns array, used by #item and #expanded-item slots.

props.name: columns
props.description_fallback: Flat list of resolved column definitions for the current row. Use to iterate over cells in a fully custom #item slot.

examples.title_fallback: Fully custom row with dynamic column iteration

entry.name: IDataTableItemKey
entry.description_fallback: Slot props for per-cell slots in <OrigamDataTable> (e.g. #item.{key}) — extends IDataTableItemBase with the resolved cell value and the column definition.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: The resolved cell value for this column, computed from the row object via the column value accessor.

props.name: column
props.description_fallback: The resolved internal column definition for this cell.

examples.title_fallback: Custom cell renderer for the name column

entry.name: IDataTableItemSlot
entry.description_fallback: Slot scope injected into every row-level slot of <OrigamDataTable> — exposes the raw item, its index, resolved columns, and the expand/select helpers.

props.name: columns
props.description_fallback: Array of resolved header descriptors for this row — use them to iterate over cells in the correct column order.

props.name: props
props.description_fallback: Merged props to spread onto the row root element (contains class, style, data-* attributes generated by rowProps).

examples.title_fallback: Custom row slot using IDataTableItemSlot

entry.name: IDataTableItemsProps
entry.description_fallback: Configures the items data source of <OrigamDataTable> — the items array, the key extractor, the selectability resolver and per-row/cell prop factories.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableItemsProps {
    items?: Array<IDataTableItem>
    itemValue?: TSelectItemKey
    itemSelectable?: TSelectItemKey
    rowProps?: TDataTableRow<any>
    cellProps?: TDataTableCell<any>
    returnObject?: boolean
}

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Array of data objects to display, one per row.

props.name: itemValue
props.description_fallback: Property path (or function) used to derive the unique key for each item — required for selection and expansion tracking.

props.name: itemSelectable
props.description_fallback: Property path (or function) that returns a boolean indicating whether a row can be selected.

props.name: rowProps
props.description_fallback: Object or function returning extra props (class, style, data-*…) merged onto each <tr> root element.

props.name: cellProps
props.description_fallback: Object or function returning extra props merged onto each <td> cell element.

props.name: returnObject
props.description_fallback: When true, v-model:modelValue and select events return the full raw object instead of the extracted itemValue.

examples.title_fallback: DataTable with custom itemValue and rowProps
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :items="users"
    item-value="id"
    :row-props="({ item }) => ({ class: item.active ? 'active-row' : '' })"
/>

entry.name: IDataTablePaginationProps
entry.description_fallback: Controls the page and items-per-page props for paginated data tables — the minimal binding surface for server-side or client-side pagination.

props.name: page
props.description_fallback: Current page number (1-based). Bind with v-model:page for two-way sync.

props.name: itemsPerPage
props.description_fallback: Number of rows displayed per page. Bind with v-model:itemsPerPage for two-way sync.

examples.title_fallback: Server-side pagination with v-model
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    v-model:page="page"
    v-model:items-per-page="perPage"
    :items="rows"
    :items-length="total"
    @update:options="loadPage"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableProps
entry.description_fallback: Complete props contract for <OrigamDataTable> — composes all DataTable sub-interfaces (items, sort, select, paginate, expand, group, headers, footer, filters) plus table-wide visibility flags and a global search string.

props.name: hideDefaultBody
props.description_fallback: Hides the default tbody rendering — use when you provide a fully custom body via the #body slot.

props.name: hideDefaultFooter
props.description_fallback: Hides the built-in pagination footer.

props.name: hideDefaultHeader
props.description_fallback: Hides the built-in column-header row.

props.name: search
props.description_fallback: Client-side search string applied to all visible columns. Pass an empty string to clear.

examples.title_fallback: Full-featured data table
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :items="users"
    :headers="headers"
    item-value="id"
    show-select
    multi-sort
    :items-per-page="10"
    search="alice"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableProvideExpanded
entry.description_fallback: Internal provide/inject contract for row expansion state in <OrigamDataTable> — exposes the expanded set and helpers for child components (expand row, virtual row, footer) to query and toggle expansion.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableProvideExpanded {
    expand: (item: IDataTableItem, value: boolean) => void
    expanded: TVModel<IDataTableExpandProps, 'expanded', Set<string>>
    expandOnClick: Ref<boolean | undefined>
    isExpanded: (item: IDataTableItem) => boolean
    toggleExpand: (item: IDataTableItem) => void
}

props.name: expand
props.description_fallback: Imperatively sets the expansion state of the given item to value.

props.name: expanded
props.description_fallback: Reactive set of expanded item keys, kept in sync with the v-model:expanded prop.

props.name: expandOnClick
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag mirroring the expandOnClick prop — when true, clicking a row toggles its expansion.

props.name: isExpanded
props.description_fallback: Returns true when the given item is currently in the expanded set.

props.name: toggleExpand
props.description_fallback: Toggles the expansion state of the given item.

examples.title_fallback: Expand row programmatically via inject
examples.code:
import { inject } from 'vue'
import { DATA_TABLE_EXPAND_KEY } from 'origam'

const { isExpanded, toggleExpand } = inject(DATA_TABLE_EXPAND_KEY)!

entry.name: IDataTableProvideGroup
entry.description_fallback: Internal provide/inject contract for row-grouping state in <OrigamDataTable> — exposes the groupBy axis, the open/closed set of group nodes and helpers to toggle, extract and query groups.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableProvideGroup {
    sortByWithGroups: ComputedRef<Array<IDataTableSortItem>>
    toggleGroup: (group: IDataTableGroup) => void
    opened: Ref<Set<string> & Omit<Set<string>, keyof Set<any>>>
    groupBy: Ref<Array<IDataTableSortItem>>
    extractRows: (items: Array<IDataTableGroupableItem | IDataTableGroup<IDataTableGroupableItem>>) => Array<IDataTableGroupableItem>
    isGroupOpen: (group: IDataTableGroup) => boolean
}

props.name: sortByWithGroups
props.description_fallback: Computed sort array that prepends the groupBy columns so groups are contiguous before any user-defined sort keys.

props.name: toggleGroup
props.description_fallback: Toggles the open/closed state of a group node.

props.name: opened
props.description_fallback: Reactive set of group IDs that are currently open (expanded).

props.name: groupBy
props.description_fallback: Reactive array of column keys used as the grouping axis.

props.name: extractRows
props.description_fallback: Flattens a mixed array of items and group nodes into a plain array of leaf items.

props.name: isGroupOpen
props.description_fallback: Returns true when the given group is currently expanded.

examples.title_fallback: Grouping rows by a column
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :items="orders"
    :headers="headers"
    :group-by="[{ key: 'status' }]"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableProvidePagination
entry.description_fallback: Internal provide/inject contract for pagination state in <OrigamDataTable> — exposes the page cursors, the total page count and navigation helpers consumed by footer and slot children.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableProvidePagination {
    page: Ref<number>
    itemsPerPage: Ref<number>
    startIndex: ComputedRef<number>
    stopIndex: ComputedRef<number>
    pageCount: ComputedRef<number>
    itemsLength: Ref<number>
    nextPage: () => void
    prevPage: () => void
    setPage: (value: number) => void
    setItemsPerPage: (value: number) => void
}

props.name: page
props.description_fallback: Current 1-based page number as a reactive ref.

props.name: itemsPerPage
props.description_fallback: Number of rows per page as a reactive ref.

props.name: startIndex
props.description_fallback: Zero-based index of the first row on the current page.

props.name: stopIndex
props.description_fallback: Zero-based index of the last row on the current page (exclusive).

props.name: pageCount
props.description_fallback: Total number of pages computed from itemsLength and itemsPerPage.

props.name: itemsLength
props.description_fallback: Total number of items across all pages (used for server-side pagination).

props.name: nextPage
props.description_fallback: Advances to the next page if available.

props.name: prevPage
props.description_fallback: Goes back to the previous page if available.

props.name: setPage
props.description_fallback: Navigates to the given page number (clamped to valid range).

props.name: setItemsPerPage
props.description_fallback: Changes the number of rows per page and resets to page 1.

examples.title_fallback: Accessing pagination state via inject
examples.code:
import { inject } from 'vue'
import { DATA_TABLE_PAGINATION_KEY } from 'origam'

const { page, pageCount, nextPage, prevPage } = inject(DATA_TABLE_PAGINATION_KEY)!

entry.name: IDataTableProvideSelection
entry.description_fallback: Internal provide/inject contract for row-selection state in <OrigamDataTable> — exposes selection helpers and computed flags consumed by checkboxes, header select-all and slot children.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableProvideSelection {
    toggleSelect: (item: IDataTableSelectableItem) => void
    select: (items: Array<IDataTableSelectableItem>, value: boolean) => void
    selectAll: (value: boolean) => void
    isSelected: (items: IDataTableSelectableItem | Array<IDataTableSelectableItem>) => boolean
    isSomeSelected: (items: IDataTableSelectableItem | Array<IDataTableSelectableItem>) => boolean
    someSelected: ComputedRef<boolean>
    allSelected: ComputedRef<boolean>
    showSelectAll: ComputedRef<boolean>
}

props.name: toggleSelect
props.description_fallback: Toggles the selection state of a single item.

props.name: select
props.description_fallback: Sets the selection state of an array of items to value.

props.name: selectAll
props.description_fallback: Selects or deselects all selectable items on the current page (or all items, depending on selectStrategy).

props.name: isSelected
props.description_fallback: Returns true when every passed item is currently selected.

props.name: isSomeSelected
props.description_fallback: Returns true when at least one of the passed items is selected.

props.name: someSelected
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag — true when at least one row is selected (drives the indeterminate state of the header checkbox).

props.name: allSelected
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag — true when all selectable rows are selected.

props.name: showSelectAll
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag — controls whether the select-all checkbox is rendered in the header.

examples.title_fallback: Injecting selection helpers in a child component
examples.code:
import { inject } from 'vue'
import { DATA_TABLE_SELECTION_KEY } from 'origam'

const { isSelected, toggleSelect, allSelected } = inject(DATA_TABLE_SELECTION_KEY)!

entry.name: IDataTableProvideSort
entry.description_fallback: Internal provide/inject contract for column-sort state in <OrigamDataTable> — exposes the current sortBy array and helpers used by column headers to query and toggle sort order.

props.name: sortBy
props.description_fallback: Reactive array of active sort descriptors — each entry has a key and an optional direction.

props.name: toggleSort
props.description_fallback: Cycles through the sort states for the given column (none → asc → desc → none when mustSort is false).

props.name: isSorted
props.description_fallback: Returns true when the given column has an active sort entry in sortBy.

examples.title_fallback: Accessing sort state in a custom header slot
examples.code:
<template #header.name="{ column, toggleSort, isSorted }">
    <button @click="toggleSort(column)">
        Name {{ isSorted(column) ? '(sorted)' : '' }}
    </button>
</template>

entry.name: IDataTableRowEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emits contract for <OrigamDataTableRow> — fires row-level expand and select events carrying the affected item and the new boolean state.

examples.title_fallback: Listening to row-level events
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :items="rows"
    :headers="headers"
    @expand="({ item, value }) => console.log('expand', item, value)"
    @select="({ item, value }) => console.log('select', item, value)"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableRowProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for <OrigamDataTableRow> — receives the resolved internal item and optional per-cell prop factory; extends ICommonsComponentProps and IDisplayProps.

props.name: item
props.description_fallback: The resolved internal item descriptor containing key, index, raw data and column values.

props.name: cellProps
props.description_fallback: Object or function returning extra props merged onto each cell element of this row.

examples.title_fallback: Custom row with cellProps factory
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :items="orders"
    :headers="headers"
    :cell-props="({ column }) => column.key === 'amount' ? { class: 'text-right' } : {}"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableRowsProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for the internal <OrigamDataTableRows> body renderer — receives the full list of items/groups, empty-state text and per-row/cell prop factories.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableRowsProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ILoaderProps, IDisplayProps {
    hideNoData?: boolean
    items?: Array<IDataTableItem | IDataTableGroup> | readonly (IDataTableItem | IDataTableGroup)[]
    noDataText?: string
    rowProps?: TDataTableRow<any>,
    cellProps?: TDataTableCell<any>
}

props.name: hideNoData
props.description_fallback: Suppresses the empty-state row when there are no items to display.

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Mixed array of resolved items and group nodes to render in the tbody.

props.name: noDataText
props.description_fallback: Text shown in the empty-state row when items is empty and hideNoData is false.

props.name: rowProps
props.description_fallback: Object or function returning extra props merged onto each <tr> element.

props.name: cellProps
props.description_fallback: Object or function returning extra props merged onto each <td> element.

examples.title_fallback: Custom no-data text
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :items="[]"
    :headers="headers"
    no-data-text="No records found."
/>

entry.name: IDataTableSelectProps
entry.description_fallback: Props that enable and configure row selection in <OrigamDataTable> — controls the checkbox column visibility, the selection strategy, the v-model array and the equality comparator.

props.name: showSelect
props.description_fallback: Renders a checkbox column in the header and each row when true.

props.name: selectStrategy
props.description_fallback: Strategy object (or "single" | "page" | "all") controlling how select-all and individual selects interact.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: Array of selected item keys (or full objects when returnObject is true). Bind with v-model.

props.name: valueComparator
props.description_fallback: Custom equality function used to determine if two item values are the same — defaults to strict equality.

examples.title_fallback: Multi-row selection with v-model
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    v-model="selected"
    :items="users"
    :headers="headers"
    show-select
    item-value="id"
/>

entry.name: IDataTableSelectStrategy
entry.description_fallback: Strategy object that controls how rows are selected in a DataTable — defines whether a select-all checkbox is shown and implements the select / selectAll / allSelected algorithms.

props.name: showSelectAll
props.description_fallback: Whether a select-all checkbox should be rendered in the header row.

props.name: allSelected
props.description_fallback: Returns the subset of items that are considered fully selected under this strategy.

props.name: select
props.description_fallback: Applies a selection toggle on a given set of items and returns the new selected set.

props.name: selectAll
props.description_fallback: Applies a select-all or deselect-all operation and returns the resulting selected set.

examples.title_fallback: Custom page-only select strategy

entry.name: IDataTableSelectableItem
entry.description_fallback: Minimal shape that every selectable row must satisfy — carries the resolved selection key and an optional flag to opt the row out of selection.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: The resolved unique key for this item (derived from itemValue). Used as the selection set entry.

props.name: selectable
props.description_fallback: When false, the row is excluded from selection regardless of the current strategy.

examples.title_fallback: Marking rows as non-selectable
examples.code:
const users = [
    { id: 1, name: 'Alice', selectable: true },
    { id: 2, name: 'Bob',   selectable: false }, // row is disabled
]

entry.name: IDataTableSlotProps
entry.description_fallback: Full slot scope injected into top-level slots (#default, #body, #footer…) of <OrigamDataTable> — aggregates pagination, sort, selection, expansion, grouping state and all toggle helpers.
entry.definition:
export interface IDataTableSlotProps<T> {
    page: number
    itemsPerPage: number
    sortBy: UnwrapRef<IDataTableProvideSort['sortBy']>
    pageCount: number
    toggleSort: IDataTableProvideSort['toggleSort']
    setItemsPerPage: IDataTableProvidePagination['setItemsPerPage']
    someSelected: boolean
    allSelected: boolean
    isSelected: IDataTableProvideSelection['isSelected']
    select: IDataTableProvideSelection['select']
    selectAll: IDataTableProvideSelection['selectAll']
    toggleSelect: IDataTableProvideSelection['toggleSelect']
    isExpanded: IDataTableProvideExpanded['isExpanded']
    toggleExpand: IDataTableProvideExpanded['toggleExpand']
    isGroupOpen: IDataTableProvideGroup['isGroupOpen']
    toggleGroup: IDataTableProvideGroup['toggleGroup']
    items: T[]
    internalItems: Array<IDataTableItem>
    groupedItems: Array<IDataTableItem<T> | IDataTableGroup<IDataTableItem<T>>>
    columns: Array<IInternalDataTableHeader>
    headers: Array<Array<IInternalDataTableHeader>>
}

props.name: page
props.description_fallback: Current page number (1-based).

props.name: itemsPerPage
props.description_fallback: Number of rows shown per page.

props.name: sortBy
props.description_fallback: Active sort descriptors (key + direction).

props.name: pageCount
props.description_fallback: Total number of pages.

props.name: toggleSort
props.description_fallback: Cycles the sort state of the given column.

props.name: setItemsPerPage
props.description_fallback: Changes the rows-per-page setting.

props.name: someSelected
props.description_fallback: True when at least one row is selected.

props.name: allSelected
props.description_fallback: True when all selectable rows are selected.

props.name: isSelected
props.description_fallback: Checks whether all passed items are selected.

props.name: select
props.description_fallback: Sets selection state for an array of items.

props.name: selectAll
props.description_fallback: Selects or deselects all rows on the current page.

props.name: toggleSelect
props.description_fallback: Toggles selection for a single item.

props.name: isExpanded
props.description_fallback: Checks whether an item row is expanded.

props.name: toggleExpand
props.description_fallback: Toggles the expansion state of an item row.

props.name: isGroupOpen
props.description_fallback: Checks whether a group node is open.

props.name: toggleGroup
props.description_fallback: Toggles the open/closed state of a group node.

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Raw items on the current page.

props.name: internalItems
props.description_fallback: Enriched internal items on the current page.

props.name: groupedItems
props.description_fallback: Items and group nodes as rendered in the tbody.

props.name: columns
props.description_fallback: Resolved column descriptors in display order.

props.name: headers
props.description_fallback: Multi-row header matrix (for column-group headers).

examples.title_fallback: Custom footer using IDataTableSlotProps

entry.name: IDataTableSortItem
entry.description_fallback: A single sort descriptor in the sortBy array — pairs a column key with an optional sort direction. Used by both the sort props and the provide/inject sort contract.

props.name: key
props.description_fallback: The column key to sort by — must match a key in the headers array.

props.name: order
props.description_fallback: Sort direction: 'asc', 'desc', true (= 'asc') or false (= 'desc'). Omit to infer ascending.

examples.title_fallback: Initial multi-column sort
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    :items="users"
    :headers="headers"
    :sort-by="[{ key: 'lastName', order: 'asc' }, { key: 'firstName', order: 'asc' }]"
    multi-sort
/>

entry.name: IDataTableSortProps
entry.description_fallback: Props controlling the sort state of a data table — active sort keys, a custom comparator, multi-column sort, and the mustSort constraint.

props.name: sortBy
props.description_fallback: Array of sort descriptors (key + optional direction) currently active on the table.

props.name: customKeySort
props.description_fallback: Custom comparator function called instead of the default string/number sort when sorting by a given column key.

props.name: multiSort
props.description_fallback: Allow the user to sort by multiple columns simultaneously.

props.name: mustSort
props.description_fallback: Prevent the sort from being cleared — once a column is sorted it cycles between asc and desc only.

examples.title_fallback: Binding sort props on a data table

entry.name: IDataTextProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for a data-list text cell — combines layout (adjacent, density, margin, padding) with color surfaces to render a labelled text value inside a data list.

props.name: text
props.description_fallback: The text value to display inside the data cell.

examples.title_fallback: Typing props for a custom data-text wrapper

entry.name: IDataTitleProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for <OrigamDataTitle> — a labeled data-list heading with full color, density and spacing support. Extends the adjacent-content, density, margin, padding, color and bg-color surfaces.

props.name: text
props.description_fallback: The title text or numeric value to display.

examples.title_fallback: Data title with custom color
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTitle
    text="Order details"
    color="primary"
    density="compact"
/>

entry.name: IDateAdapter
entry.description_fallback: Full date-manipulation adapter contract. Implement this interface to plug any date library (date-fns, Luxon, Day.js, native Date) into Origam date components.
entry.definition:
export interface IDateAdapter<T = unknown> {
    date (value?: any): T | null

    format (date: T, formatString: string): string

    toJsDate (value: T): Date

    parseISO (date: string): T

    toISO (date: T): string

    startOfDay (date: T): T

    endOfDay (date: T): T

    startOfWeek (date: T, firstDayOfWeek?: number | string): T

    endOfWeek (date: T): T

    startOfMonth (date: T): T

    endOfMonth (date: T): T

    startOfYear (date: T): T

    endOfYear (date: T): T

    isAfter (date: T, comparing: T): boolean

    isAfterDay (value: T, comparing: T): boolean

    isSameDay (date: T, comparing: T): boolean

    isSameMonth (date: T, comparing: T): boolean

    isSameYear (value: T, comparing: T): boolean

    isBefore (date: T, comparing: T): boolean

    isEqual (date: T, comparing: T): boolean

    isValid (date: any): boolean

    isWithinRange (date: T, range: [T, T]): boolean

    addMinutes (date: T, amount: number): T

    addHours (date: T, amount: number): T

    addDays (date: T, amount: number): T

    addWeeks (date: T, amount: number): T

    addMonths (date: T, amount: number): T

    getYear (date: T): number

    setYear (date: T, year: number): T

    getDiff (date: T, comparing: T | string, unit?: string): number

    getWeekArray (date: T, firstDayOfWeek?: number | string): T[][]

    getWeekdays (firstDayOfWeek?: number | string): string[]

    getMonth (date: T): number

    setMonth (date: T, month: number): T

    getDate (date: T): number

    setDate (date: T, day: number): T

    getNextMonth (date: T): T

    getPreviousMonth (date: T): T

    getHours (date: T): number

    setHours (date: T, hours: number): T

    getMinutes (date: T): number

    setMinutes (date: T, minutes: number): T
}

examples.title_fallback: Implementing a minimal date adapter

entry.name: IDateInstance
entry.description_fallback: Adapter instance shape — extends TInternalAdapter with an optional locale property. Returned by the adapter constructor and used internally by Origam date composables.

props.name: locale
props.description_fallback: Locale configuration forwarded to the underlying date library (e.g. an i18n locale string or object).

examples.title_fallback: Implementing IDateInstance for a custom adapter class

entry.name: IDateOptions
entry.description_fallback: Plugin-level date configuration passed to createOrigam({ date: … }). Specifies the adapter class or instance, optional format overrides, and locale maps.

props.name: adapter
props.type_label: (new (options: { locale: any, formats?: any }) => IDateInstance) | IDateInstance
props.description_fallback: The date adapter — either a constructor class or an already-instantiated adapter.

props.name: formats
props.description_fallback: Optional display-format overrides keyed by format name (e.g. "keyboardDate").

props.name: locale
props.description_fallback: Locale map used by the adapter to resolve date locale strings.

examples.title_fallback: Providing IDateOptions at plugin install

entry.name: IDatePickerControlsEmits
entry.description_fallback:
Emits fired by `<OrigamDatePickerControls>` — clicks on the five
toolbar buttons (year / month / prev / next / text label).
`event` is optional: the click handlers call `emits('click:prev')`
etc. without forwarding the originating MouseEvent.
entry.definition:
export interface IDatePickerControlsEmits {
    (e: 'click:year', event?: MouseEvent): void
    (e: 'click:month', event?: MouseEvent): void
    (e: 'click:prev', event?: MouseEvent): void
    (e: 'click:next', event?: MouseEvent): void
    (e: 'click:text', event?: MouseEvent): void
}

entry.name: IDatePickerControlsProps
entry.definition:
export interface IDatePickerControlsProps extends ICommonsComponentProps {
    /**
     * Highlighted item(s) in the controls toolbar. Widened to include
     * `boolean | IActiveState` so that IDatePickerProps can extend both this
     * interface and IPickerProps (which via ISheetProps → IActiveProps declares
     * `active?: boolean | IActiveState`) without TS2320.
     */
    active?: string | Array<string> | boolean | IActiveState
    disabled?: boolean
    disabledMonth?: boolean
    disabledYear?: boolean
    disabledNext?: boolean
    disabledPrev?: boolean
    nextIcon?: TIcon
    prevIcon?: TIcon
    modeIcon?: TIcon
    text?: string,
    viewMode?: TDateMode
}

props.name: active
props.description_fallback:
Highlighted item(s) in the controls toolbar. Widened to include
`boolean | IActiveState` so that IDatePickerProps can extend both this
interface and IPickerProps (which via ISheetProps → IActiveProps declares
`active?: boolean | IActiveState`) without TS2320.

entry.name: IDatePickerEmits
entry.description_fallback:
Emits fired by `<OrigamDatePicker>` — main v-model + the three navigation
channels (month / year / viewMode) the controls and tables push up.

entry.name: IDatePickerFieldProps
entry.definition:
export interface IDatePickerFieldProps extends ITextFieldProps, ITransitionComponentProps {
    menu?: boolean,
    menuProps?: IMenuProps,
    range?: boolean
    multiple?: boolean

entry.name: IDatePickerHeaderEmits
entry.description_fallback:
Emits fired by `<OrigamDatePickerHeader>` — click on the header text.
`event` is optional: the handler calls `emits('click')` without forwarding
the originating MouseEvent.

entry.name: IDatePickerHeaderProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the DatePicker header row — shows the current month/year label and handles the click that switches between month and year views. Extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IAdjacentProps and IDensityProps.

props.name: header
props.description_fallback: Label displayed in the header (e.g. "June 2025"). Computed by the parent when not provided.

props.name: transition
props.description_fallback: Transition applied when the header label changes (month/year navigation).

examples.title_fallback: Extending for a custom header

entry.name: IDatePickerMonthProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the month-grid view of the DatePicker — renders the days of a given month with optional week numbers, multi-select, range selection and animated transitions. Extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps and ICalendarProps.
entry.definition:
export interface IDatePickerMonthProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, ICalendarProps {
    hideWeekdays?: boolean
    multiple?: boolean | number | (string & {})
    range?: boolean
    showWeek?: boolean
    transition?: TTransitionProps
    reverseTransition?: TTransitionProps
}

props.name: hideWeekdays
props.description_fallback: Hide the weekday header row (Mon Tue Wed …).

props.name: multiple
props.description_fallback: Allow selecting multiple dates. Pass a number to cap the selection count.

props.name: range
props.description_fallback: Enable range selection — the first click sets the start, the second sets the end.

props.name: showWeek
props.description_fallback: Show ISO week numbers in a leading column.

props.name: transition
props.description_fallback: Transition applied when navigating forward to the next month.

props.name: reverseTransition
props.description_fallback: Transition applied when navigating backward to the previous month.

examples.title_fallback: Range date picker with week numbers
examples.code: <OrigamDatePicker :range="true" :show-week="true" />

entry.name: IDatePickerMonthsEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emits contract for the months-grid panel of the DatePicker — fires update:month when the user clicks a month tile to switch the active month.

examples.title_fallback: Listening to month selection
examples.code:
<OrigamDatePickerMonths
    :month="currentMonth"
    :year="currentYear"
    @update:month="month => (currentMonth = month)"
/>

entry.name: IDatePickerMonthsProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the months-grid panel that lets the user pick a month — displays a 4x3 grid of month tiles, optionally bounded by min/max. Extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps and IDimensionProps.

props.name: min
props.description_fallback: Minimum selectable date boundary — months before this date are disabled.

props.name: max
props.description_fallback: Maximum selectable date boundary — months after this date are disabled.

props.name: month
props.description_fallback: Currently active zero-based month index (0 = January … 11 = December). Controls the highlighted tile.

props.name: year
props.description_fallback: Four-digit year displayed above the grid and used to compute min/max constraints.

examples.title_fallback: Standalone months panel
examples.code:
<OrigamDatePickerMonths
    :month="5"
    :year="2025"
    @update:month="m => console.log(m)"
/>

entry.name: IDatePickerProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props surface for OrigamDatePicker — aggregates color, border, elevation, spacing, picker controls, month/months/years and header sub-interfaces into the top-level component contract. Supports single, multiple and range v-model values.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: The selected date(s). A single string or Date for single/range mode; an array for multiple mode.

examples.title_fallback: Basic date picker with v-model
examples.code: <OrigamDatePicker v-model="selectedDate" color="primary" :rounded="true" />

entry.name: IDatePickerYearsEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emits contract for the years-grid panel of the DatePicker — fires update:year when the user clicks a year tile to switch the active year.

examples.title_fallback: Listening to year selection
examples.code:
<OrigamDatePickerYears
    :year="currentYear"
    @update:year="y => (currentYear = y)"
/>

entry.name: IDatePickerYearsProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the years-grid panel that lets the user pick a year — displays a scrollable list of year tiles, optionally bounded by min/max. Extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps and IDimensionProps.

props.name: min
props.description_fallback: Minimum selectable year boundary — years before this value are disabled.

props.name: max
props.description_fallback: Maximum selectable year boundary — years after this value are disabled.

props.name: year
props.description_fallback: Currently active four-digit year. Controls the highlighted tile and the initial scroll position.

examples.title_fallback: Standalone years panel bounded to 2000–2030
examples.code:
<OrigamDatePickerYears
    :year="2025"
    min="2000-01-01"
    max="2030-12-31"
    @update:year="y => console.log(y)"
/>

entry.name: IDay
entry.description_fallback: Data shape for a single day cell in the Calendar grid. Carries display strings, boolean flags for all calendar states (selected, disabled, adjacent, etc.) and the raw adapter date value.
entry.definition:
export interface IDay {
    date: unknown
    isoDate: string
    formatted: string
    year: number
    month: number
    isDisabled: boolean
    isWeekStart: boolean
    isWeekEnd: boolean
    isToday: boolean
    isAdjacent: boolean
    isHidden: boolean
    isStart: boolean
    isSelected: boolean
    isEnd: boolean
    isSame: boolean
    localized: string
}

props.name: date
props.description_fallback: Raw adapter date value for this cell.

props.name: isoDate
props.description_fallback: ISO-8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD) for this cell.

props.name: formatted
props.description_fallback: Adapter-formatted display string for the day number.

props.name: year
props.description_fallback: Four-digit year of this cell.

props.name: month
props.description_fallback: Zero-based month index of this cell.

props.name: isDisabled
props.description_fallback: True when the day is outside the allowed date range.

props.name: isWeekStart
props.description_fallback: True when the day is the first day of a week row.

props.name: isWeekEnd
props.description_fallback: True when the day is the last day of a week row.

props.name: isToday
props.description_fallback: True when the day corresponds to today's date.

props.name: isAdjacent
props.description_fallback: True when the day belongs to the previous or next month (overflow cell).

props.name: isHidden
props.description_fallback: True when the cell should be visually hidden.

props.name: isStart
props.description_fallback: True when the day is the start of a selected range.

props.name: isSelected
props.description_fallback: True when the day is the selected date or within a selected range.

props.name: isEnd
props.description_fallback: True when the day is the end of a selected range.

props.name: isSame
props.description_fallback: True when start and end of the range land on the same day.

props.name: localized
props.description_fallback: Locale-aware display string for the day number (e.g. "١" in Arabic).

examples.title_fallback: Consuming IDay in a calendar slot

entry.name: IDefault
entry.description_fallback: Shape of the defaults map consumed by OrigamDefaultsProvider and the createOrigam theme component layer. Keys are "global" (applies to every component) or a component kebab-case name (e.g. "origam-btn"). Values are plain prop records.

props.name: global
props.description_fallback: Default props applied to every component in the subtree (e.g. { density: "comfortable" }).

examples.title_fallback: Providing component defaults via createOrigam theme

entry.name: IDefaultProviderProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamDefaultsProvider — a structurally transparent wrapper that injects per-component prop defaults into a subtree. Supports disabled pass-through, reset/root scoping and fully scoped isolation.
entry.definition:
export interface IDefaultProviderProps extends ICommonsComponentProps {
    /** Map of defaults keyed by `global` or component name. */
    defaults?: IDefault
    /**
     * When `true`, parent defaults are passed through unchanged — useful to
     * temporarily disable an outer DefaultsProvider without unmounting it.
     */
    disabled?: boolean
    /**
     * When set, parent defaults are NOT inherited; the subtree starts from
     * this provider's defaults only. The value is opaque (string/number) but
     * can be used to track resets in DevTools.
     */
    reset?: string | number
    /**
     * Marks the provider as a root scope. Equivalent to `reset` in behaviour
     * (no parent inheritance) but communicates a different intent (this is
     * the top of a defaults tree, not a mid-tree override).
     */
    root?: string | number
    /**
     * When `true`, parent defaults are not merged in — the subtree only
     * sees this provider's defaults. Same as `reset` but expressed
     * declaratively without needing a discriminator value.
     */
    scoped?: boolean
}

props.name: defaults
props.description_fallback: Map of default props, keyed by "global" or component name (e.g. "origam-btn").

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: When true, parent defaults pass through unchanged — useful to temporarily disable an outer provider.

props.name: reset
props.description_fallback: When set, parent defaults are NOT inherited; the subtree starts only from this provider. The value is opaque but visible in DevTools.

props.name: root
props.description_fallback: Marks this provider as the root of a defaults tree — equivalent to reset in behaviour but communicates a different intent.

props.name: scoped
props.description_fallback: When true, parent defaults are not merged in — the subtree only sees this provider's defaults.

examples.title_fallback: Scoped defaults for a form section
examples.code:
<OrigamDefaultsProvider
    :defaults="{
        global:           { density: 'compact' },
        'origam-btn':     { color: 'primary' },
        'origam-text-field': { variant: 'outlined' },
    }"
>
    <!-- All children inherit these defaults -->
</OrigamDefaultsProvider>

entry.name: IDefaultProviderSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot contract for OrigamDefaultsProvider. The component renders no visible chrome — its only slot is default, which carries the subtree that inherits the injected defaults.

examples.title_fallback: Using the default slot
examples.code:
<OrigamDefaultsProvider :defaults="{ global: { density: 'compact' } }">
    <OrigamBtn>Save</OrigamBtn>
    <OrigamBtn color="danger">Delete</OrigamBtn>
</OrigamDefaultsProvider>

entry.name: IDelayProps
entry.description_fallback: Open/close delay contract for hover-activated components (Tooltip, Menu, …). Values are in milliseconds; strings are coerced to numbers.

props.name: closeDelay
props.description_fallback: Time in milliseconds to wait before closing the component after the trigger condition is no longer met.

props.name: openDelay
props.description_fallback: Time in milliseconds to wait before opening the component once the trigger condition is met.

examples.title_fallback: Using delay props on a Tooltip
examples.code:
<origam-tooltip :open-delay="300" :close-delay="100">
    <template #activator="{ props }">
        <origam-btn v-bind="props">Hover me</origam-btn>
    </template>
    Tooltip content
</origam-tooltip>

entry.name: IDensityProps
entry.description_fallback: Density contract — controls vertical compactness of components that support multiple sizes (default, comfortable, compact). Consumed by useDensity().

props.name: density
props.description_fallback: Vertical size variant — "default" (full), "comfortable" (reduced), or "compact" (minimal). Consumed via useDensity() to emit the matching CSS class.

examples.title_fallback: Extending IDensityProps and consuming useDensity

entry.name: IDialogConfirmationEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit contract for the confirmation dialog — fires validate when the user confirms and cancel when the user dismisses.

examples.title_fallback: Listening to confirmation events
examples.code:
<OrigamDialogConfirmation
    @validate="onConfirm"
    @cancel="onDismiss"
/>

entry.name: IDialogConfirmationProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamDialogConfirmation — a pre-wired dialog that shows Confirm and (optionally) Cancel buttons. Extends IDialogProps with a single cancellable flag that shows or hides the Cancel action.

props.name: cancellable
props.description_fallback: When true (the default), a Cancel button is rendered alongside Confirm. Set to false to produce a non-dismissible acknowledgement dialog.

examples.title_fallback: Non-cancellable acknowledgement dialog
examples.code:
<OrigamDialogConfirmation
    v-model="open"
    title="Terms updated"
    :cancellable="false"
    @validate="acceptTerms"
>
    Please review and accept the new terms of service.
</OrigamDialogConfirmation>

entry.name: IDialogEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emits contract for OrigamDialog — extends the standard component emits and outside-click bubbling, and adds the isRead lifecycle hook that fires the first time the dialog body becomes fully visible (consent / ToS pattern).

examples.title_fallback: Tracking when a ToS dialog is read
examples.code:
<OrigamDialog v-model="open" @is-read="markConsentSeen">
    <template #title>Terms of Service</template>
    <!-- full text -->
</OrigamDialog>

entry.name: IDialogProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamDialog — the full-featured modal dialog. Extends overlay, card and status interfaces and adds fullscreen, focus retention, scroll-lock and size controls.

props.name: fullscreen
props.description_fallback: Expand the dialog to cover the entire viewport.

props.name: retainFocus
props.description_fallback: When true, focus is trapped inside the dialog and cannot escape to the document while it is open.

props.name: scrollable
props.description_fallback: Allow the dialog body to scroll independently of the viewport when the content overflows.

props.name: size
props.description_fallback: Predefined width preset for the dialog surface (xs | sm | md | lg | xl).

examples.title_fallback: Scrollable medium-sized dialog
examples.code:
<OrigamDialog v-model="open" size="md" :scrollable="true" :retain-focus="true">
    <template #title>My Dialog</template>
    <!-- long content -->
</OrigamDialog>

entry.name: IDimensionProps
entry.description_fallback: Sizing contract — height/width plus their min/max bounds, consumed via useDimension().dimensionStyles.
entry.definition:
export interface IDimensionProps {
    height?: number | string
    maxHeight?: number | string
    maxWidth?: number | string
    minHeight?: number | string
    minWidth?: number | string
    width?: number | string
}

props.name: height
props.description_fallback: Block size — a number (resolved to "Npx"), a CSS length, a custom-property ref or an aspect-ratio shortcut.

props.name: maxHeight
props.description_fallback: Maximum block size, same accepted shapes as height.

props.name: maxWidth
props.description_fallback: Maximum inline size, same accepted shapes as width.

props.name: minHeight
props.description_fallback: Minimum block size, same accepted shapes as height.

props.name: minWidth
props.description_fallback: Minimum inline size, same accepted shapes as width.

props.name: width
props.description_fallback: Inline size — a number (resolved to "Npx"), a CSS length, a custom-property ref or an aspect-ratio shortcut.

examples.title_fallback: Extending the interface and consuming the styles

entry.name: IDirectionProps
entry.description_fallback: Direction contract — exposes a single `direction` prop (horizontal / vertical) consumed by layout-aware components such as Tabs, Divider, InfiniteScroll and SlideGroup.

props.name: direction
props.description_fallback: Layout axis — typically "horizontal" or "vertical". Defaults to the component's natural axis when omitted.

examples.title_fallback: Extending the interface on a layout component

entry.name: IDisplayInstance
entry.description_fallback: Reactive display service — the object returned by useDisplay(). Exposes reactive breakpoint flags, current viewport dimensions, platform detection and an update() method for manual refresh.
entry.definition:
export interface IDisplayInstance {
    xs: Ref<boolean>
    sm: Ref<boolean>
    md: Ref<boolean>
    lg: Ref<boolean>
    xl: Ref<boolean>
    xxl: Ref<boolean>
    smAndUp: Ref<boolean>
    mdAndUp: Ref<boolean>
    lgAndUp: Ref<boolean>
    xlAndUp: Ref<boolean>
    smAndDown: Ref<boolean>
    mdAndDown: Ref<boolean>
    lgAndDown: Ref<boolean>
    xlAndDown: Ref<boolean>
    name: Ref<TBreakpoint>
    height: Ref<number>
    width: Ref<number>
    mobile: Ref<boolean>
    mobileBreakpoint: Ref<number | TBreakpoint>
    platform: Ref<IDisplayPlatform>
    thresholds: Ref<TDisplayThresholds>

    /** @internal */
    ssr: boolean

    update (): void
}

props.name: xs
props.description_fallback: True when the viewport is in the xs range.

props.name: sm
props.description_fallback: True when the viewport is in the sm range.

props.name: md
props.description_fallback: True when the viewport is in the md range.

props.name: lg
props.description_fallback: True when the viewport is in the lg range.

props.name: xl
props.description_fallback: True when the viewport is in the xl range.

props.name: xxl
props.description_fallback: True when the viewport is in the xxl range.

props.name: smAndUp
props.description_fallback: True when viewport width >= sm threshold.

props.name: mdAndUp
props.description_fallback: True when viewport width >= md threshold.

props.name: lgAndUp
props.description_fallback: True when viewport width >= lg threshold.

props.name: xlAndUp
props.description_fallback: True when viewport width >= xl threshold.

props.name: smAndDown
props.description_fallback: True when viewport width < md threshold.

props.name: mdAndDown
props.description_fallback: True when viewport width < lg threshold.

props.name: lgAndDown
props.description_fallback: True when viewport width < xl threshold.

props.name: xlAndDown
props.description_fallback: True when viewport width < xxl threshold.

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: Current active breakpoint name (xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl).

props.name: height
props.description_fallback: Reactive viewport height in pixels.

props.name: width
props.description_fallback: Reactive viewport width in pixels.

props.name: mobile
props.description_fallback: True when the current breakpoint is at or below the configured mobileBreakpoint.

props.name: mobileBreakpoint
props.description_fallback: Active mobile breakpoint value (reactive).

props.name: platform
props.description_fallback: Reactive platform detection snapshot.

props.name: thresholds
props.description_fallback: Reactive named breakpoint thresholds in pixels.

props.name: ssr
props.description_fallback: Static flag indicating server-side rendering context (non-reactive).

examples.title_fallback: Consuming the display instance in a component

entry.name: IDisplayOptions
entry.description_fallback: Configuration bag for the useDisplay() plugin — sets the global mobile breakpoint and the named breakpoint thresholds at install time.

props.name: mobileBreakpoint
props.description_fallback: Global breakpoint below which `mobile` is true. Defaults to "sm".

props.name: thresholds
props.description_fallback: Override the pixel widths for each named breakpoint (xs, sm, md, lg, xl, xxl).

examples.title_fallback: Passing custom thresholds to the plugin

entry.name: IDisplayPlatform
entry.description_fallback: Platform detection snapshot — a set of boolean flags that identify the current runtime environment (OS, browser, form factor). Exposed as `platform` on the IDisplayInstance reactive object.
entry.definition:
export interface IDisplayPlatform {
    android: boolean
    ios: boolean
    cordova: boolean
    electron: boolean
    chrome: boolean
    edge: boolean
    firefox: boolean
    opera: boolean
    win: boolean
    mac: boolean
    linux: boolean
    touch: boolean
    ssr: boolean
}

props.name: android
props.description_fallback: True when the UA suggests an Android device.

props.name: ios
props.description_fallback: True when the UA suggests an iOS device.

props.name: cordova
props.description_fallback: True when running inside a Cordova/Capacitor shell.

props.name: electron
props.description_fallback: True when running inside Electron.

props.name: chrome
props.description_fallback: True when the UA is Chromium-based.

props.name: edge
props.description_fallback: True when the UA is Microsoft Edge.

props.name: firefox
props.description_fallback: True when the UA is Firefox.

props.name: opera
props.description_fallback: True when the UA is Opera.

props.name: win
props.description_fallback: True when the OS is Windows.

props.name: mac
props.description_fallback: True when the OS is macOS.

props.name: linux
props.description_fallback: True when the OS is Linux (non-Android).

props.name: touch
props.description_fallback: True when a primary touch input is detected.

props.name: ssr
props.description_fallback: True when the code runs in a server-side rendering context.

examples.title_fallback: Reading platform flags from useDisplay()

entry.name: IDisplayProps
entry.description_fallback: Display contract — lets a component declare its own mobile breakpoint, overriding the global one injected by useDisplay().

props.name: mobileBreakpoint
props.description_fallback: Breakpoint below which the component is considered "mobile". Accepts a pixel value or a named breakpoint token (xs, sm, md, lg, xl, xxl).
