entry.name: IDividerProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamDivider — a thin horizontal or vertical separator. Supports inset, custom length and thickness on top of color, background-color, margin and direction.

props.name: inset
props.description_fallback: Add left/right padding so the divider does not span the full container width (horizontal) or top/bottom padding (vertical).

props.name: length
props.description_fallback: Override the divider length. Accepts a pixel number or any valid CSS length string (e.g. "80%", "4rem").

props.name: thickness
props.description_fallback: Override the divider thickness. Accepts a pixel number or any valid CSS length string (e.g. "2px").

examples.title_fallback: Inset colored divider
examples.code: <OrigamDivider color="primary" :inset="true" thickness="2" />

entry.name: IDrawerEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit contract for <OrigamDrawer> — fires update:rail when the rail collapsed state changes.

examples.title_fallback: Listening to the rail toggle
examples.code: <OrigamDrawer v-model:rail="isRail" @update:rail="onRailChange" />

entry.name: IDrawerProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamDrawer — the side navigation panel. Covers permanent, temporary, rail and expand-on-hover modes, layout reservation (push), viewport positioning (clipped), resize/route watchers and all visual surface props.
entry.definition:
export interface IDrawerProps extends ITagProps, ICommonsComponentProps, IBorderProps, IElevationProps, ILayoutItemProps, IRoundedProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IDensityProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, ITransitionComponentProps, IScrimProps, IActiveProps, IHoverProps {
    disableResizeWatcher?: boolean
    disableRouteWatcher?: boolean
    expandOnHover?: boolean
    floating?: boolean
    modelValue?: boolean
    permanent?: boolean | null
    rail?: boolean | null
    railWidth?: number | string
    temporary?: boolean
    touchless?: boolean
    width?: number | string
    sticky?: boolean
    /**
     * Whether the drawer reserves space in the layout grid (PUSHES the
     * adjacent toolbar / main / footer / etc.) or overlays them.
     *
     *   • `true`  → drawer registers with the layout's reserved-space
     *               system. Toolbar / main / footer adjacent to the
     *               drawer offset themselves accordingly.
     *   • `false` → drawer paints over the surrounding content (no
     *               reservation, no offset).
     *   • `null` (default) → derived from `permanent`: permanent
     *               drawers push by default, temporary drawers don't.
     *
     * Declared as `boolean | null` (NOT `boolean | undefined`) because
     * Vue's prop coercion turns absent boolean attributes into `false`,
     * which would silently disable the push heuristic. `null` survives
     * coercion and lets the runtime fall back to the permanent-derived
     * default.
     */
    push?: boolean | null
    /**
     * Whether the drawer slots BELOW any top-anchored layout item
     * (typically the AppBar) instead of extending the full height of
     * the layout.
     *
     *   • `true`  → drawer's effective layout order is bumped after
     *               top items, so the drawer's top edge starts where
     *               the AppBar's bottom edge ends.
     *   • `false` → drawer keeps order 0, extending full height and
     *               pushing the AppBar to its right (or claiming the
     *               full left lane regardless of top items).
     *   • `null` (default) → resolved from the HTML declaration order.
     *               AppBar before Drawer → drawer below.
     *               Drawer before AppBar → drawer full-height.
     */
    clipped?: boolean | null
}

props.name: disableResizeWatcher
props.description_fallback: Disable the internal ResizeObserver that auto-closes a temporary drawer on viewport shrink.

props.name: disableRouteWatcher
props.description_fallback: Disable automatic close on route change (useful when the drawer should persist across navigation).

props.name: expandOnHover
props.description_fallback: Expand a rail drawer to full width when the pointer enters it.

props.name: floating
props.description_fallback: Remove the border/shadow that separates the drawer from the content area for a seamless look.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: v-model open/closed state. Ignored for permanent drawers.

props.name: permanent
props.description_fallback: Always visible; ignores modelValue and overlay behaviour.

props.name: rail
props.description_fallback: Collapse the drawer to icon-only width.

props.name: railWidth
props.description_fallback: Override the icon-only collapsed width when rail is true.

props.name: temporary
props.description_fallback: Show a scrim behind the drawer and close on outside click or Escape.

props.name: touchless
props.description_fallback: Disable swipe-to-open/close gesture handling.

props.name: width
props.description_fallback: Override the default expanded drawer width.

props.name: sticky
props.description_fallback: Keep the drawer visible while the user scrolls (position: sticky semantics).

props.name: push
props.description_fallback: When true, adjacent layout items offset themselves to make room. null (default) derives from permanent.

props.name: clipped
props.description_fallback: When true, the drawer slots below top-anchored layout items (e.g. AppBar). null derives from HTML declaration order.

examples.title_fallback: Rail drawer that expands on hover
examples.code:
<OrigamDrawer :rail="true" :expand-on-hover="true" color="surface">
    <!-- nav items -->
</OrigamDrawer>

entry.name: IElevationProps
entry.description_fallback: Elevation contract — adds a shadow rung to a component via the design-token-mapped `elevation` prop, consumed by useElevation() which emits a `box-shadow: var(--origam-shadow-…)` utility class or inline style.

props.name: elevation
props.description_fallback: Shadow level — a numeric rung (0–24) mapped to the --origam-shadow-{n} token, or a named alias such as "sm" / "md" / "lg". Omitting it leaves no shadow.

examples.title_fallback: Extending IElevationProps and consuming it

entry.name: IEmptyStateProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamEmptyState — the placeholder shown when a list, table or collection has nothing to display. Five built-in visual presets (no-data, no-results, error, offline, locked) bundle icon and intent; both can be overridden.
entry.definition:
export interface IEmptyStateProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, ITypographyProps { /** * Visual preset. Bundles a default icon and intent — see * `EMPTY_STATE_PRESET_CONFIG`. The preset is the starting point; * `icon` and `iconColor` win when both are passed. * * @default 'no-data' */ preset?: TEmptyStatePreset /** * Primary heading. Rendered as a `

`-equivalent element with * the chosen `size`. Override entirely via the `#title` slot when * richer markup is needed (a link, an icon-prefix, …). */ title?: string /** * Optional sub-heading rendered below the title. Multiple lines * supported; the SCSS layer clamps the readable width via the * `--origam-empty-state---max-width` token. */ description?: string /** * Override the preset icon. Accepts the same `TIcon` shape as * ``: a MDI / iconify string, an array of SVG path * tuples, or a Vue `Component`. Use the `#icon` slot for full * SVG / `` illustrations. */ icon?: TIcon /** * Override the preset's icon color (the semantic intent that maps * to `--origam-color__action--{intent}---bg` / * `--origam-color__feedback--{intent}---bg`). */ iconColor?: TIntent /** * Vertical density. Drives icon size, title / description font * sizes, padding and gap. * * @default 'md' */ size?: TEmptyStateSize /** * Horizontal alignment of the icon / title / description / actions * stack. * * @default 'center' */ align?: TEmptyStateAlign }

props.name: preset
props.description_fallback: Visual preset — bundles a default icon and intent. One of: no-data, no-results, error, offline, locked.

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Primary heading. Override entirely via the #title slot for richer markup.

props.name: description
props.description_fallback: Optional sub-heading rendered below the title. Multiple lines supported.

props.name: icon
props.description_fallback: Override the preset icon. Accepts an MDI/iconify string, SVG path tuples or a Vue Component.

props.name: iconColor
props.description_fallback: Override the preset icon color intent (primary, success, danger, warning, info …).

props.name: size
props.description_fallback: Vertical density — drives icon size, font sizes, padding and gap.

props.name: align
props.description_fallback: Horizontal alignment of the icon / title / description / actions stack.

examples.title_fallback: Empty search results placeholder
examples.code:
<OrigamEmptyState
    preset="no-results"
    title="No results found"
    description="Try adjusting your search or filters."
    align="center"
/>

entry.name: IEmptyStateSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot contract for OrigamEmptyState. All slots are optional. The default slot replaces the entire built-in layout; targeted slots (icon, title, description, actions) swap individual pieces.
entry.definition: export interface IEmptyStateSlots { /** * Replaces the entire built-in layout (icon + title + description * + actions). Use this for a fully bespoke layout — e.g. a hero * empty page with a custom illustration and a multi-column CTA * grid. */ default?: () => any /** * Replaces the default `` rendering. Use for SVG / * `` illustrations. Mark the surface `aria-hidden="true"` * when it is purely decorative. */ icon?: () => any /** * Replaces the rendered title element. Slot wins over the `title` * prop when both are provided. */ title?: () => any /** * Replaces the rendered description element. Slot wins over the * `description` prop when both are provided. */ description?: () => any /** * Actions row rendered below the description (or the title when * `description` is absent). Typically holds 1–2 buttons (Create, * Retry, Switch to offline mode, Sign in). */ actions?: () => any }

props.name: default
props.description_fallback: Replaces the entire built-in layout (icon + title + description + actions) for a fully bespoke empty page.

props.name: icon
props.description_fallback: Replaces the default OrigamIcon. Use for SVG or illustrations. Mark aria-hidden="true" for decorative content.

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Replaces the rendered title element. Wins over the title prop when both are provided.

props.name: description
props.description_fallback: Replaces the rendered description element. Wins over the description prop when both are provided.

props.name: actions
props.description_fallback: Actions row rendered below the description. Typically holds 1–2 buttons (Create, Retry, Sign in …).

examples.title_fallback: Custom illustration with a retry action

entry.name: IEvent
entry.description_fallback: Public event data shape consumed by OrigamCalendar and useCalendar. Supports single-day, multi-day, all-day and recurring events (RRULE subset: FREQ/INTERVAL/COUNT/UNTIL/BYDAY). start/end accept ISO-8601 strings, Date objects or epoch numbers.
entry.definition:
export interface IEvent {
    /** Stable identifier — used as `:key` and for click-target wiring. */
    id: string | number
    /** Displayed title — also read by the default `aria-label`. */
    title: string
    /** Optional long-form description — surfaced in the agenda view. */
    description?: string
    /** Start instant. ISO-8601 strings and `Date` instances both work. */
    start: string | Date
    /**
     * End instant. Omitted when `allDay=true` (the calendar treats the
     * event as a single-day block) or for "point-in-time" events with
     * no measurable duration.
     */
    end?: string | Date
    /**
     * `true` to render the event in the all-day band above the time
     * grid (week / day views). Has no effect in month view.
     */
    allDay?: boolean
    /**
     * Free-form category string — used as the lookup key for
     * `categoryColors`. Useful when multiple events share a colour
     * (e.g. "meeting", "focus", "personal").
     */
    category?: string
    /**
     * Direct colour override. Wins over `category` when both are set.
     * Accepts a `TIntent` (matches the design-token rail) or any CSS
     * colour string.
     */
    color?: TIntent | string
    /**
     * RRULE subset string. The calendar expands the recurrence to
     * the visible range at render time; the original event is **not**
     * mutated.
     */
    rrule?: string
    /** Free-form metadata — passed through to the `event-click` payload. */
    metadata?: Record<string, unknown>
}

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Stable identifier used as :key and for click-target wiring.

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Displayed event title — also used as the default aria-label.

props.name: description
props.description_fallback: Optional long-form description surfaced in the agenda view.

props.name: start
props.description_fallback: Start instant. Accepts ISO-8601 strings, Date objects or epoch numbers.

props.name: end
props.description_fallback: End instant. Omit for all-day events or point-in-time events.

props.name: allDay
props.description_fallback: Render in the all-day band above the time grid (week/day views). No effect in month view.

props.name: category
props.description_fallback: Free-form category string — lookup key for categoryColors on the calendar.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Direct color override. Wins over category. Accepts a TIntent value or any CSS color string.

props.name: rrule
props.description_fallback: RRULE subset string (FREQ=DAILY|WEEKLY|MONTHLY + INTERVAL/COUNT/UNTIL/BYDAY). The calendar expands recurrences at render time.

props.name: metadata
props.description_fallback: Free-form payload passed through to the event-click handler.

examples.title_fallback: Weekly recurring meeting event

entry.name: IFieldDefaultSlotProps
entry.description_fallback: Shape of the slot props injected into the default slot of OrigamField — exposes the id, aria-describedby, active/focused state, ref and focus handlers so inner input elements can wire up correctly.
entry.definition:
export interface IFieldDefaultSlotProps {
    id: string
    'aria-describedby': string
    isActive: boolean
    isFocused: boolean | undefined
    ref: HTMLElement | undefined
    onBlur: () => void
    onFocus: () => void
}

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Unique DOM id generated by OrigamField for the inner input.

props.name: isActive
props.description_fallback: True when the field is in an active (non-empty / focused) state.

props.name: isFocused
props.description_fallback: True when the inner input is focused.

props.name: ref
props.description_fallback: Template ref to the underlying input element managed by OrigamField.

props.name: onBlur
props.description_fallback: Blur handler to be bound on the inner native input.

props.name: onFocus
props.description_fallback: Focus handler to be bound on the inner native input.

examples.title_fallback: Wiring a native input inside OrigamField
examples.code:
<OrigamField>
    <template #default="{ id, isActive, onFocus, onBlur, ref }">
        <input :id="id" :ref="ref" @focus="onFocus" @blur="onBlur" />
    </template>
</OrigamField>

entry.name: IFieldProps
entry.description_fallback: Full prop surface for OrigamField — controls label, prefix/suffix, error state, disabled/dirty/required flags, and inherits color, density, variant, rounded, elevation, size, loader, focus and adjacent-inner surfaces.
entry.definition:
export interface IFieldProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ILoaderProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IAdjacentInnerProps, IFocusProps, IDensityProps, ILabelProps, IActiveProps, IVariantProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, ISizeProps, ITypographyProps {
    centerAffix?: boolean
    dirty?: boolean
    disabled?: boolean
    /**
     * Error state for the field.
     *   - `boolean` — paints the `--error` modifier (rules-driven flow).
     *   - `string`  — paints the modifier AND becomes the inline error
     *     message rendered by consumers that opt-in (e.g. FileField's
     *     dropzone). For consumers that don't read the string, the
     *     truthy semantics still apply.
     *   - `false` / omitted — no error.
     */
    error?: string | boolean
    flat?: boolean
    inline?: boolean
    label?: string
    prefix?: string
    suffix?: string
    persistentClear?: boolean
    reverse?: boolean
    singleLine?: boolean
    required?: boolean
    /** @deprecated Use the `active` object prop instead. Kept for back-compat. */
    activeColor?: TColor
    /** @deprecated Use the `active` object prop instead. Kept for back-compat. */
    activeBgColor?: TColor
}

props.name: centerAffix
props.description_fallback: Vertically center the prepend/append icons relative to the field height.

props.name: dirty
props.description_fallback: Mark the field as dirty (user has interacted with it).

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disable the field, preventing user interaction.

props.name: error
props.description_fallback: Error state: boolean paints the --error modifier; a string also becomes the inline error message.

props.name: flat
props.description_fallback: Remove the default shadow/elevation from the field.

props.name: inline
props.description_fallback: Render the field inline instead of as a block.

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Floating label text.

props.name: prefix
props.description_fallback: Static text prepended inside the field.

props.name: suffix
props.description_fallback: Static text appended inside the field.

props.name: persistentClear
props.description_fallback: Always show the clear button, even when the field is empty.

props.name: reverse
props.description_fallback: Reverse the layout direction of the field.

props.name: singleLine
props.description_fallback: Hide the label when the field is focused or filled.

props.name: required
props.description_fallback: Mark the field as required, adding aria-required and a visual indicator.

examples.title_fallback: Field with error state and label
examples.code:
<OrigamField label="Email" :error="hasError" required>
    <template #default="{ id, onFocus, onBlur }">
        <input :id="id" type="email" @focus="onFocus" @blur="onBlur" />
    </template>
</OrigamField>

entry.name: IFileFieldEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit contract for OrigamFileField — extends IFieldEmits and IInputEmits with file-specific events: control clicks, remove, download, drop and max-size error.
entry.definition:
export interface IFileFieldEmits extends IFieldEmits, IInputEmits {
    (e: 'click:control', value: MouseEvent): void
    (e: 'mousedown:control', value: MouseEvent): void
    (e: 'click:remove', value: { file: File, index: number }): void
    (e: 'click:download', value: { file: File, index: number }): void
    (e: 'drop', value: { files: Array<File>, event: DragEvent }): void
    (e: 'error:max-size', value: { files: Array<File>, maxFileSize: number, message: Array<string> }): void
}

examples.title_fallback: Handling drop and max-size error

<OrigamFileField

/>

entry.name: IFileFieldProps
entry.description_fallback: Full prop surface for OrigamFileField — controls multiple selection, display mode (list/chips/counter), dropzone mode, upload progress, size display, custom icons and inherits the full IFieldProps and IInputProps surfaces.
entry.definition:
export interface IFileFieldProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IDensityProps, IFieldProps, IInputProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IBorderProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps {
    chips?: boolean
    counter?: boolean
    counterSizeString?: string
    counterString?: string
    placeholder?: string
    persistentPlaceholder?: boolean
    persistentCounter?: boolean
    multiple?: boolean
    showSize?: TFileSize
    modelValue?: TFile
    chipProps?: IChipProps
    divider?: string
    iconColor?: string
    maxFileSize?: number
    dragndrop?: boolean
    dragndropIcon?: string
    fileIcon?: string
    removeIcon?: string
    downloadIcon?: string
    downloadable?: boolean
    progress?: Array<number>
    dropzoneTitle?: string
    dropzoneSubtitle?: string
    browseText?: string
    maxFileSizeErrorString?: string
    /**
     * How a multi-file selection is rendered:
     *   - `'list'`     (default) — vertical card stack under the field.
     *   - `'chips'`    — each file as a closable `<OrigamChip>` inline.
     *   - `'counter'`  — single text "{n} files" + an `<OrigamCounter>`.
     *
     * Single-file selection ignores this prop and always shows the file
     * name with a paperclip prepend.
     */
    display?: TFileFieldDisplay
    /**
     * Render the field as a large outlined drop-zone instead of an inline
     * input. Equivalent to (and aliased by) the legacy `dragndrop` prop —
     * keep both for backward-compat. When `true`, the wrapper accepts
     * native drag-and-drop events, paints a `--dragging` modifier on
     * dragover, and falls back to a click-to-browse interaction.
     */
    dropzone?: boolean
}

props.name: chips
props.description_fallback: Render selected files as chips (shorthand for display="chips").

props.name: counter
props.description_fallback: Show a file count indicator.

props.name: iconColor

props.name: maxFileSize

props.name: fileIcon

props.name: downloadIcon

props.name: downloadable

props.name: progress

props.name: dropzoneTitle

props.name: dropzoneSubtitle

props.name: browseText

props.name: maxFileSizeErrorString

entry.name: IFileFieldSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot signatures for OrigamFileField — extends IFieldSlots and IInputSlots with file-specific slots: counter, field, dropzone, item, chip and selection.
entry.definition:
export interface IFileFieldSlots extends IFieldSlots, Omit<IInputSlots, 'default'> {
    counter?: (data: { counter: string, value: string | number, max?: string | number }) => any
    field?: (data: { id: string, isDisabled: boolean, isDirty: boolean, isValid: boolean | undefined, isReadonly: boolean }) => any
    dropzone?: (data: { isDragging: boolean, browse: () => void }) => any
    item?: (data: { file: File, index: number, progress: number, remove: () => void, download: () => void }) => any
    chip?: (data: { fileNames: string, totalBytes: number, totalBytesReadable: string, props: Record<string, any> }) => any
    selection?: () => any
}

props.name: counter
props.type_label: (data: { counter: string, value: string | number, max?: string | number }) => any
props.description_fallback: Custom counter display.

props.name: field
props.type_label: (data: { id: string, isDisabled: boolean, isDirty: boolean, isValid: boolean | undefined, isReadonly: boolean }) => any
props.description_fallback: Replace the inner field content.

props.name: dropzone
props.description_fallback: Custom dropzone content — isDragging reflects drag-over state, browse() opens the file picker.

props.name: item
props.type_label: (data: { file: File, index: number, progress: number, remove: () => void, download: () => void }) => any

props.name: chip

props.description_fallback: Custom chip renderer when display="chips".

entry.name: IFilterPropsOptions
entry.description_fallback: Generic utility interface — binds a props options descriptor to its extracted Props type and provides a filterProps() helper that strips props not belonging to a given sub-component. Used internally by useProps().

props.name: props
props.description_fallback: The raw Vue props options descriptor (record of PropType / validator objects).

examples.title_fallback: Using useProps() which returns IFilterPropsOptions

entry.name: IFiltersProps
entry.description_fallback: Filter contract for list / autocomplete components — declares the full filtering surface: custom function, per-key function, targeted keys, filter mode and an escape hatch to bypass filtering entirely.
entry.definition:
export interface IFiltersProps {
    customFilter?: TFilterFunction
    customKeyFilter?: TFilterKeyFunctions
    filterKeys?: TFilterKeys
    filterMode?: TFilterMode
    noFilter?: boolean,
}

props.name: customFilter
props.description_fallback: Global custom filter function applied to each item. Overrides the built-in matching logic when provided.

props.name: customKeyFilter
props.description_fallback: Per-key custom filter functions — a map from item property name to a filter function, allowing different matching strategies per field.

props.name: filterKeys
props.description_fallback: Which item properties to match against — a single key name, an array of key names, or a structured descriptor.

props.name: filterMode
props.description_fallback: How multiple filterKeys are combined: "every" (AND) | "some" (OR) | "union" | "intersection".

props.name: noFilter
props.description_fallback: When true, filtering is skipped entirely and all items are returned as-is.

examples.title_fallback: Extending IFiltersProps on a filterable list

entry.name: IFocusEmits

examples.title_fallback: Implementing IFocusEmits in a component

entry.name: IFocusProps
entry.description_fallback: Focus contract — a single `focused` prop that lets the parent control or observe the focus state of a component that manages keyboard focus internally.

props.name: focused
props.description_fallback: When true, the component renders in its focused visual state. Typically bound as v-model:focused for two-way sync.

examples.title_fallback: Two-way binding with v-model:focused

entry.name: IFormEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit contract for OrigamForm — fires submit when the form is submitted (after validation) and reset when it is reset.

entry.name: IFormField
entry.description_fallback: Internal registration record for a field inside an OrigamForm — carries the field id, validation/reset methods, the component instance, and the current valid/error state.
entry.definition:
export interface IFormField {
    id: number | string
    validate: () => Promise<Array<string>>
    reset: () => Promise<void>
    resetValidation: () => Promise<void>
    vm: Raw<ComponentInternalInstance>
    isValid?: boolean | null
    errorMessages: Array<string>
}

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Unique identifier of the field within the form.

props.name: validate
props.description_fallback: Run validation rules and return an array of error messages.

props.name: reset
props.description_fallback: Reset the field value and clear validation state.

props.name: resetValidation
props.description_fallback: Clear validation errors without resetting the value.

props.name: vm
props.description_fallback: Raw reference to the field component instance.

props.name: isValid
props.description_fallback: Current validity state: true, false, or null when untouched.

props.name: errorMessages
props.description_fallback: Current list of error messages for this field.

examples.title_fallback: Accessing registered fields via IFormProvide

entry.name: IFormProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamForm — controls disabled/readonly state, validation timing (validateOn), fast-fail behaviour, error messages, hints, scroll-to-error, and the overall validity v-model.
entry.definition:
export interface IFormProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITypographyProps {
    disabled?: boolean
    fastFail?: boolean
    readonly?: boolean
    modelValue?: boolean | null
    validateOn?: TValidateOn
    rules?: Array<any>
    errorMessages?: Array<string> | string
    hint?: string
    messages?: Array<string> | string
    scrollToError?: boolean | ScrollIntoViewOptions
}

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disable all fields inside the form.

props.name: fastFail
props.description_fallback: Stop validation after the first field failure.

props.name: readonly
props.description_fallback: Render all fields in read-only mode.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: v-model for the overall form validity. null = untouched.

props.name: validateOn
props.description_fallback: When to trigger validation: blur, input, submit, or combinations.

props.name: rules
props.description_fallback: Form-level validation rules applied on submit.

props.name: errorMessages
props.description_fallback: Form-level error messages displayed under the form.

props.name: hint
props.description_fallback: Helper text displayed below the form.

props.name: messages
props.description_fallback: Informational messages displayed below the form.

props.name: scrollToError
props.description_fallback: Scroll to the first invalid field after a failed submission.

examples.title_fallback: Form with blur-triggered validation and scroll-to-error
examples.code:
<OrigamForm
    v-model="isFormValid"
    validate-on="blur"
    :scroll-to-error="{ behavior: 'smooth' }"
>
    <!-- fields -->
</OrigamForm>

entry.name: IFormProvide
entry.description_fallback: Provide/inject contract exposed by OrigamForm to its descendant fields — lets fields register/unregister themselves and report validation state back to the parent form.
entry.definition:
export interface IFormProvide {
    register: (item: {
        id: number | string
        vm: ComponentInternalInstance
        validate: () => Promise<Array<string>>
        reset: () => Promise<void>
        resetValidation: () => Promise<void>
    }) => void
    unregister: (id: number | string) => void
    update: (id: number | string, isValid: boolean | undefined, errorMessages: Array<string>) => void
    items?: Ref<Array<IFormField>>
    isDisabled: ComputedRef<boolean>
    isReadonly: ComputedRef<boolean>
    isValidating?: Ref<boolean>
    isValid: Ref<boolean | null | undefined>
    validateOn: Ref<TValidateOn | undefined>
}

props.name: register
props.type_label:
(item: {
        id: number | string
        vm: ComponentInternalInstance
        validate: () => Promise<Array<string>>
        reset: () => Promise<void>
        resetValidation: () => Promise<void>
    }) => void
props.description_fallback: Called by a field on mount to register itself with the parent form.

props.name: unregister
props.description_fallback: Called by a field on unmount to deregister itself.

props.name: update
props.type_label: (id: number | string, isValid: boolean | undefined, errorMessages: Array<string>) => void
props.description_fallback: Called by a field after validation to report its current valid state and error messages.

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Reactive list of all registered fields.

props.name: isDisabled
props.description_fallback: Whether the form is disabled.

props.name: isReadonly
props.description_fallback: Whether the form is readonly.

props.name: isValidating
props.description_fallback: True while form validation is running.

props.name: isValid
props.description_fallback: Overall form validity.

props.name: validateOn
props.description_fallback: When validation should be triggered.

examples.title_fallback: Injecting IFormProvide inside a custom field
examples.code:
import { inject } from 'vue'
import type { IFormProvide } from 'origam'

const form = inject<IFormProvide>('FORM_PROVIDE_KEY')
form?.register({ id: myId, vm: getCurrentInstance()!, validate, reset, resetValidation })

props.name: errors

<OrigamForm @submit="async (event) => {

entry.name: IGoToInstance
entry.description_fallback: Internal service instance for useGoTo() — carries the reactive RTL flag and the resolved options used for smooth-scroll calculations.

props.name: rtl
props.description_fallback: Reactive right-to-left flag — when true, scroll direction is inverted for RTL layouts.

props.name: options
props.description_fallback: Resolved configuration object for the scroll animation (container, duration, offset, easing, patterns).

examples.title_fallback: Shape of the internal instance injected by createGoTo()

entry.name: IGoToOptions
entry.description_fallback: Configuration object for the useGoTo() smooth-scroll service — controls container, duration, offset, easing and named easing patterns.
entry.definition:
export interface IGoToOptions {
    container: ComponentPublicInstance | HTMLElement | string
    duration: number
    layout: boolean
    offset: number
    easing: string | ((t: number) => number)
    patterns: IGoToOptionsPatterns
}

props.name: container
props.description_fallback: Scrollable container — a Vue component instance, a DOM element, or a CSS selector string. Defaults to window.

props.name: duration
props.description_fallback: Scroll animation duration in milliseconds.

props.name: layout
props.description_fallback: When true, the scroll target offset accounts for layout-registered sticky elements (appbar, footer).

props.name: offset
props.description_fallback: Additional pixel offset applied on top of the target element position.

props.name: easing
props.description_fallback: Easing function — a named key from `patterns` or an inline timing function that maps progress t ∈ [0,1] to an output value.

props.name: patterns
props.description_fallback: Named easing function registry — a record of name → timing function used to resolve the `easing` string.

examples.title_fallback: Smooth-scrolling to a target with custom options

entry.name: IGoToOptionsPatterns
entry.description_fallback: Named easing function registry for the useGoTo() service — an open record where each key is an easing name and the value is a timing function mapping progress t ∈ [0,1] to an output value.

examples.title_fallback: Registering a custom easing pattern

entry.name: IGradient
entry.description_fallback: Structured gradient descriptor — one of the three accepted formats for color/bgColor/textColor props. Supports linear, radial and conic types; short form (from/to/via) or full form (stops array).
entry.definition:
export interface IGradient {
    type?: 'linear' | 'radial' | 'conic'
    from?: TIntent | string
    to?: TIntent | string
    via?: TIntent | string
    direction?: number | string
    stops?: IGradientStop[]
}

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: Gradient type. Defaults to "linear".

props.name: from
props.description_fallback: Start color of the short-form 2–3 stop gradient. A TIntent alias or raw CSS color.

props.name: to
props.description_fallback: End color of the short-form gradient.

props.name: via
props.description_fallback: Optional middle color of the short-form gradient, auto-positioned at 50%.

props.name: direction
props.description_fallback: Angle in degrees (default 135) or a CSS keyword such as "to right" / "to bottom left". For radial/conic, used as the shape/position prefix (e.g. "circle at center").

props.name: stops
props.description_fallback: Full-form stop list — overrides from/to/via entirely when provided. Each entry is an IGradientStop with a color and a percentage position.

examples.title_fallback: Short form — from / to
examples.code:
<OrigamBtn :bgColor="{ from: 'primary', to: 'success', direction: 'to right' }">
    Gradient button
</OrigamBtn>

examples.title_fallback: Full form — explicit stops

entry.name: IGradientStop
entry.description_fallback: Single stop in a multi-stop gradient — pairs a color (TIntent alias or raw CSS color) with a percentage position in the [0..100] range.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Stop color — a TIntent name (resolved to its --origam-color__action--{intent}---bg token) or a raw CSS color string (hex, rgb, hsl, named).

props.name: position
props.description_fallback: Position of the stop as a percentage in the [0..100] range.

examples.title_fallback: Building a 3-stop gradient with IGradientStop
examples.code:
import type { IGradient, IGradientStop } from 'origam'

const stops: IGradientStop[] = [
    { color: 'primary', position: 0 },
    { color: '#ff6b6b', position: 50 },
    { color: 'success', position: 100 },
]

const gradient: IGradient = { type: 'linear', direction: 90, stops }

entry.name: IGridItemProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamGridItem — controls CSS Grid placement via column, row and area (object or shorthand string syntax), plus per-item alignSelf and justifySelf overrides.
entry.definition:
export interface IGridItemProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps {
    /**
     * Inline-axis placement (`grid-column`). Two accepted shapes:
     *
     * - object: `{ start: 1, end: 5 }` or `{ start: 1, span: 4 }` —
     *   the component serialises to the right CSS.
     * - string: `'1 / 5'`, `'span 2'`, `'main-start / main-end'` —
     *   passed verbatim.
     */
    column?: IGridLineSpec | string | number
    /**
     * Block-axis placement (`grid-row`). Same accepted shapes as `column`.
     */
    row?: IGridLineSpec | string | number
    /**
     * `grid-area` name. When set, overrides `column` / `row` (CSS rule).
     * Use this in conjunction with the parent grid's `areas` prop.
     */
    area?: string
    /**
     * Per-item `align-self` override. Falls back to the parent grid's
     * `alignItems` when unset.
     */
    alignSelf?: TGridPlaceSelf
    /**
     * Per-item `justify-self` override. Falls back to the parent grid's
     * `justifyItems` when unset.
     */
    justifySelf?: TGridPlaceSelf
}

props.name: column
props.description_fallback: Inline-axis placement (grid-column). Pass an object { start, end } / { start, span } or a CSS string like "1 / 5".

props.name: row
props.description_fallback: Block-axis placement (grid-row). Same accepted shapes as column.

props.name: area
props.description_fallback: grid-area name. When set, overrides column and row.

props.name: alignSelf
props.description_fallback: Per-item align-self override.

props.name: justifySelf
props.description_fallback: Per-item justify-self override.

examples.title_fallback: Placing an item across columns with object syntax
examples.code:
<OrigamGridItem :column="{ start: 1, span: 2 }" :row="{ start: 2, end: 4 }">
    Content
</OrigamGridItem>

entry.name: IGridLineSpec
entry.description_fallback: Object syntax for a CSS grid line specification used by OrigamGridItem — express column/row placement with start, end, or span without writing raw CSS grid syntax.

props.name: start
props.description_fallback: Inclusive starting grid line. Accepts a number or a named line.

props.name: end
props.description_fallback: Exclusive ending grid line. Accepts a number or a named line.

props.name: span
props.description_fallback: Span N tracks from start. Wins over end when both are set.

examples.title_fallback: Spanning two columns from a named line
examples.code: <OrigamGridItem :column="{ start: 'sidebar-start', span: 2 }" />

entry.name: IGridProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for OrigamGrid — a declarative CSS Grid container. Covers track templates (columns/rows), gap, areas, auto-flow, alignment, and all standard visual surfaces (color, border, elevation, dimension).
entry.definition:
export interface IGridProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, IDimensionProps, IMarginProps, IPaddingProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IBgColorProps, IColorProps, IBorderProps {
    /**
     * Track template for the inline axis (`grid-template-columns`).
     *
     * - `number` (e.g. `12`) — sugar for `repeat(N, 1fr)`.
     * - `string` (e.g. `'1fr 2fr 1fr'` or `'repeat(auto-fill, minmax(200px, 1fr))'`)
     *   — passed verbatim.
     * - `string[]` (e.g. `['200px', '1fr', '200px']`) — joined with single space.
     *
     * @default 'auto'
     */
    columns?: TGridTracks
    /**
     * Track template for the block axis (`grid-template-rows`).
     * Same accepted shapes as `columns`.
     *
     * @default 'auto'
     */
    rows?: TGridTracks
    /**
     * Both-axis gap. Accepts:
     *
     * - one of the size tokens `'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl'`
     *   (resolved via `tokens/component/grid.json`).
     * - any CSS length string (`'24px'`, `'1rem'`, `'1rem 2rem'`).
     * - a plain `number` interpreted as pixels.
     *
     * `columnGap` / `rowGap` take precedence on their respective axis.
     *
     * @default 'md'
     */
    gap?: TGridGapSize | string | number
    /**
     * Inline-axis-only gap override. Same accepted shapes as `gap`.
     */
    columnGap?: TGridGapSize | string | number
    /**
     * Block-axis-only gap override. Same accepted shapes as `gap`.
     */
    rowGap?: TGridGapSize | string | number
    /**
     * `grid-template-areas`. Two accepted shapes:
     *
     * - `string[]` — every entry is one row of the template
     *   (`['header header', 'sidebar main']`). Each entry is wrapped in
     *   quotes by the component, so the consumer doesn't have to.
     * - `string` — raw CSS, passed verbatim. Use it when you want to
     *   inline `". header ."` style escape hatches with multiple double-
     *   quoted rows yourself.
     */
    areas?: string | ReadonlyArray<string>
    /**
     * `grid-auto-flow`. Controls how implicit tracks are filled when
     * items don't declare an explicit area.
     *
     * @default 'row'
     */
    autoFlow?: TGridAutoFlow
    /**
     * `grid-auto-columns` — sizes for implicit columns. Free-form CSS.
     */
    autoColumns?: string
    /**
     * `grid-auto-rows` — sizes for implicit rows. Free-form CSS.
     */
    autoRows?: string
    /**
     * `align-items` — block-axis alignment of grid items inside their cells.
     *
     * @default 'stretch'
     */
    alignItems?: TGridPlaceItems
    /**
     * `justify-items` — inline-axis alignment of grid items inside their cells.
     *
     * @default 'stretch'
     */
    justifyItems?: TGridPlaceItems
    /**
     * `align-content` — block-axis alignment of the whole grid inside
     * its container (only relevant when the grid is smaller than its
     * container on that axis).
     */
    alignContent?: TGridPlaceContent
    /**
     * `justify-content` — inline-axis alignment of the whole grid
     * inside its container.
     */
    justifyContent?: TGridPlaceContent
    /**
     * Toggles `display: inline-grid` instead of `display: grid`. Useful
     * for inline-flow layouts (e.g. a label + tag pill where you want
     * the grid to size to its content).
     *
     * @default false
     */
    inline?: boolean
}

props.name: columns
props.description_fallback: Track template for the inline axis (grid-template-columns). Accepts a number (repeat N 1fr), a raw CSS string, or an array of track sizes.

props.name: rows
props.description_fallback: Track template for the block axis (grid-template-rows). Same shapes as columns.

props.name: gap
props.description_fallback: Both-axis gap. Accepts a size token (xs/sm/md/lg/xl), a CSS length string, or a number (px).

props.name: columnGap
props.description_fallback: Inline-axis gap override. Takes precedence over gap on the column axis.

props.name: rowGap
props.description_fallback: Block-axis gap override. Takes precedence over gap on the row axis.

props.name: areas
props.description_fallback: grid-template-areas. Pass a string[] (one row per entry) or a raw CSS string.

props.name: autoFlow
props.description_fallback: grid-auto-flow. Controls how implicit tracks are placed when items have no explicit area.

props.name: autoColumns
props.description_fallback: grid-auto-columns sizes for implicit columns. Free-form CSS.

props.name: autoRows
props.description_fallback: grid-auto-rows sizes for implicit rows. Free-form CSS.

props.name: alignItems
props.description_fallback: align-items — block-axis alignment of grid items inside their cells.

props.name: justifyItems
props.description_fallback: justify-items — inline-axis alignment of grid items inside their cells.

props.name: alignContent
props.description_fallback: align-content — block-axis alignment of the whole grid inside its container.

props.name: justifyContent
props.description_fallback: justify-content — inline-axis alignment of the whole grid inside its container.

props.name: inline
props.description_fallback: Toggles display: inline-grid instead of display: grid.

examples.title_fallback: Responsive 3-column grid with named areas
examples.code:
<OrigamGrid
    :columns="3"
    gap="md"
    :areas="['header header header', 'sidebar main aside']"
>
    <OrigamGridItem area="header">…</OrigamGridItem>
    <OrigamGridItem area="sidebar">…</OrigamGridItem>
    <OrigamGridItem area="main">…</OrigamGridItem>
    <OrigamGridItem area="aside">…</OrigamGridItem>
</OrigamGrid>

entry.name: IGroupEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit signature for components that participate in a selectable group — declares the `group:selected` event used to propagate selection state to the group provider.

examples.title_fallback: Implementing IGroupEmits on a group item

entry.name: IGroupItem
entry.description_fallback: Internal registration record for a single item inside a group — carries the numeric id assigned by the group provider, a reactive value ref and a reactive disabled ref.

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Unique numeric identifier assigned by the group provider at registration time.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Reactive value ref — the logical value this item represents in the group selection model.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Reactive disabled state — when true, the item cannot be selected.

examples.title_fallback: Shape of an item registered in a group provider

entry.name: IGroupItemProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for an individual item inside a selectable group — its value, disabled state and optional selected class override.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: The value represented by this item when selected inside the parent group.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Prevents the item from being selected when true.

props.name: selectedClass
props.description_fallback: CSS class applied to this item when it is the selected one, overriding the group-level selectedClass.

examples.title_fallback: Extending IGroupItemProps on a tab or chip item

entry.name: IGroupItemProvide
entry.description_fallback: Runtime injection provided by each item component to its descendants — exposes identity, selection state, toggle and a back-reference to the parent group.
entry.definition:
export interface IGroupItemProvide {
    id: number
    isSelected: Ref<boolean>
    toggle: () => void
    select: (value: boolean) => void
    selectedClass: Ref<Array<(string | undefined)> | false>
    value: Ref<unknown>
    disabled: Ref<boolean | undefined>
    group: IGroupProvide
}

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Unique numeric id assigned by the parent group on registration.

props.name: isSelected
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag — true when this item is in the group selection.

props.name: toggle
props.description_fallback: Toggles the selected state of this item inside the group.

props.name: select
props.description_fallback: Explicitly sets the selected state to the given boolean.

props.name: selectedClass
props.description_fallback: Resolved CSS class list to apply when selected, or false when not selected.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Reactive ref wrapping the item value (mirrors the value prop).

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Reactive disabled state — true prevents selection.

props.name: group
props.description_fallback: Back-reference to the parent group provide object.

examples.title_fallback: Injecting the group-item provide in a child component
examples.code:
import { inject } from 'vue'
import type { IGroupItemProvide } from 'origam'

const groupItem = inject<IGroupItemProvide>('groupItem')
const { isSelected, toggle } = groupItem!

entry.name: IGroupProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for a selectable group container — controls the v-model, multiplicity, mandatory selection and the selected-class propagated to items.
entry.definition:
export interface IGroupProps {
    disabled?: boolean
    modelValue?: any
    multiple?: boolean
    mandatory?: boolean
    max?: number
    selectedClass?: string
}

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: When true, disables all items in the group preventing any selection change.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: The current selection — a single value or an array in multiple mode.

props.name: multiple
props.description_fallback: Allows more than one item to be selected at the same time.

props.name: mandatory
props.description_fallback: Enforces that at least one item is always selected; deselecting the last item is blocked.

props.name: max
props.description_fallback: Maximum number of items that can be selected simultaneously (only meaningful in multiple mode).

props.name: selectedClass
props.description_fallback: CSS class propagated to every selected item, overridable per-item via IGroupItemProps.selectedClass.

examples.title_fallback: Extending IGroupProps on a tab bar

entry.name: IGroupProvide
entry.description_fallback: Runtime injection provided by a group component to all its item descendants — exposes registration, selection control, navigation and the full item list.
entry.definition:
export interface IGroupProvide {
    register: (item: IGroupItem, cmp: ComponentInternalInstance) => void
    unregister: (id: number) => void
    select: (id: number, value: boolean) => void
    selected: Ref<Readonly<Array<number>>>
    isSelected: (id: number) => boolean
    prev: () => void
    next: () => void
    selectedClass: Ref<string | undefined>
    items: ComputedRef<Array<{
        id: number
        value: unknown
        disabled: boolean | undefined
    }>>
    disabled: Ref<boolean | undefined>
    getItemIndex: (value: unknown) => number
}

props.name: register
props.description_fallback: Registers a new item in the group; called by each item on mount.

props.name: unregister
props.description_fallback: Removes an item from the group by its numeric id; called on unmount.

props.name: select
props.description_fallback: Sets the selected state of a specific item by id.

props.name: selected
props.description_fallback: Reactive list of currently selected item ids.

props.name: isSelected
props.description_fallback: Returns true if the item with the given id is currently selected.

props.name: prev
props.description_fallback: Selects the previous item in registration order (wraps around).

props.name: next
props.description_fallback: Selects the next item in registration order (wraps around).

props.name: selectedClass
props.description_fallback: CSS class applied to selected items, reactive to the group-level prop.

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Computed list of all registered items with their id, value and disabled state.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Whether the entire group is disabled.

props.name: getItemIndex
props.description_fallback: Returns the registration index of the item whose value matches the argument, or -1.

examples.title_fallback: Injecting the group provide in an item component
examples.code:
import { inject } from 'vue'
import type { IGroupProvide } from 'origam'

const group = inject<IGroupProvide>('group')
group?.next()

entry.name: IHTMLExpandElement
entry.description_fallback: Extended HTMLElement interface used internally by the expand transition utilities — stores the initial style snapshot and the parent reference so the transition can be cleanly reversed.

props.name: _parent
props.description_fallback: Reference to the parent element captured at transition start.

props.name: _initialStyle
props.description_fallback: Snapshot of the element initial transition, overflow, height, and width CSS values, restored on leave.

examples.title_fallback: Internal usage in the expand transition enter hook

entry.name: IHeaderCellProps
entry.description_fallback: Shared props for DataTable header cells — controls sort behavior, sticky positioning, and sort direction icons.
entry.definition:
export interface IHeaderCellProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps {
    disableSort?: boolean
    headerProps?: any
    sticky?: boolean
    multiSort?: boolean
    sortAscIcon?: TIcon
    sortDescIcon?: TIcon
}

props.name: disableSort
props.description_fallback: Disable sorting for this column.

props.name: headerProps
props.description_fallback: Extra props forwarded to the header cell element.

props.name: sticky
props.description_fallback: Make this column header sticky (position: sticky).

props.name: multiSort
props.description_fallback: Enable multi-column sort on this header.

props.name: sortAscIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon rendered when the column is sorted ascending.

props.name: sortDescIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon rendered when the column is sorted descending.

examples.title_fallback: Custom sort icons on a DataTable header
examples.code:
<OrigamDataTable
    sort-asc-icon="mdi-arrow-up"
    sort-desc-icon="mdi-arrow-down"
    :headers="headers"
    :items="rows"
/>

entry.name: IHotkeyOptions
entry.description_fallback: Configuration options for the useHotkey composable and the v-hotkey directive — controls the listened event type, whether inputs are excluded and sequence timing.

props.name: event
props.description_fallback: Keyboard event to listen on — 'keydown' (default) or 'keyup'.

props.name: inputs
props.description_fallback: When true, the hotkey fires even when focus is inside an input, textarea or select element. Defaults to false.

props.name: preventDefault
props.description_fallback: When true, calls event.preventDefault() before invoking the handler. Defaults to false.

props.name: sequenceTimeout
props.description_fallback: Milliseconds allowed between successive keys in a sequence binding before the sequence resets. Defaults to 1000.

examples.title_fallback: Passing options to useHotkey
examples.code:
import { useHotkey } from 'origam'
import type { IHotkeyOptions } from 'origam'

const options: IHotkeyOptions = {
    event: 'keydown',
    inputs: false,
    preventDefault: true,
    sequenceTimeout: 800,
}

useHotkey('ctrl+k', () => openSearch(), options)

entry.name: IHoverDirectiveBinding
entry.description_fallback: Typed directive binding for v-hover — extends Vue's DirectiveBinding with a typed value (boolean or config object) and the directive modifiers.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Directive binding value — a boolean to simply enable/disable, or an IHoverDirectiveConfig object for fine-grained class and handler control.

props.name: modifiers
props.description_fallback: Directive modifiers: callback is the function invoked on hover change; stop prevents event propagation when true.

examples.title_fallback: Using the typed binding in a custom directive implementation

entry.name: IHoverDirectiveConfig
entry.description_fallback: Object configuration accepted by v-hover — specifies which CSS class to toggle and optional mouseenter/mouseleave handlers.

props.name: class
props.description_fallback: CSS class toggled on the element while hovered. Defaults to `{componentName}--hover` when omitted.

props.name: mouseenter
props.description_fallback: Handler called when the pointer or touch enters the element.

props.name: mouseleave
props.description_fallback: Handler called when the pointer or touch leaves the element.

examples.title_fallback: Passing a config object to v-hover

entry.name: IHoverEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit signature for components that propagate their hover state — emits update:hover with a boolean whenever the pointer enters or leaves.

examples.title_fallback: Implementing IHoverEmits on a component

entry.name: IHoverHtmlElement
entry.description_fallback: HTMLElement augmented with a private _hover property that stores the v-hover directive's runtime state on the DOM node.

props.name: _hover
props.description_fallback: Internal runtime state attached by the v-hover directive on the DOM element — holds handlers, class and touch flags.

examples.title_fallback: Casting an element to IHoverHtmlElement in a directive hook

entry.name: IHoverHtmlElementHover
entry.description_fallback: Internal state object stored by the v-hover directive on the DOM element — holds the enabled flag, active class, touch detection and event handlers.
entry.definition:
export interface IHoverHtmlElementHover {
    enabled?: boolean
    class?: string
    touched?: boolean
    isTouch?: boolean
    mouseenter?: (el: HTMLElement, e: Event) => void
    mouseleave?: (el: HTMLElement, e: Event) => void
}

props.name: enabled
props.description_fallback: Whether the v-hover directive is currently active on this element.

props.name: class
props.description_fallback: CSS class toggled by the directive while the element is hovered.

props.name: touched
props.description_fallback: True if the last interaction originated from a touch event.

props.name: isTouch
props.description_fallback: Whether the device is a touch-capable device.

props.name: mouseenter
props.description_fallback: Stored mouseenter handler for cleanup on directive unmount.

props.name: mouseleave
props.description_fallback: Stored mouseleave handler for cleanup on directive unmount.

examples.title_fallback: Inspecting the internal hover state on a mounted element

entry.name: IHoverOptions
entry.description_fallback: Runtime options resolved by the v-hover directive after merging the binding value — holds the final CSS class to toggle on the element.

props.name: class
props.description_fallback: The CSS class that will be added to the element while it is hovered. Derived from the directive binding or defaulting to a component-specific hover class.

examples.title_fallback: Reading the resolved hover options inside a directive

entry.name: IHoverProps
entry.description_fallback: Prop contract for components that expose a controllable hover state — accepts a boolean to force-enable hover or an IHoverState object for per-property overrides while hovered.

props.name: hover
props.description_fallback: Controls the hover state: undefined/false = natural mouseenter/leave; true = forced on; IHoverState object = natural tracking with per-property visual overrides (color, bgColor, border, rounded, elevation, padding, margin, gap) while hovered.

examples.title_fallback: Using IHoverProps with an object state override
examples.code:
<template>
    <origam-card :hover="{ bgColor: 'surface-raised', elevation: 'md' }">
        Hover to elevate
    </origam-card>
</template>

entry.name: IIconAliases
entry.description_fallback: Index-signature map from alias name to icon value, used to register custom icon aliases in the Origam icon configuration.

examples.title_fallback: Registering custom icon aliases in the plugin config

entry.name: IIconComponentProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props surface for OrigamIcon — extends the base IIconProps with color, background color, size, padding, margin, border, dimension, rounded, and a disabled flag.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Renders the icon in a visually disabled state.

examples.title_fallback: Icon with size, color and padding
examples.code:
<OrigamIcon
    icon="mdi-star"
    color="primary"
    size="lg"
    :padding="2"
/>

entry.name: IIconProps
entry.description_fallback: Minimal icon prop surface shared across all Origam components that display an icon — a single optional icon prop accepting any TIcon value.

props.name: icon
props.description_fallback: Icon to display. Accepts an MDI name string, a Vue component, or an SVG node.

examples.title_fallback: Using IIconProps to type a wrapper component

entry.name: IIconSet
entry.description_fallback: Descriptor for a custom Origam icon set — holds the Vue component that the icon renderer will mount when this set is active.

props.name: component
props.description_fallback: The Vue component (or functional component) used to render each icon in this set.

examples.title_fallback: Registering a custom SVG icon set
examples.code:
import type { IIconSet } from 'origam'
import MySvgIcon from './MySvgIcon.vue'

const myIconSet: IIconSet = { component: MySvgIcon }

app.use(origam, { icons: { defaultSet: 'custom', sets: { custom: myIconSet } } })

entry.name: IImgEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit contract for OrigamImg — mirrors the native lifecycle: loadstart fires before the network request, load when fully decoded, error when the browser gives up.

examples.title_fallback: Handling image load and error events
examples.code:
<OrigamImg
    src="/hero.jpg"
    alt="Hero image"
    @load="({ src }) => console.log('loaded', src)"
    @error="({ src }) => console.warn('failed', src)"
/>

entry.name: IImgProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for OrigamImg — covers source, lazy loading, responsive sizes, gradient overlays, cross-origin policy, and transition configuration.
entry.definition:
export interface IImgProps extends IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IResponsiveProps, ITransitionComponentProps, ILazyProps {
    alt?: string
    cover?: boolean,
    draggable?: boolean
    gradient?: string
    lazySrc?: string
    options?: IIntersectionObserverInit
    sizes?: string
    src?: string | ISrcObject
    crossorigin?: TCrossOrigin
    referrerpolicy?: TReferrerPolicy
    srcset?: string
    position?: string
}

props.name: alt
props.description_fallback: Alternative text for the image (required for a11y).

props.name: cover
props.description_fallback: Apply object-fit: cover to fill the container without letterboxing.

props.name: draggable
props.description_fallback: Whether the image is draggable.

props.name: gradient
props.description_fallback: CSS gradient string applied as an overlay on top of the image.

props.name: lazySrc
props.description_fallback: Low-resolution placeholder shown while the real src is loading.

props.name: options
props.description_fallback: IntersectionObserver options for the lazy-load trigger.

props.name: sizes
props.description_fallback: HTML sizes attribute for responsive image selection.

props.name: src
props.description_fallback: Image source. Accepts a URL string or an ISrcObject with srcset and lazySrc.

props.name: crossorigin
props.description_fallback: CORS setting for the image request (anonymous or use-credentials).

props.name: referrerpolicy
props.description_fallback: Referrer policy sent with the image request.

props.name: srcset
props.description_fallback: HTML srcset attribute for responsive image selection.

props.name: position
props.description_fallback: CSS object-position value (e.g. "top center").

examples.title_fallback: Responsive lazy image with a gradient overlay
examples.code:
<OrigamImg
    src="/hero.jpg"
    lazy-src="/hero-thumb.jpg"
    cover
    gradient="to top, rgba(0,0,0,.6), transparent"
    alt="Landscape"
    sizes="(max-width: 600px) 100vw, 50vw"
/>

entry.name: IIndeterminateEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit signature for three-state controls — emits update:indeterminate with a boolean to enable v-model:indeterminate two-way binding on Switch, Checkbox and similar components.

examples.title_fallback: Implementing IIndeterminateEmits on a Checkbox component

entry.name: IInfiniteScrollEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit contract for OrigamInfiniteScroll — the load event fires when the sentinel enters the viewport; the handler must call done() with ok, empty, or error to signal the result.

examples.title_fallback: Fetching the next page on load
examples.code:
<OrigamInfiniteScroll @load="async ({ done }) => {
    const more = await fetchNextPage()
    done(more.length ? 'ok' : 'empty')
}" />

entry.name: IInfiniteScrollIntersectEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit contract for the lower-level OrigamInfiniteScrollIntersect sentinel — bubbles IntersectionObserver entries as an intersect event carrying the visibility state and scroll side.

examples.title_fallback: Using the low-level intersect sentinel
examples.code:
<OrigamInfiniteScrollIntersect
    :root-ref="scrollContainerRef"
    @intersect="({ isIntersecting, side }) => isIntersecting && loadPage(side)"
/>

entry.name: IInfiniteScrollIntersectProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the low-level OrigamInfiniteScrollIntersect sentinel element — configures the IntersectionObserver root, scroll side, and margin.

props.name: side
props.description_fallback: Which scroll direction this sentinel monitors ('start' | 'end' | 'both').

props.name: rootRef
props.description_fallback: The scrollable container element passed as the IntersectionObserver root.

props.name: margin
props.description_fallback: IntersectionObserver rootMargin — triggers load before the sentinel is fully visible.

examples.title_fallback: Sentinel with 200px pre-load margin
examples.code:
<OrigamInfiniteScrollIntersect
    :root-ref="listRef"
    side="end"
    margin="0px 0px 200px 0px"
    @intersect="onIntersect"
/>

entry.name: IInfiniteScrollProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for OrigamInfiniteScroll — configures scroll side, intersection mode, empty/load-more text labels, root margin, and visual surfaces.
entry.definition:
export interface IInfiniteScrollProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IDimensionProps, ITagProps, IDirectionProps, ITypographyProps {
    side?: TInfiniteScrollSide
    mode?: TInfiniteScrollMode
    loadMoreText?: string
    emptyText?: string
    margin?: string
}

props.name: side
props.description_fallback: Which end of the scroll container triggers load ('start' | 'end' | 'both').

props.name: mode
props.description_fallback: Intersection mode: 'intersect' (automatic) or 'manual' (shows a load-more button).

props.name: loadMoreText
props.description_fallback: Label for the manual load-more button.

props.name: emptyText
props.description_fallback: Text displayed when done is called with empty.

props.name: margin
props.description_fallback: IntersectionObserver rootMargin for the sentinel.

examples.title_fallback: Automatic infinite scroll on the bottom of a list
examples.code:
<OrigamInfiniteScroll
    side="end"
    mode="intersect"
    empty-text="No more results"
    @load="loadMore"
>
    <ArticleCard v-for="a in articles" :key="a.id" v-bind="a" />
</OrigamInfiniteScroll>

entry.name: IInlineEditActionsSlotBindings
entry.description_fallback: Scoped slot bindings exposed by the #actions slot of OrigamInlineEdit — provides confirm/cancel callbacks and a pending flag for rendering custom action buttons.

props.name: confirm
props.description_fallback: Run the validator and commit the draft if it passes.

props.name: cancel
props.description_fallback: Discard the draft and return to display mode.

props.name: isPending
props.description_fallback: True while an async validator is in flight.

examples.title_fallback: Custom confirm / cancel buttons via #actions slot
examples.code:
<OrigamInlineEdit v-model="title">
    <template #actions="{ confirm, cancel, isPending }">
        <button :disabled="isPending" @click="confirm">Save</button>
        <button @click="cancel">Discard</button>
    </template>
</OrigamInlineEdit>

entry.name: IInlineEditDisplaySlotBindings
entry.description_fallback: Scoped slot bindings exposed by the #display slot of OrigamInlineEdit — provides the current value, an edit trigger, empty/placeholder state, and the disabled flag.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Current model value coerced to string.

props.name: edit
props.description_fallback: Switches the component into edit mode.

props.name: isEmpty
props.description_fallback: True when the value is empty or nullish — use to drive placeholder styling.

props.name: placeholder
props.description_fallback: Resolved placeholder string with a sane fallback.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Mirrors the disabled prop for visual handling.

examples.title_fallback: Custom display affordance using a heading

entry.name: IInlineEditEditSlotBindings
entry.description_fallback: Scoped slot bindings exposed by the #edit slot of OrigamInlineEdit — gives full control over the input layout while delegating state-machine logic to the component.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Current draft value (two-way; mutate via setValue).

props.name: setValue
props.description_fallback: Update the draft value from within the slot.

props.name: confirm
props.description_fallback: Run the validator and, if it passes, commit the draft.

props.name: cancel
props.description_fallback: Discard the draft and return to display mode.

props.name: error
props.description_fallback: Current validation error message, or null when none.

props.name: isPending
props.description_fallback: True while an async validator is in flight.

examples.title_fallback: Custom textarea input in the #edit slot

entry.name: IInlineEditEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit contract for OrigamInlineEdit — covers the v-model echo, edit/cancel/confirm lifecycle events, and validation error reporting.

examples.title_fallback: Persisting to an API on confirm
examples.code:
<OrigamInlineEdit
    v-model="title"
    @confirm="value => api.patchTitle(value)"
    @validate-error="msg => toast.error(msg)"
/>

entry.name: IInlineEditProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for OrigamInlineEdit — the edit-in-place component. Controls validation rules, auto-focus, select-on-focus, confirm/cancel triggers, multiline mode, and built-in action buttons.
entry.definition:
export interface IInlineEditProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, ITypographyProps {
    /**
     * Current value (v-model target). Accepts both `string` and
     * `number` for ergonomics — the internal draft is normalised to a
     * string, then `update:modelValue` re-emits with the same shape as
     * the original (number stays number, string stays string).
     */
    modelValue: string | number
    /**
     * Placeholder shown on the input AND surfaced inside the default
     * `#display` slot when `modelValue` is empty. Consumers wrap with
     * `t()` if they need i18n.
     */
    placeholder?: string
    /**
     * Array of validation rules using the standard DS contract
     * (same as `OrigamTextField` / `OrigamInput` rules). Each rule
     * is a function receiving the current string value and returning
     * `true` (pass) or an error message string (fail). Rules are
     * evaluated sequentially on confirm; the first failure surfaces
     * as the inline error and blocks the commit.
     *
     * When both `rules` and `validate` are provided, `rules` are
     * evaluated first. If any rule fails, `validate` is skipped.
     * Only the first error message is displayed.
     */
    rules?: Array<TInlineEditRule>
    /**
     * Validation callback. Returns `true` (or `Promise<true>`) when
     * the draft is acceptable, or an error message string that will
     * be surfaced in a `role="alert"` element near the input.
     *
     * Sync validators run on every Enter / blur. Async validators
     * keep the editor open until the returned Promise settles.
     *
     * When both `rules` and `validate` are provided, `rules` are
     * evaluated first. `validate` only runs if all rules pass.
     */
    validate?: TInlineEditValidator
    /**
     * Auto-focus the input when entering edit mode. Setting this to
     * false is useful for forms where keyboard focus is managed by a
     * parent (e.g. a wizard that steps focus through siblings).
     *
     * @default true
     */
    autoFocus?: boolean
    /**
     * Select all the input's text when it gains focus on edit entry.
     * Common for "click title to rename" patterns — the user can
     * immediately type a replacement without selecting first.
     *
     * @default true
     */
    selectOnFocus?: boolean
    /**
     * Confirm the draft when the input loses focus. When false, the
     * editor stays open on blur and the user must explicitly Enter
     * to confirm or Escape to cancel.
     *
     * @default true
     */
    confirmOnBlur?: boolean
    /**
     * Confirm on `Enter`. Disable when the edit zone is a multiline
     * textarea and Enter should produce a newline (Cmd/Ctrl+Enter to
     * confirm is the convention — apps wire this manually).
     *
     * @default true
     */
    confirmOnEnter?: boolean
    /**
     * Cancel (revert the draft) on `Escape`.
     *
     * @default true
     */
    cancelOnEscape?: boolean
    /**
     * Disables the component — the display affordance becomes
     * non-interactive (button `disabled`, span `aria-disabled`), the
     * `edit()` method short-circuits, and any in-flight edit is
     * cancelled (draft discarded).
     *
     * @default false
     */
    disabled?: boolean
    /**
     * Render a `<textarea>` instead of a `<input>` in edit mode.
     * When true, `confirmOnEnter` should generally be disabled by the
     * consumer so newlines are not swallowed.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    multiline?: boolean
    /**
     * Strip surrounding whitespace before emitting `update:modelValue`
     * AND before running the validator. Disable for fields where
     * trailing spaces are significant (passwords, …).
     *
     * @default true
     */
    trim?: boolean
    /**
     * Native HTML input type when not in multiline mode.
     *
     * @default 'text'
     */
    inputType?: TInlineEditInputType
    /**
     * Surface a loading affordance (CSS class hook) on the root while
     * an async `validate` Promise is in flight. The component sets
     * `aria-busy="true"` regardless — this prop only controls the
     * visual hook.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    loadingOnConfirm?: boolean
    /**
     * Render built-in action buttons (Edit / Confirm / Cancel) next to
     * the component.
     *
     * When `false` (default), only keyboard shortcuts are available:
     * Enter to confirm, Escape to cancel, click on the display to edit.
     *
     * When `true`, three icon buttons are rendered:
     * - **Edit** — visible in display mode, enters edit mode on click.
     * - **Confirm** — visible in edit mode, commits the draft on click.
     * - **Cancel** — visible in edit mode, discards the draft on click.
     *
     * The `disabled` prop also disables all three buttons.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    showActions?: boolean
}

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: Current value (v-model target). Accepts string or number.

props.name: placeholder
props.description_fallback: Placeholder shown on the input and in the default display slot when the value is empty.

props.name: rules
props.description_fallback: Array of validation rules evaluated on confirm. First failure blocks the commit.

props.name: validate
props.description_fallback: Sync or async validation callback. Runs after rules pass. Returns true or an error message.

props.name: autoFocus
props.description_fallback: Auto-focus the input when entering edit mode.

props.name: selectOnFocus
props.description_fallback: Select all text when the input gains focus on edit entry.

props.name: confirmOnBlur
props.description_fallback: Confirm the draft when the input loses focus.

props.name: confirmOnEnter
props.description_fallback: Confirm on Enter. Disable for multiline mode where Enter should insert a newline.

props.name: cancelOnEscape
props.description_fallback: Cancel (revert) the draft on Escape.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables the component. The display affordance becomes non-interactive.

props.name: multiline
props.description_fallback: Render a <textarea> instead of <input> in edit mode.

props.name: trim
props.description_fallback: Strip surrounding whitespace before emitting and validating.

props.name: inputType
props.description_fallback: Native HTML input type when not in multiline mode.

props.name: loadingOnConfirm
props.description_fallback: Surface a loading affordance (CSS class hook) while an async validator is in flight.

props.name: showActions
props.description_fallback: Render built-in Edit / Confirm / Cancel icon buttons next to the component.

examples.title_fallback: Edit-in-place title with async validation
examples.code:
<OrigamInlineEdit
    v-model="title"
    :validate="async v => v.length >= 3 || 'At least 3 characters'"
    show-actions
    loading-on-confirm
/>

entry.name: IIntersectDirectiveBinding
entry.description_fallback: Typed directive binding for v-intersect — extends Vue's DirectiveBinding with a typed value (handler or handler+options) and once/quiet modifiers.

entry.name: IIntersectHtmlElement
entry.description_fallback: HTMLElement augmented with a private _observe property that the v-intersect directive uses to store its per-observer runtime state on the DOM node.

props.description_fallback: Internal map keyed by observer id, used by v-intersect to track and clean up each IntersectionObserver attached to the element.

entry.name: IIntersectHtmlElementObserve
entry.description_fallback: Index signature map stored on an element by the v-intersect directive — each key is an observer id and the value holds the corresponding IntersectionObserver instance and its options.

entry.name: IIntersectionObserverInit
entry.description_fallback: Typed options bag for configuring an IntersectionObserver — mirrors the native IntersectionObserverInit with nullable root and flexible threshold.

props.name: root
props.description_fallback: The element used as the viewport for checking visibility. Defaults to the browser viewport when null or omitted.

props.name: rootMargin
props.description_fallback: CSS margin around the root applied before computing intersections, e.g. "10px 0px".

props.name: threshold
props.description_fallback: Single ratio or list of ratios at which the observer callback fires. Defaults to 0.

</OrigamLazy>

import { inject } from 'vue'
