entry.definition:
export interface IParallaxLayerProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps {
    /**
     * Parallax speed multiplier for this layer.
     *  - `0`   → fixed (no movement, sticks to host)
     *  - `0.5` → half the scroll speed (background — far)
     *  - `1`   → moves with the scroll (neutral)
     *  - `>1`  → faster than the scroll (foreground — near)
     *
     * Negative values reverse the direction.
     */
    speed?: number

    /**
     * Static X-axis offset (px) applied on top of the parallax translation.
     * Useful to nudge a layer without changing its speed.
     */
    offsetX?: number

    /**
     * Static Y-axis offset (px) applied on top of the parallax translation.
     */
    offsetY?: number

    /**
     * Optional `z-index` override. By default layers stack in document order.
     */
    zIndex?: number
}

props.name: speed
props.description_fallback:
Parallax speed multiplier for this layer.
 - `0`   → fixed (no movement, sticks to host)
 - `0.5` → half the scroll speed (background — far)
 - `1`   → moves with the scroll (neutral)
 - `>1`  → faster than the scroll (foreground — near)

Negative values reverse the direction.

props.name: offsetX
props.description_fallback:
Static X-axis offset (px) applied on top of the parallax translation.
Useful to nudge a layer without changing its speed.

props.name: offsetY
props.description_fallback: Static Y-axis offset (px) applied on top of the parallax translation.

props.name: zIndex
props.description_fallback: Optional `z-index` override. By default layers stack in document order.

entry.name: IParallaxLayerProvide
entry.description_fallback:
Provide context exposed by the enriched OrigamParallax to its
`<OrigamParallaxLayer>` children. Distinct from the legacy
`IParallaxProvide` consumed by `<OrigamParallaxElement>` (kept
separate to avoid coupling the two APIs).
entry.definition:
export interface IParallaxLayerProvide {
    direction: Ref<TParallaxDirection>
    easing: Ref<TParallaxEasing | string>
    disabled: Ref<boolean>
    /**
     * Live scroll progress, normalised to `[0, 1]`. `0` = host just entered
     * the viewport, `1` = host just left.
     */
    progress: Ref<number>
    /**
     * Live mouse-ratio for `direction="both"` on `event="move"` —
     * `{ x: 0..1, y: 0..1 }`. Falls back to `{ x: 0.5, y: 0.5 }` when no
     * mouse data is available.
     */
    mouseRatio: Ref<{ x: number, y: number }>
    /**
     * Whether the runtime is using the CSS scroll-driven path (true) or the
     * JS rAF fallback (false). Layers may opt out of the JS work when the
     * CSS path is active.
     */
    cssScrollDriven: Ref<boolean>
    /**
     * Whether `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` is honoured — layers MUST
     * stay at offset 0 when this is true.
     */
    reducedMotion: Ref<boolean>
    /**
     * Register / unregister a layer with the parent. The parent reads the
     * registry to compute and apply per-layer transforms each frame.
     */
    register: (layer: IParallaxLayerRegistry) => void
    unregister: (id: symbol) => void
}

props.name: progress
props.description_fallback:
Live scroll progress, normalised to `[0, 1]`. `0` = host just entered
the viewport, `1` = host just left.

props.name: mouseRatio
props.description_fallback:
Live mouse-ratio for `direction="both"` on `event="move"` —
`{ x: 0..1, y: 0..1 }`. Falls back to `{ x: 0.5, y: 0.5 }` when no
mouse data is available.

props.name: cssScrollDriven
props.description_fallback:
Whether the runtime is using the CSS scroll-driven path (true) or the
JS rAF fallback (false). Layers may opt out of the JS work when the
CSS path is active.

props.name: reducedMotion
props.description_fallback:
Whether `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` is honoured — layers MUST
stay at offset 0 when this is true.

props.name: register
props.description_fallback:
Register / unregister a layer with the parent. The parent reads the
registry to compute and apply per-layer transforms each frame.

entry.name: IParallaxLayerRegistry
entry.description_fallback: Internal layer descriptor registered into the parent OrigamParallax via provide/inject. The parent reads speed, offsetX and offsetY to compute the per-frame transform, then applies it to the shared target HTMLElement.
entry.definition:
export interface IParallaxLayerRegistry {
    /**
     * Internal layer descriptor as registered into the parent OrigamParallax
     * via `provide` / `inject`. The parent reads `speed`, `offsetX`,
     * `offsetY` to compute the per-frame transform, then applies it via the
     * shared `target` HTMLElement.
     */
    id: symbol
    speed: number
    offsetX: number
    offsetY: number
    target: HTMLElement
}

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Unique symbol identifier for the layer. Used to register and unregister the layer in the parent context.

props.name: speed
props.description_fallback: Parallax speed multiplier. 0 = fixed, 0.5 = half scroll speed, 1 = neutral, >1 = faster than scroll. Negative values reverse direction.

props.name: offsetX
props.description_fallback: Static X-axis offset (px) applied on top of the parallax translation.

props.name: offsetY
props.description_fallback: Static Y-axis offset (px) applied on top of the parallax translation.

props.name: target
props.description_fallback: Reference to the DOM element that receives the per-frame CSS transform.

examples.title_fallback: Using IParallaxLayerRegistry in a custom layer
examples.code:
import type { IParallaxLayerRegistry } from 'origam'

const layer: IParallaxLayerRegistry = {
    id: Symbol('my-layer'),
    speed: 0.5,
    offsetX: 0,
    offsetY: 0,
    target: document.querySelector('.layer-el') as HTMLElement,
}

entry.name: IParallaxProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamParallax>. Extends common component, color, spacing, border, dimension and audio contracts, plus parallax-specific controls for easing, speed, direction, event type and threshold.
entry.definition:
export interface IParallaxProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IBorderProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IDimensionProps, IAudioProps, IUseAudioProps {
    /**
     * @deprecated Use `duration` instead. `animationDuration` is kept as a
     * silent alias for backwards-compat and will be removed in v3.0.0.
     * When both are passed, `animationDuration` wins so existing consumers
     * relying on the old prop name keep their behaviour, but a one-shot
     * console warning is emitted at runtime to flag the migration.
     */
    animationDuration?: number
    /**
     * Easing curve. Accepts either:
     *  - a TParallaxEasing keyword (`'linear' | 'ease-out' | 'spring'`),
     *    routed through the scroll-driven multi-layer code path,
     *  - or any raw CSS timing-function string (legacy mouse / scroll
     *    transition path, e.g. `'cubic-bezier(...)'`).
     *
     * The keyword form is preferred for new code; the raw string form is
     * kept for backwards compatibility with v2.x.
     */
    easing?: TParallaxEasing | string
    perspective?: number
    event?: TParallaxEvent
    active?: boolean
    duration?: number

    // Phase 4 — multi-layer parallax.

    /**
     * Direction of the parallax effect.
     *  - `'vertical'` (default) → translateY based on vertical scroll progress.
     *  - `'horizontal'`         → translateX based on vertical scroll progress.
     *  - `'both'`               → translate on both axes; on `event="move"`
     *                            this maps to a 2D mouse ratio.
     */
    direction?: TParallaxDirection

    /**
     * Global parallax speed multiplier when no `<OrigamParallaxLayer>` is
     * provided (single-layer mode). Equivalent to wrapping the slot in a
     * single layer with this speed. Default `0.5`.
     */
    speed?: number

    /**
     * Force the parallax effect OFF (translate stays at 0 regardless of
     * scroll / events). Distinct from `active` which only freezes the
     * mouse-driven mode. Useful for SSR, snapshot tests, or `prefers-
     * reduced-motion` integrations.
     */
    disabled?: boolean

    /**
     * Proportion (0 → 1) of the host that must enter the viewport before
     * the parallax effect activates. `0` (default) starts the effect the
     * moment the host's top edge crosses the bottom of the viewport;
     * `0.3` waits until 30% of the host is visible.
     */
    threshold?: number
}

props.name: animationDuration
props.description_fallback: Deprecated — use `duration` instead. Kept as a silent alias for backwards compat; a one-shot console warning is emitted when used.

props.name: easing
props.description_fallback: Easing curve. Accepts a TParallaxEasing keyword (linear | ease-out | spring) or any raw CSS timing-function string.

props.name: perspective
props.description_fallback: CSS perspective depth (px) applied to the parallax container for 3D transform effects.

props.name: event
props.description_fallback: Event type that drives the parallax — scroll, move (mouse), or similar. Controls which input is read.

props.name: active
props.description_fallback: Freezes the mouse-driven mode when false. Distinct from `disabled` which stops all effects.

props.name: duration
props.description_fallback: Transition duration (ms) for the parallax interpolation. Replaces the deprecated `animationDuration`.

props.name: direction
props.description_fallback: Direction of the parallax effect — "vertical" (translateY), "horizontal" (translateX), or "both" (2D on mouse move).

props.name: speed
props.description_fallback: Global speed multiplier in single-layer mode. Default 0.5. Equivalent to wrapping slot in a single layer with this speed.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Forces the parallax effect OFF (translate stays at 0). Useful for SSR, snapshot tests or prefers-reduced-motion integrations.

props.name: threshold
props.description_fallback: Proportion (0→1) of host visible in viewport before the effect activates. 0 = starts immediately at viewport edge, 0.3 = waits until 30% visible.

examples.title_fallback: Using IParallaxProps for a multi-layer parallax
examples.code:
import type { IParallaxProps } from 'origam'

const parallaxConfig: Partial<IParallaxProps> = {
    direction: 'vertical',
    speed: 0.4,
    easing: 'ease-out',
    threshold: 0.1,
    disabled: false,
}

entry.name: IParallaxProvide
entry.description_fallback: Provide context exposed by OrigamParallax to its OrigamParallaxElement children via Vue inject. Carries reactive refs for audio data, pointer event position, movement state, easing and container shape.
entry.definition:
export interface IParallaxProvide {
    audioData: Ref<any>
    eventData: Ref<TPoint>
    movement: Ref<{ x: number, y: number, target?: IBox | DOMRect }>
    isMoving: Ref<boolean>
    event: Ref<TParallaxEvent>
    duration: Ref<number>
    easing: Ref<string>
    shape: Ref<IBox | null>
}

props.name: audioData
props.description_fallback: Reactive audio analysis data (frequency/amplitude array) when the parallax is driven by audio input.

props.name: eventData
props.description_fallback: Reactive current pointer/mouse position as a normalised TPoint { x, y }.

props.name: movement
props.type_label: Ref<{ x: number, y: number, target?: IBox | DOMRect }>
props.description_fallback: Reactive current movement vector plus optional target bounding box. Consumed by OrigamParallaxElement to compute transforms.

props.name: isMoving
props.description_fallback: True while a pointer/scroll movement is in progress.

props.name: event
props.description_fallback: The active event type (scroll | move | audio…) driving this parallax instance.

props.name: duration
props.description_fallback: Transition duration (ms) shared with all children for interpolation.

props.name: easing
props.description_fallback: CSS timing-function string shared with children for transition easing.

props.name: shape
props.description_fallback: Bounding box of the host parallax container, updated on resize. Null before mount.

examples.title_fallback: Injecting the parallax context in a child component

entry.name: IPasswordFieldEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emits contract for <OrigamPasswordField>. Extends IFieldEmits and IInputEmits with a control click event, a control mousedown event and a strength-level update event.

examples.title_fallback: Listening to password strength changes
examples.code:
<origam-password-field
    v-model="password"
    v-model:strength="strength"
    @click:control="onControlClick"
/>

entry.name: IPasswordFieldProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for <OrigamPasswordField>. Extends ITextFieldProps with show/hide toggle icons, strength bar, configurable requirements checklist and a minimal mode that strips all adornments.
entry.definition:
export interface IPasswordFieldProps extends ITextFieldProps {
    /** Icon shown when the password is HIDDEN (toggle reveals). */
    onIcon?: TIcon
    /** Icon shown when the password is VISIBLE (toggle hides). */
    offIcon?: TIcon
    /** Native maxlength attribute mirrored into the requirements popup. */
    maxlength?: number
    /**
     * Master switch for the strength-requirements popup. When false, the
     * field behaves exactly like a TextField with type="password".
     */
    requirements?: boolean
    /**
     * Keep the requirements popup open even when the field is not focused.
     * Useful for forms that want the requirements always visible.
     */
    persistentRequirements?: boolean
    /** Minimum password length (default 8) — drives the `minLength` rule. */
    minLength?: number
    /** Require at least one lowercase letter. */
    needTiny?: boolean
    /** Require at least one uppercase letter. */
    needUppercase?: boolean
    /** Require at least one digit. */
    needNumber?: boolean
    /** Require at least one non-alphanumeric character. */
    needSpecial?: boolean
    /** Forwarded to the internal `<OrigamMenu>` (location, delays, etc.). */
    menuProps?: IMenuProps
    /** Reserved — toggle the requirements menu programmatically. */
    menu?: boolean
    /**
     * Renders a 4-segment strength bar under the input. Each segment fills
     * as the password gets stronger (weak → fair → good → strong) using
     * `--origam-password-field__strength---bg-{level}` semantic tokens.
     */
    strengthBar?: boolean
    /**
     * Inline requirements checklist (the modern, layout-aware API).
     * Pass an array of `IPasswordRequirement` predicates to render a
     * checklist under the input. When `requirements === true` and
     * `requirementRules` is undefined, the component falls back to
     * `DEFAULT_PASSWORD_REQUIREMENTS` (≥8 chars, ≥1 uppercase, ≥1
     * number, ≥1 special).
     *
     * Distinct from `rules` (inherited from `IValidationProps`) which
     * is the form-level validation rule array — those still flow through
     * to the underlying `<origam-input>`.
     */
    requirementRules?: IPasswordRequirement[]
    /**
     * Layout for the requirements checklist:
     *   - `list` (default) — vertical `<ul>` of `<li>` rows
     *   - `tiles` — wrap of `<OrigamChip>` pills
     */
    requirementsLayout?: 'list' | 'tiles'
    /**
     * Strip every decoration (toggle eye, strength bar, requirements
     * checklist). Useful for confirm-password sub-fields where the
     * adornments would only add noise.
     */
    minimal?: boolean
}

props.name: onIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon displayed when the password is HIDDEN (clicking reveals the value).

props.name: offIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon displayed when the password is VISIBLE (clicking hides the value).

props.name: maxlength
props.description_fallback: Native maxlength attribute mirrored into the requirements popup.

props.name: requirements
props.description_fallback: Master switch for the strength-requirements popup. When false the field behaves exactly like a standard TextField.

props.name: persistentRequirements
props.description_fallback: Keep the requirements popup open even when the field is not focused. Useful for forms that want requirements always visible.

props.name: minLength
props.description_fallback: Minimum password length (default 8). Drives the minLength rule injected automatically.

props.name: needTiny
props.description_fallback: Require at least one lowercase letter.

props.name: needUppercase
props.description_fallback: Require at least one uppercase letter.

props.name: needNumber
props.description_fallback: Require at least one digit.

props.name: needSpecial
props.description_fallback: Require at least one non-alphanumeric character.

props.name: menuProps
props.description_fallback: Props forwarded to the internal <OrigamMenu> (location, delays, etc.).

props.name: menu
props.description_fallback: Programmatically controls the requirements menu open/close state.

props.name: strengthBar
props.description_fallback: Renders a 4-segment strength bar under the input. Segments fill as the password grows stronger using semantic colour tokens.

props.name: requirementRules
props.description_fallback: Inline requirements checklist. Pass an array of IPasswordRequirement predicates. Falls back to DEFAULT_PASSWORD_REQUIREMENTS when requirements=true and this is omitted.

props.name: requirementsLayout
props.description_fallback: Layout for the requirements checklist — "list" (default) vertical ul/li rows, or "tiles" OrigamChip pills.

props.name: minimal
props.description_fallback: Strips every decoration (toggle eye, strength bar, requirements checklist). Useful for confirm-password fields where adornments add noise.

examples.title_fallback: PasswordField with strength bar and requirements
examples.code:
<origam-password-field
    v-model="password"
    :requirements="true"
    :strength-bar="true"
    :need-uppercase="true"
    :need-number="true"
    :min-length="10"
/>

entry.name: IPasswordFieldSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slots contract for <OrigamPasswordField>. Extends IFieldSlots and IInputSlots (minus default) with an info slot for the requirements popup body, a counter slot and a field slot to replace the native input.

props.name: info
props.description_fallback: Replaces the default strength-requirements popup body. Receives the current requirements state as bindings.

props.name: counter
props.type_label: (data: { counter: string, max?: string | number, value: string | number }) => any
props.description_fallback: Replaces the character counter rendered inside the details slot.

props.name: field
props.type_label: (data: { id: string, isDisabled: boolean, isDirty: boolean, isValid: boolean | undefined, isReadonly: boolean }) => any
props.description_fallback: Replaces the default <input> element. Receives field state bindings.

examples.title_fallback: Custom requirements popup via the info slot

entry.name: IPasswordRequirement
entry.description_fallback: Descriptor for one rule shown in the requirements checklist or tile grid of <OrigamPasswordField>. The test() predicate is evaluated reactively against the current password value.

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Stable identifier used as :key and as data-cy suffix in stories.

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Human-readable label rendered next to the check/cross icon.

props.name: test
props.description_fallback: Predicate evaluated against the current password value. Returns true when the rule is satisfied.

examples.title_fallback: Defining custom password requirements
examples.code:
import type { IPasswordRequirement } from 'origam'

const requirements: IPasswordRequirement[] = [
    { id: 'min-length', label: 'At least 8 characters', test: (v) => v.length >= 8 },
    { id: 'uppercase', label: 'One uppercase letter', test: (v) => /[A-Z]/.test(v) },
    { id: 'number', label: 'One number', test: (v) => /[0-9]/.test(v) },
]

entry.name: IPasswordStrength
entry.description_fallback: Result returned by computeStrength() and consumed by <OrigamPasswordField> to drive the strength bar UI. Carries a numeric score (0..4) and a semantic level identifier.

props.name: score
props.description_fallback: Numeric score 0..4 — number of bar segments that should fill. Maps 1:1 to the colour tier via level.

props.name: level
props.description_fallback: Colour-tier identifier (weak | fair | good | strong) resolving to the --origam-password-field__strength---bg-{level} token.

entry.name: IPositionProps
entry.description_fallback: CSS position contract — the positioning scheme plus numeric/string inset values for each side. Consumed by fixed or sticky surfaces such as app bars, FABs and snackbars.
entry.definition:
export interface IPositionProps {
    position?: TPosition

    top?: number | string
    bottom?: number | string
    left?: number | string
    right?: number | string
}

props.name: position
props.description_fallback: CSS position scheme — "static" | "relative" | "absolute" | "fixed" | "sticky". Maps directly to the position CSS property.

props.name: top
props.description_fallback: Top inset. A number is resolved to "Npx"; a string is used verbatim (any CSS length).

props.name: bottom
props.description_fallback: Bottom inset. Same value shapes as `top`.

props.name: left
props.description_fallback: Left inset. Same value shapes as `top`.

props.name: right
props.description_fallback: Right inset. Same value shapes as `top`.

examples.title_fallback: Extending the interface for a sticky component

props.name: rotate
props.description_fallback: Starting angle of the arc in degrees. Default 0 = top of the circle. Accepts any CSS angle or a raw number (interpreted as degrees).

entry.name: IProgressLinearProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for <OrigamProgressLinear>. Extends IProgressTypeProps, IRoundedProps and ILocationProps with buffer value, clickable track, reverse direction and stream mode.

props.name: bufferValue
props.description_fallback: Secondary buffer fill level (0..max). Renders a lighter secondary segment behind the main fill, useful for media buffering states.

props.name: clickable
props.description_fallback: Allows the user to click the track to seek/set the value. Emits update:modelValue on click.

props.name: reverse
props.description_fallback: Fills the bar from right to left instead of left to right.

props.name: stream
props.description_fallback: Adds a streaming animation on the buffer area to suggest incoming data.

entry.name: IProgressProps
entry.description_fallback: Unified props contract for <OrigamProgress> — the polymorphic wrapper that renders either a linear or circular indicator depending on the type prop. Extends both IProgressLinearProps and IProgressCircularProps.

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: Selects the progress variant — "linear" or "circular". Determines which renderer is mounted.

examples.title_fallback: Switching between linear and circular
examples.code:

    v-model="value"
    :type="isSpinner ? 'circular' : 'linear'"

entry.name: IProgressTypeProps
entry.description_fallback: Base props shared by all progress variants (linear and circular). Carries value, indeterminate mode, thickness, active flag, absolute positioning, max value, striped mode and an accessible label.
entry.definition:
export interface IProgressTypeProps extends ITagProps, ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps {
    indeterminate?: boolean
    modelValue?: string | number
    thickness?: string | number
    active?: boolean
    absolute?: boolean
    max?: number | string
    striped?: boolean
    /**
     * Accessible label for the progress bar (aria-label). When omitted,
     * defaults to `'Loading'`. Pass a localised string via your i18n
     * provider or the parent component's `labels` mechanism.
     */
    label?: string
}

props.name: indeterminate
props.description_fallback: Puts the progress bar in indeterminate mode — animates continuously without a value.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: Current progress value (v-model). Interpreted as a percentage between 0 and max.

props.name: thickness
props.description_fallback: Track thickness. A number is resolved to px; a string is used verbatim.

props.name: active
props.description_fallback: Whether the progress indicator is visible. When false the element is hidden without being removed from the DOM.

props.name: absolute
props.description_fallback: Positions the progress bar absolutely (typically at the top of a container).

props.name: max
props.description_fallback: Maximum value. Defaults to 100.

props.name: striped
props.description_fallback: Adds a diagonal stripe pattern over the filled portion (linear only).

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Accessible aria-label for the progress bar. Defaults to "Loading" when omitted.

examples.title_fallback: Determinate progress bar with max and label
examples.code:
<origam-progress-linear
    :model-value="42"
    :max="100"
    label="Upload progress"
    color="primary"
/>

entry.name: IQrCodeLogo
entry.description_fallback: Optional logo overlay rendered at the centre of an OrigamQrCode. The overlay is painted on top of the matrix — error-correction compensates for covered modules up to the configured level.
entry.definition:
export interface IQrCodeLogo {
    /**
     * Logo source. Any URL accepted by `<image href>` works — SVG, PNG,
     * data-URI, …. The SVG fragment loads the asset asynchronously; if
     * it fails the QR remains valid (only the overlay disappears).
     */
    src: string
    /**
     * Logo width as a proportion of the QR width (0..1).
     *
     * @default 0.2
     */
    size?: number
    /**
     * Pixel padding of the (opaque) backdrop drawn around the logo.
     * Keeps the modules immediately adjacent to the logo readable —
     * without padding, the scanner sees a soft-edged blob and fails.
     *
     * @default 6
     */
    padding?: number
    /**
     * Backdrop colour painted under the logo (and within the padding
     * box). Defaults to the QR background; pass a different value to
     * paint a coloured halo (e.g. brand colour for a logotype that
     * already includes its own white box).
     *
     * @default — falls back to the QR `background` prop.
     */
    background?: string
}

props.name: src
props.description_fallback: Logo source URL. Any URL accepted by <image href> — SVG, PNG, data-URI. If the asset fails to load the QR matrix remains valid.

props.name: size
props.description_fallback: Logo width as a proportion of the QR width (0..1). Default 0.2. Values above 0.3 trigger a console.warn as no error-correction level survives that.

props.name: padding
props.description_fallback: Pixel padding of the opaque backdrop drawn around the logo. Default 6. Keeps adjacent modules readable.

props.name: background
props.description_fallback: Backdrop colour under the logo and within the padding box. Defaults to the QR background. Pass a brand colour to create a coloured halo.

examples.title_fallback: QR code with a centred logo overlay
examples.code:
import type { IQrCodeLogo } from 'origam'

const logo: IQrCodeLogo = {
    src: '/logo.svg',
    size: 0.2,
    padding: 8,
    background: '#ffffff',
}

entry.name: IQrCodeOptions
entry.description_fallback: Per-matrix overrides applied inside the SVG (modules + quiet zone) independently of the wrapper-level props. Pass via the qrCodeProps prop on <OrigamQrCode>.
entry.definition: export interface IQrCodeOptions extends IRoundedProps, IBgColorProps, IColorProps {}

examples.title_fallback: Custom module color and rounded corners

entry.name: IQrCodeProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamQrCode> — SSR-safe SVG QR code renderer. Extends all common DS surface contracts plus QR-specific controls for value, error correction level, quiet zone, overlays and accessibility.
entry.definition:
export interface IQrCodeProps
    extends ICommonsComponentProps,
        ITagProps,
        IColorProps,
        IBgColorProps,
        IRoundedProps,
        IBorderProps,
        IElevationProps,
        ISizeProps,
        IPaddingProps,
        IMarginProps {
    /**
     * Text or URL encoded into the QR matrix. UTF-8 is supported via
     * the underlying `qrcode-generator` Byte mode.
     */
    value: string
    /**
     * Optional heading rendered above the QR matrix inside the
     * wrapper. Useful when the QR is presented alongside meta
     * (campaign name, recipient, …) — no manual extra markup needed.
     */
    title?: string
    /**
     * Per-matrix overrides — see `IQrCodeOptions`. When omitted, the
     * SVG inherits the wrapper-level `color` / `bgColor` / and
     * defaults to square modules.
     */
    qrCodeProps?: IQrCodeOptions
    /**
     * Reed-Solomon redundancy level. Higher levels reserve more matrix
     * cells for correction codewords (and therefore tolerate more
     * damage / overlay). See `TQrCodeErrorCorrectionLevel` for the
     * tradeoff matrix.
     *
     * @default 'M'
     */
    errorCorrectionLevel?: TQrCodeErrorCorrectionLevel
    /**
     * Number of modules reserved as quiet-zone padding around the
     * matrix. The ISO spec mandates ≥4 — anything smaller risks
     * scanner failures, anything larger is purely aesthetic.
     *
     * @default 4
     */
    quietZone?: number
    /**
     * Optional centred icon overlay — rendered via `<OrigamIcon>` on
     * top of the matrix. Coexists with `image`; the `#center` slot
     * overrides both when provided.
     */
    icon?: TIcon
    /**
     * Optional centred image overlay. Accepts a raw URL (string) or
     * the standard `ISrcObject` shape (src / srcset / lazySrc /
     * aspectRatio / alt). Rendered as an `<OrigamAvatar>` overlay.
     */
    image?: string | ISrcObject
    /**
     * Accessible label used as `aria-label`. When omitted, the
     * component falls back to `"QR code for {value}"`.
     */
    ariaLabel?: string
}

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Text or URL encoded into the QR matrix. UTF-8 is supported via the underlying qrcode-generator Byte mode.

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Optional heading rendered above the QR matrix inside the wrapper.

props.name: qrCodeProps
props.description_fallback: Per-matrix overrides (color, bgColor, rounded) applied to the SVG internals, not the wrapper.

props.name: errorCorrectionLevel
props.description_fallback: Reed-Solomon redundancy level (L | M | Q | H). Higher levels tolerate more damage at the cost of density. Default "M".

props.name: quietZone
props.description_fallback: Number of modules reserved as quiet-zone padding. ISO spec mandates >=4. Default 4.

props.name: icon
props.description_fallback: Optional centred MDI icon overlay rendered on top of the matrix via <OrigamIcon>.

props.name: image
props.description_fallback: Optional centred image overlay. Accepts a raw URL or an ISrcObject shape. Rendered as an <OrigamAvatar> overlay.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description_fallback: Accessible aria-label. Falls back to "QR code for {value}" when omitted.

examples.title_fallback: QR code with high error correction and icon overlay
examples.code:
<origam-qr-code
    value="https://origam.dev"
    title="Scan to visit"
    error-correction-level="H"
    :quiet-zone="6"
    icon="mdi-origam"
    size="200"
    rounded="md"
/>

entry.name: IQrCodeSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slots contract for <OrigamQrCode>. The center slot replaces the default icon/image overlay. When provided, both icon and image props are ignored.

props.name: center
props.description_fallback: Replaces the default icon or image overlay. Receives the resolved size of the central reserved square in module units so the consumer can paint a custom SVG or icon without re-deriving the geometry.

examples.title_fallback: Custom center overlay with brand logo
examples.code:
<origam-qr-code value="https://origam.dev" error-correction-level="H">
    <template #center="{ size }">
        <image
            :width="size"
            :height="size"
            :x="-size / 2"
            :y="-size / 2"
            href="/logo.svg"
        />
    </template>
</origam-qr-code>

entry.definition:

entry.description_fallback: Emit signature for <OrigamRatingFieldItem>. Extends IClickEmits with mouseenter/mouseleave so the parent RatingField can drive the half-rating hover preview from pointer movement over individual star items.

    :value="3"

entry.name: IRatingFieldItemProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for a single star/icon cell inside <OrigamRatingField>. Declares the item-level state (value, filled, hovered, checked) alongside the full visual surface (color, size, density, rounded, border, elevation, padding, margin).
entry.definition:
export interface IRatingFieldItemProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, IColorProps, IDensityProps, IRippleProps, ISizeProps, IBorderProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps {
    name?: string
    index?: number
    value: number
    label?: string
    itemAriaLabel?: string
    showStar?: boolean
    isFilled?: boolean
    isHovered?: boolean
    isHovering?: boolean
    disabled?: boolean
    readonly?: boolean
    fullIcon?: TIcon
    emptyIcon?: TIcon
    halfIncrements?: boolean
    checked?: boolean
    length?: number | string
}

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: HTML name attribute forwarded to the underlying radio input — groups items in the same rating field.

props.name: index
props.description_fallback: Zero-based position of this item in the rating row — used by the parent for keyboard navigation.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Numeric rating value this item represents (1-based by convention). Required.

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Accessible label for this star position — rendered as aria-label or a visually-hidden string.

props.name: showStar
props.description_fallback: Whether to render the icon. When false the item occupies space but shows no icon (used for spacer items in half-increment mode).

props.name: isFilled
props.description_fallback: True when the item value is at or below the current model value — paints the filled icon variant.

props.name: isHovered
props.description_fallback: True when the pointer is hovering over this specific item, enabling per-item hover styling.

props.name: isHovering
props.description_fallback: True when any item in the row is being hovered — used to dim non-hovered items.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Prevents interaction and applies the disabled visual state.

props.name: readonly
props.description_fallback: Makes the item non-interactive but keeps its normal visual appearance (no disabled dimming).

props.name: fullIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon displayed when the item is filled. Defaults to the parent RatingField fullIcon.

props.name: emptyIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon displayed when the item is empty. Defaults to the parent RatingField emptyIcon.

props.name: halfIncrements
props.description_fallback: Enables half-step selection — two invisible inputs occupy each star cell.

props.name: checked
props.description_fallback: Whether the underlying radio input is in the checked state.

props.name: length
props.description_fallback: Total number of items in the row — forwarded for accessibility labels.

examples.title_fallback: Rendering a single rating item
examples.code:
<OrigamRatingFieldItem
    :value="3"
    :is-filled="true"
    full-icon="mdi-star"
    empty-icon="mdi-star-outline"
/>

entry.name: IRatingFieldProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamRatingField>. Extends the input contract (IInputProps), ripple effect (IRippleProps), tag polymorphism (ITagProps), and label (ILabelProps) with rating-specific props such as icon customisation, half-increments, hover preview, and per-item aria labels.
entry.definition:
export interface IRatingFieldProps extends IInputProps, IRippleProps, ITagProps, ILabelProps {
    name?: string
    itemAriaLabel?: string
    clearable?: boolean
    disabled?: boolean
    emptyIcon?: TIcon
    fullIcon?: TIcon
    halfIncrements?: boolean
    hover?: boolean
    length?: number | string
    readonly?: boolean
    modelValue?: number | string
    itemLabels?: Array<string>
    itemLabelPosition?: TBlock
}

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: HTML name for the underlying radio inputs — important for form submission and label association.

props.name: itemAriaLabel
props.description_fallback: Aria-label template applied to each star item. Supports interpolation tokens for value and length.

props.name: clearable
props.description_fallback: Clicking the currently selected item clears the rating back to 0 / undefined.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables all interactions and applies the disabled visual state.

props.name: emptyIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon rendered for unselected positions. Defaults to the DS star-outline token.

props.name: fullIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon rendered for selected positions. Defaults to the DS star token.

props.name: halfIncrements
props.description_fallback: Allows 0.5 step selection — each star cell gets two invisible half-width inputs.

props.name: hover
props.description_fallback: Shows a hover preview: hovering over an item temporarily lights all items up to that position.

props.name: length
props.description_fallback: Total number of star items to render. Accepts a number or a string coerced to number. Default 5.

props.name: readonly
props.description_fallback: Makes the field non-interactive while keeping normal visual appearance.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: The current rating value bound with v-model.

props.name: itemLabels
props.description_fallback: Array of per-item labels shown above or below each star. Length should match the `length` prop.

props.name: itemLabelPosition
props.description_fallback: Vertical placement of the per-item labels relative to the star row.

examples.title_fallback: Basic star rating with half-increments
examples.code:
<OrigamRatingField
    v-model="score"
    :length="5"
    :half-increments="true"
    :hover="true"
    full-icon="mdi-star"
    empty-icon="mdi-star-outline"
/>

entry.name: IResizeState
entry.description_fallback: Return shape of the `useResizeObserver` composable — a ref to the observed element and a deep-readonly ref to its most recent `DOMRectReadOnly` measurements.

props.name: resizeRef
props.description_fallback: Attach this ref to the element you want to observe. The ResizeObserver starts watching as soon as the ref is populated and stops when it becomes null/undefined.

props.name: contentRect
props.description_fallback: Reactive snapshot of the last measured DOMRectReadOnly. Undefined before the first measurement or when the element is detached. Deep-readonly to prevent accidental mutation.

examples.title_fallback: Reading dimensions from useResizeObserver

entry.name: IResolvedCalendarOptions
entry.description_fallback: Resolved, fully-defaulted option object produced by the useCalendar composable after normalising ICalendarComponentProps. Every field is non-optional at this layer — the composable guarantees a concrete value for each, so downstream rendering logic never has to guard against undefined.
entry.definition:
export interface IResolvedCalendarOptions {
    view: TCalendarView
    currentDate: Date
    events: Array<IEvent>
    firstDayOfWeek: number
    timeFormat: TCalendarTimeFormat
    locale: string
    selectable: boolean
    eventColorKey: string
    categoryColors: Record<string, TIntent | string>
    weekendHighlight: boolean
    showWeekNumbers: boolean
    dayStartHour: number
    dayEndHour: number
    slotDuration: number
    minDate: Date | null
    maxDate: Date | null
    disabledDatesPredicate: (date: Date) => boolean
}

props.name: view
props.description_fallback: Active view mode — month, week, day, or agenda.

props.name: currentDate
props.description_fallback: Anchor date driving the currently rendered period.

props.name: events
props.description_fallback: Expanded event list; recurring events have been unwrapped into concrete occurrences.

props.name: firstDayOfWeek
props.description_fallback: Weekday index (0=Sunday, 1=Monday) for the leftmost column header.

props.name: timeFormat
props.description_fallback: Clock display format — "12h" or "24h".

props.name: locale
props.description_fallback: BCP 47 locale tag used for weekday and month name rendering.

props.name: selectable
props.description_fallback: Whether drag-select on the grid is enabled.

props.name: eventColorKey
props.description_fallback: Property name read from IEvent for colour resolution ("category" or "color").

props.name: categoryColors
props.description_fallback: Map from category string to intent token or raw CSS colour.

props.name: weekendHighlight
props.description_fallback: Whether Saturday and Sunday receive a tinted background in month/week views.

props.name: showWeekNumbers
props.description_fallback: Whether an ISO week-number column is prepended in week/month views.

props.name: dayStartHour
props.description_fallback: First hour rendered in the timeline for week and day views.

props.name: dayEndHour
props.description_fallback: Last hour rendered in the timeline for week and day views.

props.name: slotDuration
props.description_fallback: Minutes per timeline row — 15, 30, or 60.

props.name: minDate
props.description_fallback: Lower navigation bound; null means no lower limit.

props.name: maxDate
props.description_fallback: Upper navigation bound; null means no upper limit.

props.name: disabledDatesPredicate
props.description_fallback: Compiled predicate derived from the disabledDates prop — always a function at this layer.

examples.title_fallback: Consuming resolved options in a custom calendar composable

entry.name: IResolvedMaskConfig
entry.description_fallback: Normalised mask configuration returned by resolveMaskConfig after accepting a raw string pattern, a preset key, or an IMaskOptions object as input. At this layer the pattern is always a raw template string and every field is guaranteed non-optional.

props.name: pattern
props.description_fallback: Raw token-walker template string. # = digit, A = letter, * = any char, other characters are literals.

props.name: validator
props.description_fallback: Optional secondary validation function applied to the unmasked value. null when no validator was provided.

props.name: required
props.description_fallback: When true, isValid returns false until every consumer token in the pattern is filled.

examples.title_fallback: Consuming a resolved mask config in useMask

entry.name: IResponsiveProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamResponsive>. Merges dimension, commons, padding, margin, border, and rounded surfaces with three responsive-specific props: aspectRatio (locks the intrinsic ratio), contentClass (targets the inner wrapper), and inline (switches the outer element between block and inline-block).

props.name: aspectRatio
props.description_fallback: Intrinsic aspect-ratio expressed as a number (16/9 = 1.777) or a CSS fraction string ("16/9"). Applied via the CSS aspect-ratio property on the wrapper.

props.name: contentClass
props.description_fallback: Additional CSS class applied to the inner content wrapper, not the outer root element. Useful to style the content independently from the outer responsive shell.

props.name: inline
props.description_fallback: Renders the outer element as inline-block instead of block, allowing the component to flow alongside inline content.

examples.title_fallback: Locking a video embed to 16:9 with IResponsiveProps
examples.code:
<OrigamResponsive aspect-ratio="16/9" width="100%">
    <iframe src="https://youtube.com/embed/xxx" />
</OrigamResponsive>

entry.name: IRichToolbarEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit signature for the internal <OrigamRichToolbar> sub-component of OrigamTextareaField. Surfaces a single "format" event carrying the command id and an optional value (URL for link insertion) so the textarea can apply the formatting to its content model.

examples.title_fallback: Handling the format event in a custom toolbar
examples.code:
<OrigamRichToolbar
    :items="toolbarItems"
    :active="activeState"

entry.name: IRichToolbarProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the internal <OrigamRichToolbar> sub-component of OrigamTextareaField. Kept package-private so the API can evolve without breaking external consumers. Exposes the command list, current active-format state, optional position, and disabled flag.

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Ordered list of toolbar command identifiers to render as buttons. Passed down from the parent TextareaField.

props.name: active
props.description_fallback: Map of currently active formatting states (bold, italic, link, …) — drives the pressed/active visual on each button.

props.name: position
props.description_fallback: Vertical placement of the toolbar relative to the textarea — "top" or "bottom". Default "top".

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables all toolbar buttons when the parent TextareaField is disabled.

examples.title_fallback: Rendering the rich toolbar with a custom command set
examples.code:
<OrigamRichToolbar
    :items="['bold', 'italic', 'link']"

entry.name: IRippleDirectiveBinding
entry.description_fallback: Typed directive binding for `v-ripple` — extends Vue's DirectiveBinding with a narrowed `value` (boolean or `{ class }` object) and typed `modifiers` for center, circle and stop behaviours.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Binding value. `true` enables the ripple with defaults; `false` disables it; an object with a `class` string appends that class to every ripple element.

props.name: modifiers
props.description_fallback: Directive modifiers: `center` anchors the wave origin to the element centre; `circle` forces a perfectly circular wave; `stop` prevents event propagation when the ripple triggers.

examples.title_fallback: Accessing the typed binding in a directive hook

entry.name: IRippleElement
entry.description_fallback: Individual ripple DOM element — extends Element with an optional `dataset` typed as `IRippleElementDataset`, giving typed access to the `isHiding` and `activated` data attributes written by the ripple animation engine.

props.name: dataset
props.description_fallback: Typed dataset providing access to `isHiding` and `activated` flags written by the ripple animation lifecycle.

examples.title_fallback: Checking ripple animation state

entry.name: IRippleElementDataset
entry.description_fallback: Typed `dataset` shape on a ripple wave element — two string flags written by the animation engine to track whether the wave is currently fading out and whether it has been activated.

props.name: isHiding
props.description_fallback: Set to "true" when the ripple wave is in its fade-out phase. Used to coordinate removal from the DOM after the animation completes.

props.name: activated
props.description_fallback: ISO timestamp string written when the wave is first activated. Used to compute animation timing relative to the pointer event.

examples.title_fallback: Reading dataset flags from a ripple wave element

entry.name: IRippleHtmlElement
entry.description_fallback: Augmented HTMLElement that carries the `_ripple` private state bag written by `v-ripple`. All fields are optional so the interface can be safely cast from any HTMLElement and read without null-guard noise.

props.name: _ripple
props.description_fallback: Private state bag attached to the host element by the v-ripple directive. Holds configuration (enabled, centered, circle, class) and animation bookkeeping (touched, isTouch, showTimer, showTimerCommit).

examples.title_fallback: Checking ripple state on an element

entry.name: IRippleHtmlElementRipple
entry.description_fallback: Internal state bag stored on a DOM element's _ripple property by the v-ripple directive. Tracks whether the ripple is enabled, centered, circular, and manages the show-timer commit callback.
entry.definition:
export interface IRippleHtmlElementRipple {
    enabled?: boolean
    centered?: boolean
    circle?: boolean
    class?: string
    touched?: boolean
    isTouch?: boolean
    showTimer?: number
    showTimerCommit?: null | (() => void)
}

props.name: enabled
props.description_fallback: Whether the ripple effect is active on this element.

props.name: centered
props.description_fallback: When true, the ripple always starts from the centre of the element rather than from the pointer position.

props.name: circle
props.description_fallback: When true, the ripple is forced to a perfect circle (useful for icon buttons).

props.name: class
props.description_fallback: Extra CSS class added to each ripple wave element.

props.name: touched
props.description_fallback: Marks that the last interaction was a touch event.

props.name: isTouch
props.description_fallback: Whether the current device has active touch support.

props.name: showTimer
props.description_fallback: Timer ID used to delay the ripple show on touch to distinguish taps from scrolls.

props.name: showTimerCommit
props.description_fallback: Callback queued by showTimer — invoked to actually paint the ripple, or cleared on cancel.

examples.title_fallback: Accessing the internal ripple state on an element

entry.name: IRippleOptions
entry.description_fallback: Options passed to the v-ripple directive when an object value is used. Controls the visual behaviour of the ripple wave: extra class, centred start, and forced circular shape.

props.name: class
props.description_fallback: Extra CSS class appended to every ripple wave element for custom styling.

props.name: center
props.description_fallback: When true, the ripple always starts from the centre of the host element.

props.name: circle
props.description_fallback: When true, constrains the ripple to a perfect circle regardless of the element dimensions.

examples.title_fallback: Using v-ripple with options
examples.code:
<OrigamBtn v-ripple="{ center: true, circle: true, class: 'my-ripple' }">
    Click me
</OrigamBtn>

entry.name: IRippleProps
entry.description_fallback: Component prop surface for the ripple effect. Extended by interactive components (Btn, Chip, Card, ListItem…) to opt in to the v-ripple ink-wave animation on pointer interaction.

props.name: ripple
props.description_fallback: Enable the ripple ink-wave. Pass true/false to toggle, or an object with a class key to attach a custom CSS class to the wave element.

examples.title_fallback: Extending IRippleProps on a custom component

entry.name: IRoundedProps
entry.description_fallback: Corner-radius contract for every component whose surface can be rounded. Supports named token rungs (small/default/large/…), a boolean shorthand for the default token, or raw CSS border-radius values.
entry.definition:
export interface IRoundedProps {
    /**
     * Corner-radius selector. Three modes:
     *  - **named variant** (`'small' | 'default' | 'large' | …`,
     *    cf. `ROUNDED` enum) → emits `--rounded-{name}` class so the
     *    component's SCSS can pick the right token rung.
     *  - **boolean `true`** (or empty string `''`) → emits the legacy
     *    `--rounded` class for components that only have a single rounded
     *    state (e.g. `OrigamBtn` defaulting to `radius.2xl`).
     *  - **CSS value** (`'4px'`, `'4px 0 4px 0'`, `100`) → emits inline
     *    `border-radius` declarations via `useRounded.roundedStyles`.
     */
    rounded?: boolean | number | string | TRounded | null | undefined
    roundedTopRight?: boolean | number | string
    roundedTopLeft?: boolean | number | string
    roundedBottomLeft?: boolean | number | string
    roundedBottomRight?: boolean | number | string
}

props.name: rounded
props.type_label: boolean | number | string | TRounded | null | undefined
props.description_fallback: Corner-radius selector: a TRounded named rung emits --rounded-{name} class; boolean true (or empty string) emits --rounded; a number or CSS string is applied as a raw border-radius via inline style.

props.name: roundedTopRight
props.description_fallback: Applies rounding only to the top-right corner. Accepts the same shapes as rounded.

props.name: roundedTopLeft
props.description_fallback: Applies rounding only to the top-left corner. Accepts the same shapes as rounded.

props.name: roundedBottomLeft
props.description_fallback: Applies rounding only to the bottom-left corner. Accepts the same shapes as rounded.

props.name: roundedBottomRight
props.description_fallback: Applies rounding only to the bottom-right corner. Accepts the same shapes as rounded.

examples.title_fallback: Extending IRoundedProps and consuming rounded styles

entry.name: IRowProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamRow>, the grid row container. Extends commons, tag, padding, margin, border, color, density, align, and justify surfaces with two row-specific props: gutters (column/row gap shorthand) and direction (flex-direction override).

props.name: gutters
props.description_fallback: Uniform gap between columns and rows. Accepts a CSS length string or a spacing-scale number (converted to px). Overrides default grid-gap.

props.name: direction
props.description_fallback: Flex-direction of the row — "row" (default), "row-reverse", "column", or "column-reverse".

examples.title_fallback: Building a responsive two-column layout with OrigamRow
examples.code:
<OrigamRow :gutters="16" align="center" justify="space-between">
    <OrigamCol :cols="6">Left</OrigamCol>
    <OrigamCol :cols="6">Right</OrigamCol>
</OrigamRow>

entry.name: IRtlInstance
entry.description_fallback: Runtime RTL context returned by the locale composable. Exposes whether the current locale renders right-to-left and the per-locale RTL override map.

props.name: isRtl
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag — true when the active locale is right-to-left.

props.name: rtl
props.description_fallback: Map of locale codes to their RTL status, merged from IRtlOptions at install time.

examples.title_fallback: Reading the RTL flag from the locale composable

entry.name: IRtlOptions
entry.description_fallback: Install-time RTL configuration merged into the locale options of createOrigam(). Maps locale codes to a boolean indicating whether that locale is right-to-left.

props.name: rtl
props.description_fallback: Map of locale code → is-RTL, e.g. { ar: true, he: true }. Merged with built-in defaults at plugin install time.

examples.title_fallback: Passing RTL options to createOrigam

entry.name: IRtlProps
entry.description_fallback: Per-instance RTL override prop. Extended by container components that need to force a right-to-left text direction independently of the global locale setting.

props.name: rtl
props.description_fallback: When true, forces the component subtree into right-to-left layout regardless of the current locale.

examples.title_fallback: Forcing RTL on a specific component
examples.code:
<OrigamCard :rtl="true">
    <!-- Content will render right-to-left -->
</OrigamCard>

entry.name: ISample
entry.description_fallback: Internal data-point shape used by the velocity composable. A sample records a timestamp (t) and a distance (d) to compute pointer velocity for inertia-scroll calculations.

props.name: t
props.description_fallback: Timestamp of the sample in milliseconds (typically performance.now()).

props.name: d
props.description_fallback: Distance covered (in pixels) since the previous sample — used to derive instantaneous velocity.

examples.title_fallback: ISample in the velocity composable context

entry.name: IScrimProps
entry.description_fallback: Minimal prop mixin that adds a backdrop/scrim control to overlay components. A boolean true shows the default semi-transparent backdrop; a string value is treated as a CSS color override for the scrim surface.

props.name: scrim
props.description_fallback: Controls the backdrop behind the overlay. Pass true for the default semi-transparent scrim, false/undefined to disable, or a CSS color string to override the scrim color.

examples.title_fallback: Extending IScrimProps on a custom overlay

entry.name: IScrollArguments
entry.description_fallback: Arguments bag passed into scroll-behaviour handler functions. Provides a reactive ref indicating whether the target element can currently scroll, allowing behaviours to guard against no-op updates.

props.name: canScroll
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag set to true when the scroll target has scrollable overflow. Behaviours should read this before mutating layout.

examples.title_fallback: Using IScrollArguments in a custom scroll behaviour

entry.name: IScrollProps
entry.description_fallback: Scroll-aware behaviour props for AppBar and similar layout components. Declares the scroll target, threshold and a space-separated list of behaviours (hide, collapse, elevate, fade-image, inverted).
entry.definition:
export interface IScrollProps {
    /**
     * Space-separated list of scroll behaviours, e.g. `"hide inverted"` or
     * `"elevate active"`. `inverted` is a MODIFIER that flips the trigger of
     * `hide` / `collapse` / `elevate` and does nothing on its own. The
     * `(string & {})` member keeps single-token autocomplete while allowing
     * multi-token combinations.
     */
    scrollBehavior?: TScrollBehavior | (string & {})
    scrollTarget?: string
    scrollThreshold?: string | number
}

props.name: scrollBehavior
props.description_fallback: Space-separated list of scroll behaviours, e.g. "hide inverted" or "elevate active". The inverted modifier flips the trigger direction for hide / collapse / elevate and does nothing on its own.

props.name: scrollTarget
props.description_fallback: CSS selector or element ID of the scroll container to observe. Defaults to the nearest scrollable ancestor when omitted.

props.name: scrollThreshold
props.description_fallback: Scroll distance (in pixels) the target must travel before the behaviour activates. Accepts a number or a CSS length string.

examples.title_fallback: AppBar hiding on scroll with a threshold
examples.code:
<OrigamAppBar
    scroll-behavior="hide inverted"
    scroll-target="#main-content"
    :scroll-threshold="100"
/>

entry.name: IScrollStrategyData
entry.description_fallback: Runtime data bag passed to every TScrollStrategyFn. Gives the strategy access to the overlay root element, content element, positioning target, active state flag and the update-location callback to recompute floating position.
entry.definition:
export interface IScrollStrategyData {
    root: Ref<HTMLElement | undefined>
    contentEl: Ref<HTMLElement | undefined>
    targetEl: Ref<HTMLElement | undefined>
    isActive: Ref<boolean>
    updateLocation: Ref<((e: Event) => void) | undefined>
}

props.name: root
props.description_fallback: The overlay root element whose scroll listeners are managed by the strategy.

props.name: contentEl
props.description_fallback: The floating content element (tooltip bubble, menu panel, …).

props.name: targetEl
props.description_fallback: The anchor / activator element that the floating panel is positioned relative to.

props.name: isActive
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag controlling overlay visibility. Strategies may set this to false to close the overlay on scroll.

props.name: updateLocation
props.description_fallback: Callback provided by the positioning engine. The strategy calls it on scroll events to recompute the floating panel position.

examples.title_fallback: Writing a custom TScrollStrategyFn

entry.name: IScrollStrategyProps
entry.description_fallback: Overlay scroll-strategy props. Extended by Overlay, Tooltip and related components to let consumers choose how the floating panel reacts when the page or its ancestor container scrolls.

props.name: scrollStrategy
props.description_fallback: Named strategy (block, close, none, reposition) or a custom TScrollStrategyFn callback that receives IScrollStrategyData.

props.name: contained
props.description_fallback: When true, the overlay is positioned inside its scroll container rather than the document root, so the positioning engine observes the container scroll instead.

examples.title_fallback: Configuring scroll strategy on a Tooltip
examples.code:
<OrigamTooltip scroll-strategy="close" :contained="true">
    <template #activator="{ props }">
        <OrigamBtn v-bind="props">Hover me</OrigamBtn>
    </template>
    Tooltip content
</OrigamTooltip>

entry.name: ISelectEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit signature for <OrigamSelect>. Extends the commons, focus, adjacent, and adjacent-inner emit contracts with three select-specific events: click:control (pointer click on the control surface), mousedown:control (mousedown before the dropdown opens), and update:menu (v-model:menu synchronisation).

examples.title_fallback: Tracking menu open/close state
examples.code:
<OrigamSelect
    v-model="selected"
    v-model:menu="isOpen"
    @click:control="onControlClick"
    @update:menu="onMenuChange"
/>

entry.name: ISelectProps
entry.description_fallback: Full prop surface for <OrigamSelect>. Aggregates the text-field base, density, adjacent slots, field chrome, input contract, spacing, border, rounded, elevation, item list configuration, transitions, filters, and lazy loading, then adds select-specific behaviour: chips, multiple selection, menu control, autocomplete, and search.
entry.definition:
export interface ISelectProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, ITextFieldProps, IDensityProps, IAdjacentProps, IAdjacentInnerProps, IFieldProps, IInputProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IBorderProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IItemProps, ITransitionComponentProps, IFiltersProps, ILazyProps {
    chips?: boolean
    closableChips?: boolean
    hideNoData?: boolean
    hideSelected?: boolean
    listProps?: IListProps
    menu?: boolean
    menuIcon?: TIcon
    menuProps?: IMenuProps
    chipProps?: IChipProps
    multiple?: boolean
    noDataText?: string
    openOnClear?: boolean

    autocomplete?: boolean
    autoSelectFirst?: boolean | string
    clearOnSelect?: boolean
    divider?: string
    search?: string
    closeText?: string
    openText?: string
}

props.name: chips
props.description_fallback: Renders selected values as chip tags inside the control instead of plain text.

props.name: closableChips
props.description_fallback: Adds a close/remove button to each selection chip. Requires chips=true.

props.name: hideNoData
props.description_fallback: Hides the "no data" message in the dropdown when the search returns no results.

props.name: hideSelected
props.description_fallback: Removes already-selected items from the dropdown list (useful for multiple selects).

props.name: listProps
props.description_fallback: Props forwarded to the internal OrigamList rendered inside the dropdown.

props.name: menu
props.description_fallback: v-model:menu — controls the open/closed state of the dropdown programmatically.

props.name: menuIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon shown in the append-inner slot to indicate the dropdown is available. Defaults to mdi-chevron-down.

props.name: menuProps
props.description_fallback: Props forwarded to the internal OrigamMenu (position, max-height, transition, etc.).

props.name: chipProps
props.description_fallback: Props forwarded to every selection chip (color, size, variant…).

props.name: multiple
props.description_fallback: Allows selecting more than one item. modelValue becomes an array.

props.name: noDataText
props.description_fallback: Text displayed when the dropdown list is empty or the search yields no results.

props.name: openOnClear
props.description_fallback: Re-opens the dropdown after the user clears the selection via the clear icon.

props.name: autocomplete
props.description_fallback: Enables text-input filtering — the user types to narrow the dropdown list.

props.name: autoSelectFirst
props.description_fallback: Auto-highlights the first matching item while the user types. Pass "exact" to restrict to exact-match prefix.

props.name: clearOnSelect
props.description_fallback: Clears the search input after an item is selected (useful for multiple autocomplete).

props.name: divider
props.description_fallback: Character used to separate multi-select values in the display string.

props.name: search
props.description_fallback: v-model:search — exposes the autocomplete search string for external control.

props.name: closeText
props.description_fallback: Accessible label for the close/collapse action on the dropdown.

props.name: openText
props.description_fallback: Accessible label for the open/expand action on the dropdown.

examples.title_fallback: Multi-select with chips and autocomplete
examples.code:
<OrigamSelect
    v-model="selected"
    :items="options"
    :multiple="true"
    :chips="true"
    :closable-chips="true"
    :autocomplete="true"
    label="Select frameworks"
/>

entry.name: ISelectionControlEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit signature for <OrigamSelectionControl> (the base for Checkbox, Switch, and Radio). Extends ICommonsComponentEmits with a single click:label event fired when the user clicks the label text rather than the control itself.

examples.title_fallback: Intercepting label clicks on a selection control
examples.code:
<OrigamCheckbox
    v-model="accepted"
    label="I agree to the terms"
    @click:label="onLabelClick"

entry.name: ISelectionControlGroupEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit signature for <OrigamSelectionControlGroup>. Extends ICommonsComponentEmits, inheriting the standard v-model (update:modelValue) and lifecycle emits. No group-specific events are declared beyond the commons contract.
entry.definition:
export interface ISelectionControlGroupEmits extends ICommonsComponentEmits {

}

examples.title_fallback: Binding v-model on a selection control group
examples.code:
<OrigamSelectionControlGroup
    v-model="selectedValues"
    :items="options"
    type="checkbox"
/>

entry.name: ISelectionControlGroupProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamSelectionControlGroup>, the container that manages a group of checkboxes, radios, or switch controls. Extends commons, color, density, and ripple surfaces with group-specific props: input type, inline layout, icons, multiple selection, name grouping, readonly/disabled state, and a custom value comparator.
entry.definition:
export interface ISelectionControlGroupProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IDensityProps, IRippleProps {
    disabled?: boolean
    /** Error state. Accepts `boolean` or a `string` error message (same contract as `IValidationProps`). */
    error?: string | boolean
    inline?: boolean
    falseIcon?: TIcon
    trueIcon?: TIcon
    multiple?: boolean
    name?: string
    readonly?: boolean
    modelValue?: any
    type?: string
    valueComparator?: (a: any, b: any) => boolean
    items?: Array<any> | Record<string, any>
}

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables all controls in the group.

props.name: error
props.description_fallback: Puts all controls in the error visual state.

props.name: inline
props.description_fallback: Lays out controls horizontally instead of vertically.

props.name: falseIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon used for the unchecked/false state of each control.

props.name: trueIcon
props.description_fallback: Icon used for the checked/true state of each control.

props.name: multiple
props.description_fallback: Allows multiple items to be selected simultaneously. modelValue becomes an array.

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: Shared HTML name attribute applied to all child inputs — groups them for native form submission.

props.name: readonly
props.description_fallback: Makes all controls non-interactive while keeping normal visual appearance.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: The current value(s) bound with v-model. A single value for single-select, an array for multiple.

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: HTML input type forwarded to each child control — "checkbox", "radio", or "switch".

props.name: valueComparator
props.description_fallback: Custom equality function used to match modelValue against item values. Defaults to strict equality.

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Data source for generating child controls. Accepts an array of values or a key/value record.

examples.title_fallback: Generating a checkbox group from an items array
examples.code:
<OrigamSelectionControlGroup
    v-model="selected"
    :items="['Vue', 'React', 'Angular']"
    type="checkbox"
    :inline="true"
/>

entry.name: ISelectionControlGroupSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot signatures for <OrigamSelectionControlGroup>. Exposes a default slot for fully custom rendering of all items, an item slot for per-item customisation, and an open index signature to allow arbitrary named slots to be forwarded from parent components.

props.name: default
props.description_fallback: Replaces the entire item list rendering. Receives the full items array/record and is responsible for rendering all controls.

props.name: item
props.description_fallback: Renders a single item in the group. Receives the item value and its index. Use this to customise individual control appearance without replacing the whole list.

examples.title_fallback: Custom item rendering in a selection group
examples.code:
<OrigamSelectionControlGroup :items="options" type="checkbox">
    <template #item="{ item, index }">
        <OrigamCheckbox :key="index" :label="item.label" :value="item.value" />
    </template>
</OrigamSelectionControlGroup>

entry.name: ISelectionControlProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamSelectionControl>, the base component shared by Checkbox, Switch, and Radio. Extends commons, a partial of the group props (excluding items), color, bgColor, active/hover state, and density, then adds individual control-level bindings: label, trueValue, falseValue, value, and required.

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Text label rendered beside the control.

props.name: trueValue
props.description_fallback: The value emitted when the control is checked/on. Defaults to true.

props.name: falseValue
props.description_fallback: The value emitted when the control is unchecked/off. Defaults to false.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: The value contributed to a group modelValue when this control is checked (array mode).

props.name: required
props.description_fallback: Marks the input as required for native form validation.

    v-model="permission"

entry.name: ISelectionControlSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot signatures for <OrigamSelectionControl>. Three slots give full control over the rendering: default replaces the entire control surface, label replaces the label text, and input replaces the underlying native input element.

props.name: default
props.type_label: (data: { model: any, color?: TColor, bgColor?: TColor, icon?: TIcon, props: any }) => any
props.description_fallback: Replaces the entire control surface. Receives the current model value, the active icon, and the internal props object.

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Replaces the label text. Useful for rich label content (icons, links, formatted text).

props.name: input
props.type_label: (data: { model: any, color?: TColor, bgColor?: TColor, icon?: TIcon, props: any, textColorStyles?: any, backgroundColorStyles?: any }) => any
props.description_fallback: Replaces the native input element while keeping the surrounding label/ripple scaffolding. Receives internal props, the current icon, and the model ref.

examples.title_fallback: Custom label with a link inside a checkbox

entry.name: ISelectionGroupContext
entry.description_fallback: Provide/inject context passed from SelectionControlGroup to each child SelectionControl. Exposes the shared reactive modelValue, a forceUpdate trigger for when the parent needs to re-evaluate all children, and an onForceUpdate registration hook for children to subscribe.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: Shared reactive ref holding the current group selection. Children read and write through this ref; the group synchronises changes to the parent v-model.

props.name: forceUpdate
props.description_fallback: Called by the group when an external change requires all children to re-check their checked state (e.g. programmatic clear or reset).

props.name: onForceUpdate
props.description_fallback: Registration function used by each child to subscribe a callback that runs when forceUpdate is called.

entry.name: ISheetEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit signature for <OrigamSheet>. Two events drive the v-model surface: update:snap fires whenever a gesture or programmatic snapTo() call settles on a new snap-point id, and update:open fires on the closed/non-closed boundary so the parent stays in sync when the user dismisses via swipe-down.

examples.title_fallback: Binding v-model on a bottom sheet
examples.code:
<OrigamSheet
    v-model:open="sheetOpen"
    v-model:snap="currentSnap"
    :snaps="['peek', 'half', 'full']"
    @update:snap="onSnapChange"
/>

entry.name: ISheetProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamSheet> — a bottom/side sheet with optional swipe-to-expand gesture, discrete snap-points, and the full design surface (color, border, spacing, elevation, position).
entry.definition:
export interface ISheetProps extends ITagProps, ICommonsComponentProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IBorderProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IDimensionProps, ILocationProps, IPositionProps, IActiveProps, IHoverProps {
    /**
     * Edge the sheet is anchored to. Drives the bottom-swipe gesture
     * (only `'bottom'` activates the touch-drag handle today; the other
     * sides reserve the prop for future "side-sheet" patterns).
     *
     * Defaults to `undefined` so existing consumers (Picker, Dialog,
     * ColorPickerField, PasswordField) keep their pre-Phase-3 layout.
     */
    side?: TDirectionBoth

    /**
     * Enable the mobile-style swipe-to-expand / swipe-to-dismiss
     * gesture. When true (and `side === 'bottom'`), the component
     * renders a drag handle and binds `useSheetSwipe`.
     *
     * False by default — every existing consumer of `OrigamSheet`
     * (notably `OrigamDialog`) inherits a no-op gesture layer.
     */
    swipeable?: boolean

    /**
     * Discrete snap-points the sheet can settle on between drags. The
     * height value is either a number (interpreted as px) or a CSS
     * length string (`'120px'`, `'50vh'`, `'90%'`).
     *
     * Defaults to closed/peek/half/full at 0 / 120px / 50vh / 90vh.
     */
    snapPoints?: ReadonlyArray<TSheetSnapPoint>

    /** Initial snap id when the sheet mounts. Defaults to `'half'`. */
    defaultSnap?: TSheetSnapId

    /**
     * v-model:open binding — `false` ⇄ `closed` snap, anything truthy
     * keeps the last non-closed snap (or `defaultSnap` on first mount).
     */
    open?: boolean

    /**
     * Disables the swipe gesture. The sheet stays at `defaultSnap` and
     * pointer events on the handle no-op.
     */
    disabled?: boolean

    /**
     * Persistent sheets cannot be swiped past `closed` — a fast
     * downward flick instead snaps to the next non-zero point (`peek`
     * by default). Mirrors `OrigamDialog`'s `persistent` semantics.
     */
    persistent?: boolean
}

props.name: side
props.description_fallback: Edge the sheet is anchored to. Only bottom activates the swipe-to-expand gesture today; other values are reserved for future side-sheet patterns.

props.name: swipeable
props.description_fallback: Enable the mobile-style swipe-to-expand / swipe-to-dismiss gesture. Requires side === "bottom".

props.name: snapPoints
props.description_fallback: Discrete snap-points the sheet can settle on between drags. Each point is a px number or a CSS length string.

props.name: defaultSnap
props.description_fallback: Initial snap id when the sheet mounts. Defaults to "half".

props.name: open
props.description_fallback: v-model:open binding — false maps to the closed snap, truthy keeps the last non-closed snap.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables the swipe gesture entirely. The sheet stays at defaultSnap and pointer events on the handle are no-ops.

props.name: persistent
props.description_fallback: Persistent sheets cannot be swiped past closed — a fast downward flick snaps back instead of dismissing.

examples.title_fallback: Bottom sheet with swipe gesture
examples.code:
<OrigamSheet
    side="bottom"
    swipeable
    :snap-points="['120px', '50vh', '90vh']"
    default-snap="half"
    v-model:open="isOpen"
/>

entry.name: ISheetSwipeOptions
entry.description_fallback: Options bag passed to useSheetSwipe() — the bottom-sheet drag gesture composable. Wires reactive refs for the sheet element, the drag handle, snap-points, and behavioural flags.

props.name: el
props.description_fallback: The sheet root element — used to read its current rendered height.

props.name: handle
props.description_fallback: The drag-handle element — pointer events bind to this only, keeping the sheet body interactive.

props.name: snapPoints
props.description_fallback: Ordered list of snap points (any order; the composable sorts them). Defaults to closed / peek / half / full.

props.name: defaultSnap
props.description_fallback: Initial snap id; defaults to "half".

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: When true, ignore pointer events entirely.

props.name: persistent
props.description_fallback: When true, dragging past closed snaps back instead of dismissing.

examples.title_fallback: Passing options to useSheetSwipe

entry.name: ISheetSwipeReturn
entry.description_fallback: Public surface returned by useSheetSwipe(). Exposes reactive state (currentSnap, dragOffset, isDragging, currentSnapHeight) and imperative controls (snapTo, dismiss) to drive the bottom-sheet from outside the composable.

props.name: currentSnap
props.description_fallback: The currently-active snap id.

props.name: dragOffset
props.description_fallback: Live drag delta during a gesture (px from snap baseline).

props.name: isDragging
props.description_fallback: True while a pointer is held on the handle.

props.name: snapTo
props.description_fallback: Imperative snap — programmatically move the sheet to a named snap point.

props.name: dismiss
props.description_fallback: Convenience alias for snapTo("closed").

props.name: currentSnapHeight
props.description_fallback: Resolved height (in px) of the active snap point. Useful for layout maths and test assertions.

examples.title_fallback: Programmatic sheet control via useSheetSwipe
