entry.name: ISliderFieldProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamSliderField> — a range input with three visual variants (field, timer, audio), optional waveform peaks, range mode, tick marks, hover tooltip, and the full design surface.
entry.definition:
export interface ISliderFieldProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IDensityProps, IColorProps, IInputProps, IFocusProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IBorderProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IRippleProps, IDirectionProps {
    disabled?: boolean
    required?: boolean
    label?: string
    error?: boolean
    readonly?: boolean
    max?: number | string
    min?: number | string
    step?: number | string
    /**
     * Visual props forwarded to the track sub-component (rail / fill / ticks).
     * Color / size / rounded only — pointer math is owned by the native
     * `<input type="range">`.
     */
    trackProps?: ISliderFieldTrackProps
    reverse?: boolean
    modelValue?: number | string | Array<number> | Array<string>
    range?: boolean

    showTicks?: TAlways
    ticks?: Array<number> | Record<string, string>
    tickSize?: TSize | number
    /**
     * Compact "inset" style — the draggable thumb sits **inside** the
     * track (smaller, no overflow). Mirrors `OrigamSwitch`'s `inset`
     * flag. Useful in tight contexts like a volume tooltip or a compact
     * form row.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    inset?: boolean

    /**
     * Visual variant of the slider.
     *
     * - `'field'`  → default, wraps the slider in `<origam-input>` chrome
     *               (label, messages, prepend/append). Use for form-grade
     *               sliders (e.g. settings panels, ColorPicker HSL slider).
     * - `'timer'`  → bare wrapper without `<origam-input>`. Hairline rail
     *               (2 px at rest, 4 px on hover/focus), thumb hidden
     *               until hover/focus/scrub. Use for media-scrubber-style
     *               UI (video timeline, volume slider).
     * - `'audio'`  → same as `timer`, plus a waveform background painted
     *               from `peaks`. Use for `OrigamAudio` waveform scrubbing.
     *
     * @default 'field'
     */
    variant?: TSliderFieldVariant

    /**
     * Secondary fill rendered from `min` to `buffered`, layered behind
     * the active fill at reduced opacity. Mirrors a video player's
     * "loaded ranges" indicator. Has no effect when undefined.
     */
    buffered?: number

    /**
     * Hide the thumb at rest and only reveal it on container `:hover`,
     * `:focus-within`, or during a drag. CSS-only — no JS state.
     *
     * For `variant='timer'` and `variant='audio'`, this is effectively
     * always on (forced via the variant's class). For `variant='field'`,
     * the consumer must opt in explicitly.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    showThumbOnHoverOnly?: boolean

    /**
     * Render a small floating tooltip above the rail showing the value
     * under the cursor. The cursor X position is forwarded as a CSS
     * variable updated in a single RAF-throttled `pointermove` handler.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    showHoverTooltip?: boolean

    /**
     * Formatter applied to the hover tooltip text. Receives the value
     * under the cursor (clamped to `[min, max]`).
     *
     * @default (value: number) => String(value)
     */
    formatHoverTooltip?: (value: number) => string

    /**
     * Waveform peaks in the range `[0, 1]`. When set with
     * `variant='audio'`, peaks are painted as vertical bars BEHIND the
     * track via inline SVG: bars left of the thumb use the active color,
     * bars right use a 35 %-mixed fade. No effect for other variants.
     */
    peaks?: ReadonlyArray<number>
}

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables the slider — prevents interaction and applies a disabled visual state.

props.name: required
props.description_fallback: Marks the field as required (validation semantic).

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Field label rendered by the wrapping OrigamInput chrome (variant="field" only).

props.name: error
props.description_fallback: Forces the danger intent on both color channels — drives the error visual state.

props.name: readonly
props.description_fallback: Makes the slider non-interactive while keeping it visually enabled.

props.name: max
props.description_fallback: Maximum value of the range. Forwarded to the underlying <input type="range">.

props.name: min
props.description_fallback: Minimum value of the range.

props.name: step
props.description_fallback: Step increment for snapping.

props.name: trackProps
props.description_fallback: Visual props forwarded to the track sub-component (color, size, rounded, ticks).

props.name: reverse
props.description_fallback: Reverses the start direction of the fill.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: Bound value (v-model). Array for range mode.

props.name: range
props.description_fallback: Enables two-thumb range mode — modelValue must be an array of two values.

props.name: showTicks
props.description_fallback: Controls tick mark visibility.

props.name: ticks
props.description_fallback: Custom tick positions and optional labels.

props.name: tickSize
props.description_fallback: Size of tick dot markers.

props.name: inset
props.description_fallback: Compact inset style — the draggable thumb sits inside the track. Defaults to false.

props.name: variant
props.description_fallback: Visual variant: "field" (default, with input chrome), "timer" (bare rail, hidden thumb), "audio" (timer + waveform peaks).

props.name: buffered
props.description_fallback: Secondary fill from min to buffered (e.g. loaded video buffer). Layered behind active fill at reduced opacity.

props.name: showThumbOnHoverOnly
props.description_fallback: Hides the thumb at rest and reveals it on hover/focus/drag. Forced on for timer and audio variants.

props.name: showHoverTooltip
props.description_fallback: Renders a floating tooltip above the rail showing the value under the cursor.

props.name: formatHoverTooltip
props.description_fallback: Formatter for the hover tooltip text. Receives the clamped cursor value.

props.name: peaks
props.description_fallback: Waveform peaks in [0, 1]. Used with variant="audio" to paint vertical bars behind the track.

examples.title_fallback: Audio waveform scrubber
examples.code:
<OrigamSliderField
    variant="audio"
    :peaks="waveformPeaks"
    :min="0"
    :max="duration"
    v-model="currentTime"
    :format-hover-tooltip="t => formatTime(t)"
/>

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Bold first line. Useful for short labels (e.g. "Saved").

props.name: message
props.description_fallback: Body text under the title (or sole line when title is absent).

props.name: intent

import { useSnackbarGroup } from 'origam'
import type { ISnackbarGroupItemOptions } from 'origam'

const { notify } = useSnackbarGroup()

const opts: ISnackbarGroupItemOptions = {
    title: 'Changes saved',
    intent: 'success',
    duration: 3000,
    dismissible: true,
    onDismiss: () => console.log('dismissed'),
}

notify(opts)

entry.name: ISnackbarGroupProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for <OrigamSnackbarGroup> — a multi-toast container that orchestrates a queue of OrigamSnackbar instances driven through the useSnackbarGroup({ id }) composable.
entry.definition:
export interface ISnackbarGroupProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps {
    /**
     * Identifier of the stack this container renders. Pair with
     * `useSnackbarGroup({ id })` to spawn items into the same stack.
     * Multiple stacks can coexist (e.g. a global one + a per-region
     * one) — each one is keyed independently.
     *
     * @default 'default'
     */
    id?: string
    /**
     * Anchor location on the viewport. Drives both the absolute
     * positioning (top/bottom + left/right/center pinning) and the
     * `direction` default.
     *
     * @default 'bottom-right'
     */
    location?: TSnackbarGroupLocation
    /**
     * Maximum number of items rendered concurrently. When `notify()`
     * pushes a new item past `max`, the oldest item in the stack is
     * evicted FIFO (its `onDismiss` callback still fires).
     *
     * @default 5
     */
    max?: number
    /**
     * Default auto-dismiss timeout (ms) applied to items that do not
     * supply their own `duration`. Pass `0` to make all items sticky
     * by default.
     *
     * @default 5000
     */
    defaultDuration?: number
    /**
     * Gap between stacked items (CSS dimension — `'12px'`, `'1rem'`,
     * `8`, …).
     *
     * @default '12px'
     */
    spacing?: string | number
    /**
     * Stacking order. When unset, defaults to `'top-down'` for
     * `top-*` locations and `'bottom-up'` for `bottom-*` locations
     * — matching the natural reading direction of fresh items.
     */
    direction?: TSnackbarGroupDirection
}

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Identifier of the stack this container renders. Pair with useSnackbarGroup({ id }) to push items into the same stack. Defaults to "default".

props.name: location
props.description_fallback: Anchor location on the viewport — drives both absolute positioning and the direction default. Defaults to "bottom-right".

props.name: max
props.description_fallback: Maximum number of items rendered concurrently. When exceeded, the oldest item is evicted FIFO. Defaults to 5.

props.name: defaultDuration
props.description_fallback: Default auto-dismiss timeout (ms) for items without their own duration. Pass 0 to make all items sticky. Defaults to 5000.

props.name: spacing
props.description_fallback: Gap between stacked items (CSS dimension). Defaults to "12px".

props.name: direction
props.description_fallback: Stacking order. Defaults to "top-down" for top-* locations and "bottom-up" for bottom-* locations.

examples.title_fallback: Global toast container at top-right
examples.code:
<OrigamSnackbarGroup
    id="global"
    location="top-right"
    :max="3"
    :default-duration="4000"
/>

entry.name: ISnackbarItemProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for <OrigamSnackbarItem> — the pure visual layer of a single toast notification. Owns intent theming, icon/title/message layout, action buttons, dismiss button, and ARIA semantics.
entry.definition:
export interface ISnackbarItemProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITypographyProps {
    /**
     * Semantic intent — drives icon defaults and surface colouring via
     * `--origam-color__feedback--{intent}---*` tokens.
     *
     * @default 'info'
     */
    intent?: TIntent

    /**
     * Optional title rendered as a bold first line.
     * Useful for short labels (e.g. "Saved", "Error").
     */
    title?: string

    /**
     * Body text rendered under the title (or as the sole line when
     * `title` is absent). Can be omitted when content is provided
     * via the `default` slot.
     */
    message?: string

    /**
     * Prepend icon override. When omitted, defaults to the per-intent
     * icon (`mdiCheckCircle` for success, `mdiAlertCircle` for danger,
     * etc.). Pass `false` to suppress the icon entirely.
     */
    icon?: TIcon | false

    /**
     * Action buttons rendered inline after the text block.
     * Each action fires `handler()` and dismisses the item by default
     * unless `keepOpen: true`.
     */
    actions?: ReadonlyArray<ISnackbarGroupItemAction>

    /**
     * Whether to render the close (✕) dismiss button.
     *
     * @default true
     */
    dismissible?: boolean

    /**
     * Accessible label for the dismiss button.
     *
     * @default 'Dismiss notification'
     */
    dismissLabel?: string

    /**
     * ARIA role for the item element. Inferred from `intent` when not
     * set explicitly: `warning` / `danger` → `'alert'`, others → `'status'`.
     */
    role?: 'status' | 'alert'

    /**
     * ARIA live region politeness. Inferred from `intent` when not set
     * explicitly: `warning` / `danger` → `'assertive'`, others → `'polite'`.
     */
    ariaLive?: 'polite' | 'assertive'

    /**
     * `data-cy` attribute injected on the root item element, forwarded
     * from the parent stack/snackbar so each toast is addressable in e2e
     * tests.
     */
    dataCy?: string
}

props.name: intent
props.description_fallback: Semantic intent — drives icon defaults and surface coloring. Defaults to "info".

props.name: title
props.description_fallback: Optional title rendered as a bold first line.

props.name: message
props.description_fallback: Body text rendered under the title (or as the sole line when title is absent).

props.name: icon
props.description_fallback: Prepend icon override. Pass false to suppress the icon entirely. Defaults to the per-intent icon.

props.name: actions
props.description_fallback: Action buttons rendered inline after the text block.

props.name: dismissible
props.description_fallback: Whether to render the close dismiss button. Defaults to true.

props.name: dismissLabel
props.description_fallback: Accessible label for the dismiss button. Defaults to "Dismiss notification".

props.name: role
props.description_fallback: ARIA role for the item element. Inferred from intent when not set: warning/danger → "alert", others → "status".

props.name: ariaLive
props.description_fallback: ARIA live region politeness. Inferred from intent: warning/danger → "assertive", others → "polite".

props.name: dataCy
props.description_fallback: data-cy attribute injected on the root item element for e2e test addressing.

examples.title_fallback: Danger alert toast with custom icon
examples.code:
<OrigamSnackbarItem
    intent="danger"
    title="Upload failed"
    message="The file exceeds the 10 MB limit."
    :icon="mdiCloudOffOutline"
    dismissible
/>

entry.name: ISnackbarProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamSnackbar> — a positioned notification surface with auto-dismiss timer, optional progress bar, multi-line and vertical layout modes, and the full design surface (color, border, elevation, spacing).
entry.definition:
export interface ISnackbarProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, IStatusProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IOverlayProps, IPositionProps, ILocationProps, IRoundedProps, IBorderProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IElevationProps, ITransitionComponentProps, IActiveProps, IHoverProps {
    multiLine?: boolean
    text?: string
    timer?: boolean | string
    timeout?: number | string
    vertical?: boolean
}

props.name: multiLine
props.description_fallback: Allows the snackbar text to wrap across multiple lines.

props.name: text
props.description_fallback: Body text of the snackbar. Can be used instead of the default slot.

props.name: timer
props.description_fallback: Shows a progress bar indicating the remaining auto-dismiss time. Pass a color string to tint it.

props.name: timeout
props.description_fallback: Auto-dismiss delay in ms. Defaults to 5000. Pass -1 to disable auto-dismiss.

props.name: vertical
props.description_fallback: Stacks the text and action vertically instead of inline.

examples.title_fallback: Auto-dismiss snackbar with timer
examples.code:
<OrigamSnackbar
    v-model="show"
    text="Your changes have been saved."
    :timeout="4000"

    location="bottom-center"
/>

entry.name: ISrcObject
entry.description_fallback: Structured image source descriptor for <OrigamImg>. Allows passing src, srcset, lazySrc, aspectRatio and alt as a single object to the src prop instead of individual attributes.
entry.definition:
export interface ISrcObject {
    src?: string
    srcset?: string
    lazySrc?: string
    aspectRatio: number
    alt?: string
}

props.name: src
props.description_fallback: The primary image URL.

props.name: srcset
props.description_fallback: Responsive srcset string for the image.

props.name: lazySrc
props.description_fallback: Low-quality placeholder image URL shown while the main src loads.

props.name: aspectRatio
props.description_fallback: Intrinsic aspect ratio (width / height). Required to reserve layout space before the image loads.

props.name: alt
props.description_fallback: Alt text for the image. Forwarded to the underlying element.

examples.title_fallback: Passing an ISrcObject to OrigamImg
examples.code:
import type { ISrcObject } from 'origam'

const image: ISrcObject = {
    src: '/hero.jpg',

    lazySrc: '/hero-blur.jpg',

    alt: 'Hero banner',
}

entry.name: IStackProvide
entry.description_fallback: Vue provide/inject contract shared by the overlay-stack composable. Allows child overlays to register themselves with a parent stack so z-index ordering is managed globally rather than per-component.

props.name: activeChildren
props.description_fallback: Set of numeric IDs of currently open child overlays. The stack composable adds an ID on open and removes it on close to track depth ordering.

entry.name: IStateEffectConfig
entry.description_fallback: Shared shape for state-aware visual overrides (hover, active). When passed as an object to the hover or active prop, its keys override the corresponding resting props only while the state is engaged.

props.name: enabled
props.description_fallback: Force the state on regardless of mouse / pointer events. Useful for controlled hover toggled by parent state.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Foreground (text / icon) colour override while the state is engaged.

props.name: bgColor
props.description_fallback: Surface background colour override while the state is engaged.

props.name: border
props.description_fallback: Border width / style / direction override while the state is engaged.

props.name: rounded
props.description_fallback: Corner radius override while the state is engaged.

props.name: elevation
props.description_fallback: Box-shadow elevation override while the state is engaged.

props.name: padding
props.description_fallback: Padding scalar override while the state is engaged. Per-axis padding is not supported in state overrides.

props.name: margin
props.description_fallback: Margin scalar override while the state is engaged.

props.name: gap
props.description_fallback: Flex/grid gap override while the state is engaged. Consumed by components that expose a gap prop.

examples.title_fallback: Using hover and active overrides on OrigamCard
examples.code:
<OrigamCard
    bg-color="primary"
    :hover="{ bgColor: 'success', border: 'thick', rounded: 'lg' }"

/>

props.description_fallback: Semantic status level — success, warning, danger or info. Controls the colour and default icon of the status indicator.

props.name: statusIconPosition

entry.name: IStepperItem

props.description_fallback: Drives the visual state of the step independently of modelValue (e.g. complete, error).

entry.name: ISticky
entry.description_fallback: Return shape of the useSticky composable. Exposes the root element ref, a reactive flag indicating whether the component is currently stuck, and the computed CSS styles for the layout item slot.

props.description_fallback: Template ref to the sticky component root element, used to measure its dimensions for the layout item slot.

props.name: isSticky
props.description_fallback: True when the component has passed its scroll threshold and is currently in its sticky (fixed) position.

props.name: layoutItemStyles

entry.name: IStyleTagOptions
entry.description_fallback: Options bag for the useStyleTag composable — controls media query, immediate injection, manual mode, and the DOM id of the injected <style> element.
entry.definition:
export interface IStyleTagOptions extends IConfigurableDocument {
    /**
     * Media query for styles to apply
     */
    media?: string

    /**
     * Load the style immediately
     *
     * @default true
     */
    immediate?: boolean

    /**
     * Manual controls the timing of loading and unloading
     *
     * @default false
     */
    manual?: boolean

    /**
     * DOM id of the style tag
     *
     * @default auto-incremented
     */
    id?: string
}

props.name: media
props.description_fallback: Media query string for the style tag (e.g. "print", "(prefers-color-scheme: dark)").

props.name: immediate
props.description_fallback: When true (default), the style is injected immediately on composable setup.

props.name: manual
props.description_fallback: When true, injection and removal are fully manual — the composable does not load/unload automatically.

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: DOM id attribute set on the injected <style> tag. Auto-incremented when omitted.

examples.title_fallback: Injecting a scoped style tag with a media query

entry.name: ISwitchProps
entry.description_fallback: Full prop surface for <OrigamSwitch> — extends selection-control, input, color, border, elevation, density, margin/padding, rounded and active/hover contracts, with three switch-specific facets: indeterminate, inset and flat.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description_fallback: Puts the switch in a three-state (indeterminate) mode — neither ON nor OFF.

props.name: inset
props.description_fallback: Enables the Material inset variant — taller, fully-rounded rail with inner thumb.

props.name: flat
props.description_fallback: Removes elevation and border from the switch track surface.

entry.name: ISwitchTrackEmits

entry.name: ISwitchTrackProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamSwitchTrack> — the rounded rail behind the thumb. Owns the visual surface (bgColor = rail, color = foreground/thumb intent) and exposes inset, error, disabled and readonly states.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: Whether the switch is currently ON. Drives the --dirty modifier on the track.

props.name: isValid
props.description_fallback: Validation state forwarded from the surrounding <OrigamInput>.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disabled state — applies the muted token and blocks pointer events.

props.name: readonly
props.description_fallback: Readonly state — keeps visual appearance but blocks input changes.

props.name: error
props.description_fallback: Error state — overrides the rail background with the danger token.

props.name: inset
props.description_fallback: Inset (Material) variant — taller, fully-rounded rail.

examples.title_fallback: Rendering a track in error state
examples.code:
<OrigamSwitchTrack
  :model-value="false"
  :error="true"
  bgColor="danger"
  inset
/>

entry.name: ISwitchTrackSlots
entry.description_fallback: Slot signatures for <OrigamSwitchTrack> — exposes track.true and track.false half-label slots plus an overlay slot for absolute-positioned decorations (e.g. a loading bar).
entry.definition:
export interface ISwitchTrackSlots extends ICommonsComponentSlots {
    'track.true'?: (props: ISwitchTrackSlotsProps) => any
    'track.false'?: (props: ISwitchTrackSlotsProps) => any
    /**
     * Free-form overlay rendered inside the track AFTER the
     * `track.true` / `track.false` half-labels. Used by `OrigamSwitch`
     * to host a linear progress bar when `loading={ type: 'line' }`,
     * but consumers can put any absolute-positioned decoration here
     * (gradient sweep, sparkles, …) without subclassing the track.
     */
    overlay?: (props: ISwitchTrackSlotsProps) => any
}

props.name: overlay
props.description_fallback: Free-form overlay rendered inside the track after the half-labels. Used for loading bars or decorative elements.

examples.title_fallback: Icon labels on each half of the track
examples.code:
<OrigamSwitch v-model="dark">
  <template #track.true>
    <OrigamIcon icon="mdi-weather-night" size="10" />
  </template>
  <template #track.false>
    <OrigamIcon icon="mdi-weather-sunny" size="10" />
  </template>
</OrigamSwitch>

entry.name: ISwitchTrackSlotsProps
entry.description_fallback: Slot payload forwarded to the track.true, track.false and overlay slots of <OrigamSwitchTrack> — provides model (ON/OFF) and isValid so slot content can adapt contextually.

props.name: model
props.description_fallback: Current ON/OFF state of the switch — true = ON.

props.name: isValid
props.description_fallback: Validation state forwarded from the surrounding input context. null = pristine.

examples.title_fallback: Conditional icon based on model + validity
examples.code:
<template #track.true="{ model, isValid }">
  <OrigamIcon
    :icon="isValid === false ? 'mdi-alert' : 'mdi-check'"
    size="10"
  />
</template>

props.description_fallback: HTML tag rendered as the panels container root.

import { inject } from 'vue'
import { ORIGAM_TAB_PANELS_KEY } from 'origam'
import type { ITabPanelsProvide } from 'origam'

const { transition, isReversed } = inject<ITabPanelsProvide>(ORIGAM_TAB_PANELS_KEY)!

entry.definition:
export interface ITableProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IBorderProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IHoverProps, IDimensionProps, IDensityProps, ITagProps, ITypographyProps {
    fixedHeader?: boolean
    fixedFooter?: boolean

props.name: fixedHeader
props.description_fallback: Pins the <thead> to the top of the scroll area so it stays visible while scrolling.

props.name: fixedFooter
props.description_fallback: Pins the <tfoot> to the bottom of the scroll area.

props.name: caption
props.description_fallback: Text content for the <caption> element (accessibility + SEO).

props.name: captionVisible
props.description_fallback: When false (default) the caption is visually hidden but accessible. Set to true to render it visibly.

entry.name: ITextFieldEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emit contract for <OrigamTextField> — aggregates field and input lifecycle events, control click/mousedown events, and mask-specific validity and completion signals.
entry.definition:
export interface ITextFieldEmits extends IFieldEmits, IInputEmits {
    (e: 'click:control', value: MouseEvent): void
    (e: 'mousedown:control', value: MouseEvent): void
    /**
     * Emitted on every input/paste when a mask is active.
     * Carries the current validity status (pattern + validator).
     */
    (e: 'valid', value: boolean): void
    /**
     * Emitted when every consumer slot of the mask has been
     * filled. The unmasked value is provided for convenience.
     */
    (e: 'complete', value: { complete: boolean, unmasked: string }): void
}

entry.name: ITextMaskProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for <OrigamTextMask> — a typographic "window" component that paints text as a transparent fill over an animated background (background-clip: text). Accepts gradient objects, raw CSS gradient strings, preset names or image URLs.
entry.definition:
export interface ITextMaskProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, ITypographyProps {
    /**
     * Text to mask. Ignored when the `default` slot is provided.
     *
     * Rich markup (e.g. multi-line headlines, inline `<span>` overrides)
     * should go through the slot instead.
     */
    text?: string
    /**
     * Background painted behind the text glyphs. REQUIRED.
     *
     * Accepts:
     *   - A structured {@link IGradient} object — resolved via
     *     `resolveGradient()` and emitted as a `linear|radial|conic-gradient`
     *     CSS function call. Stops can reference intents (`primary`,
     *     `success`, …) — the resolver swaps them for token-driven CSS
     *     variables, so the gradient re-tints automatically when the
     *     theme changes.
     *   - A raw CSS gradient string (`linear-gradient(...)`, …) — emitted
     *     verbatim.
     *   - A preset name (`gradient-sunset`, etc.) — resolves to the
     *     matching `--origam-gradient---{slug}` CSS variable.
     *   - Any other string — used as a raw `background-image` value
     *     (typical: `url(/hero.jpg)` for an image, `url(data:...)` for an
     *     inline asset).
     */
    background: string | IGradient
    /**
     * Animate the background. Combined with `animationType` to pick a
     * keyframe set.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    animated?: boolean
    /**
     * Duration of one animation cycle. Accepts any CSS `<time>` value
     * (`'3s'`, `'750ms'`, …). Forwarded to `animation-duration`.
     *
     * @default '3s'
     */
    animationDuration?: string
    /**
     * Animation strategy. See {@link TTextMaskAnimation}.
     *
     * @default 'pan'
     */
    animationType?: TTextMaskAnimation
    /**
     * `text-align` of the masked content.
     *
     * @default 'left'
     */
    align?: TBlockquoteAlign
}

props.name: text
props.description_fallback: Text to mask. Ignored when the default slot is provided.

props.name: background
props.description_fallback: Background painted behind the text glyphs. Accepts a gradient object, raw CSS gradient string, preset name or image URL. Required.

props.name: animated
props.description_fallback: Animates the background. Combined with animationType to pick a keyframe set.

props.name: animationDuration
props.description_fallback: Duration of one animation cycle. Accepts any CSS <time> value ('3s', '750ms', …).

props.name: animationType
props.description_fallback: Animation strategy. Default is 'pan'.

props.name: align
props.description_fallback: text-align of the masked content.

examples.code:
<OrigamTextMask
  text="Design system"
  background="gradient-ocean"

  :font-size="64"

/>

entry.description_fallback: Full prop surface for <OrigamTextareaField> — extends field, input, color, density, adjacent-inner, padding/margin, border, rounded and elevation contracts. Adds auto-grow, rows, counter, prefix/suffix, rich mode and toolbar configuration.
entry.definition:
export interface ITextareaFieldProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IDensityProps, IFieldProps, IInputProps, IPaddingProps, IMarginProps, IBorderProps, IRoundedProps, IElevationProps, IAdjacentInnerProps, ITypographyProps {

    persistentCounter?: boolean
    noResize?: boolean
    rows?: number | string
    maxRows?: number | string
    suffix?: string
    modelModifiers?: string | boolean
    /**
     * Render mode. `'plain'` (default) keeps the legacy `<textarea>` element.
     * `'rich'` swaps it for a `contenteditable` surface + rich toolbar.
     */
    mode?: TTextareaMode
    /**
     * When `mode="rich"`, format of the value emitted via `update:modelValue`.
     * `'html'` (default) emits sanitised HTML, `'markdown'` emits a
     * conservative CommonMark subset.

     * Ordered list of toolbar commands to display in rich mode. Pass
     * `false` to hide the toolbar entirely.
     */

     * Position of the toolbar relative to the editing surface in rich mode.
     */

}

entry.name: ITimelineProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamTimeline>. Supports vertical and horizontal orientations, data-driven rendering via items, side alignment, and the full color/size/density surface.
entry.definition:
export interface ITimelineProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, ITagProps, IColorProps, ISizeProps, IDensityProps {
    items?: ITimelineEntry[]
    /**
     * Layout direction.
     *  - `'vertical'` (default): dots stacked top→bottom, content next to each dot
     *    (`side` chooses left / right / alternating).
     *  - `'horizontal'`: dots laid out left→right inside a scroll-snapping
     *    track so the user can navigate point-to-point with a swipe / scroll.
     *    Content (title/subtitle/body) renders BELOW each dot.
     */
    orientation?: TTimelineOrientation
    side?: 'start' | 'end' | 'alternating'
    truncateLine?: boolean
    ariaLabel?: string
}

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Data array driving automatic item rendering. Each entry maps to an OrigamTimelineItem.

props.name: orientation
props.description_fallback: Layout direction: vertical (default, top to bottom) or horizontal (left to right with scroll-snap).

props.name: side
props.description_fallback: Default side for item content relative to the connector line.

props.name: truncateLine
props.description_fallback: Hide the trailing connector line after the last item.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description_fallback: Accessible label for the timeline landmark (aria-label on the root element).

examples.title_fallback: Horizontal timeline with alternating sides
examples.code:
<OrigamTimeline
    :items="steps"
    orientation="horizontal"
    side="alternating"
    color="primary"
    aria-label="Project milestones"
/>

entry.name: ITouchHandlers
entry.description_fallback: Named callback map for the v-touch directive — one optional handler per lifecycle event (start, end, move) and per swipe direction (left, right, up, down).

export interface ITouchHandlers {
    start?: (wrapperEvent: { originalEvent: TouchEvent } & ITouchData) => void
    end?: (wrapperEvent: { originalEvent: TouchEvent } & ITouchData) => void
    move?: (wrapperEvent: { originalEvent: TouchEvent } & ITouchData) => void
    left?: (wrapper: ITouchData) => void
    right?: (wrapper: ITouchData) => void
    up?: (wrapper: ITouchData) => void
    down?: (wrapper: ITouchData) => void
}

props.name: start
props.description_fallback: Called when a touch gesture starts.

props.name: end
props.description_fallback: Called when a touch gesture ends.

props.name: move
props.description_fallback: Called on every touchmove event.

props.name: left
props.description_fallback: Called when the gesture is recognised as a leftward swipe.

props.name: right
props.description_fallback: Called when the gesture is recognised as a rightward swipe.

props.name: up
props.description_fallback: Called when the gesture is recognised as an upward swipe.

props.name: down
props.description_fallback: Called when the gesture is recognised as a downward swipe.

entry.name: ITouchStoredHandlers
entry.description_fallback: Internal store of raw DOM event listeners attached by the v-touch directive — kept on the element so they can be correctly removed on directive unmount.

props.name: touchstart
props.description_fallback: Raw touchstart listener stored for removeEventListener on unmount.

props.name: touchend
props.description_fallback: Raw touchend listener stored for removeEventListener on unmount.

props.name: touchmove
props.description_fallback: Raw touchmove listener stored for removeEventListener on unmount.

examples.title_fallback: Internal usage in the v-touch directive lifecycle

entry.name: ITouchValue
entry.description_fallback: The binding value accepted by the v-touch directive — extends ITouchHandlers with optional parent-element delegation and addEventListener options.

props.name: parent
props.description_fallback: When true, event listeners are attached to the element's parent instead of the element itself.

props.name: options
props.description_fallback: Options forwarded verbatim to addEventListener (capture, passive, once).

entry.name: ITransitionComponentProps
entry.description_fallback: Minimal transition surface mixed into overlay and floating components. Accepts a transition name string, a full TTransitionProps object, or false/true to disable/use the component default.

props.name: transition
props.description_fallback: Transition configuration: a named transition string, a full props object, true to use the component default, or false to disable.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disable the transition entirely (equivalent to transition=false).

examples.title_fallback: Disabling transition on a tooltip
examples.code: <OrigamTooltip :transition="false" text="No animation" />

entry.name: ITransitionProps
entry.description_fallback: Core transition configuration shared by all origam transition wrappers. Controls the Vue transition name, mode, group mode, leave behavior, and origin for transform-based animations.
entry.definition:
export interface ITransitionProps {
    mode?: TTransitionMode
    disabled?: boolean
    name?: string
    group?: boolean
    hideOnLeave?: boolean
    leaveAbsolute?: boolean
    origin?: string
}

props.name: mode
props.description_fallback: Vue transition mode: in-out, out-in, or default (simultaneous).

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Skip the transition entirely — content appears/disappears instantly.

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: CSS class prefix for the transition (e.g. origam-fade applies .origam-fade-enter-active etc.).

props.name: group
props.description_fallback: Render a <transition-group> instead of <transition> for list animations.

props.name: hideOnLeave
props.description_fallback: Set visibility: hidden during the leave phase to avoid flash artifacts on overflow: hidden parents.

props.name: leaveAbsolute
props.description_fallback: Position the leaving element absolutely so entering content can take its place immediately.

props.name: origin
props.description_fallback: CSS transform-origin override for scale-based transitions (e.g. top center).

examples.title_fallback: Scale transition with top-center origin

entry.name: ITransitionWindowProps
entry.description_fallback: Transition props extended with an optional window context reference. Used by window/tab/step components so the transition direction can adapt based on the active index provided via IWindowProvide.

props.name: window
props.description_fallback: Injected window context that carries the current/previous index used to pick the slide direction.

examples.title_fallback: Extending ITransitionWindowProps in a step component

entry.name: ITranslateScaleProps
entry.description_fallback: Extends ITransitionProps with a target reference used by the translate-scale transition. The target anchors the transform-origin so the element scales from the activator position rather than its own center.

props.name: target
props.description_fallback: DOM element or pixel coordinates that anchor the translate+scale transform origin. When provided the element animates from/to that point.

examples.title_fallback: Scale from a known pixel point

entry.name: ITreeviewNode
entry.description_fallback: Shape of a single node in the treeview data tree. Supports recursive nesting via the children array, optional icon, custom size, disabled state, and expandability control.
entry.definition:
export interface ITreeviewNode {
    id: string
    label: string
    icon?: TIcon
    size?: string
    children?: ITreeviewNode[]
    disabled?: boolean
    expandable?: boolean
}

props.name: id
props.description_fallback: Unique identifier for the node. Used as the key for selection and expansion state.

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Display text for the node.

props.name: icon
props.description_fallback: MDI icon name rendered before the label.

props.name: size
props.description_fallback: Override the icon size for this specific node.

props.name: children
props.description_fallback: Nested child nodes. Presence of this field (even empty) marks the node as a branch.

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Prevent interaction (selection and expansion) on this node.

props.name: expandable
props.description_fallback: Show the expand chevron even when children is empty or undefined (useful for lazy-load patterns).

examples.title_fallback: Building a file-system treeview tree

entry.name: ITreeviewNodeProps
entry.description_fallback: Props contract for <OrigamTreeviewNode>. Internal component rendering a single tree node. Receives the full ITreeviewNode data object plus its nesting depth for indentation.

props.name: node
props.description_fallback: The node data object to render (id, label, icon, children, disabled, expandable).

props.name: depth
props.description_fallback: Nesting depth starting at 0 for root nodes. Used to compute the left padding indent.

examples.title_fallback: Direct usage of OrigamTreeviewNode
examples.code:
<OrigamTreeviewNode
    :node="{ id: 'root', label: 'src', icon: 'mdi-folder', children: [] }"
    :depth="0"
/>

entry.name: ITreeviewProps
entry.description_fallback: Full props contract for <OrigamTreeview>. Hierarchical tree with multi/single selection, expansion control, line guides, lazy-expand support, and the full color/bgColor/size/density surface.
entry.definition:
export interface ITreeviewProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, ISizeProps, IDensityProps {
    items: ITreeviewNode[]
    modelValue?: string[] | string
    expandedValue?: string[]
    selectMode?: TTreeviewSelectMode
    selectableNodes?: TTreeviewSelectableNodes
    showLines?: boolean
    expandOnClick?: boolean
}

props.name: items
props.description_fallback: Flat or nested array of tree nodes driving the render.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: Selected node id(s). Array for multi-select, string for single-select. Binds via v-model.

props.name: expandedValue
props.description_fallback: IDs of currently expanded branch nodes. Controlled via v-model:expandedValue.

props.name: selectMode
props.description_fallback: Selection strategy: none, single, or multiple.

props.name: selectableNodes
props.description_fallback: Which node types can be selected: all, leaf, or branch.

props.name: showLines
props.description_fallback: Render vertical guide lines connecting parent to children.

props.name: expandOnClick
props.description_fallback: Expand/collapse a branch node when the row label area is clicked (not just the chevron).

examples.title_fallback: Multi-select treeview with guide lines
examples.code:
<OrigamTreeview
    :items="fileTree"
    v-model="selected"
    select-mode="multiple"
    selectable-nodes="leaf"
    :show-lines="true"
/>

entry.name: ITreeviewProvide
entry.description_fallback: Context object provided by OrigamTreeview to all descendant OrigamTreeviewNode components via Vue inject. Exposes reactive state accessors and mutation handlers without prop-drilling.
entry.definition:
export interface ITreeviewProvide {
    toggleExpanded: (id: string) => void
    toggleSelected: (id: string) => void
    isExpanded: (id: string) => boolean
    isSelected: (id: string) => boolean
    selectMode: ComputedRef<TTreeviewSelectMode>
    selectableNodes: ComputedRef<TTreeviewSelectableNodes>
    showLines: ComputedRef<boolean>
    expandOnClick: ComputedRef<boolean>
    color: ComputedRef<string | undefined>
}

props.name: toggleExpanded
props.description_fallback: Expand or collapse the branch node with the given id.

props.name: toggleSelected
props.description_fallback: Select or deselect the node with the given id according to the current selectMode.

props.name: isExpanded
props.description_fallback: Returns true if the branch node with the given id is currently expanded.

props.name: isSelected
props.description_fallback: Returns true if the node with the given id is currently selected.

props.name: selectMode
props.description_fallback: Reactive selection strategy (none / single / multiple) forwarded from ITreeviewProps.

props.name: selectableNodes
props.description_fallback: Reactive filter controlling which node types (all / leaf / branch) can be selected.

props.name: showLines
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag controlling whether vertical guide lines are rendered.

props.name: expandOnClick
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag controlling whether a row click expands/collapses the branch.

props.name: color
props.description_fallback: Reactive color intent forwarded to child nodes for consistent selection highlight.

examples.title_fallback: Injecting the treeview context in a custom node renderer
examples.code:
import { inject } from 'vue'
import { TREEVIEW_PROVIDE_KEY } from 'origam/consts'
import type { ITreeviewProvide } from 'origam'

const ctx = inject<ITreeviewProvide>(TREEVIEW_PROVIDE_KEY)

entry.name: ITypographyProps
entry.definition:
export interface ITypographyProps {
    /**
     * Font family token. Maps to `--origam-font__family---{fontFamily}`
     * (sans · mono · serif).
     * When unset, the component keeps its theme font-family.
     */
    fontFamily?: TFontFamily
    /**
     * Font size token. Maps to `--origam-font__size---{fontSize}`
     * (xs · sm · md · lg · xl · 2xl · 3xl · 4xl · 5xl).
     * When unset, the component keeps its theme / density font-size.
     */
    fontSize?: TFontSize
    /**
     * Font weight token. Maps to `--origam-font__weight---{fontWeight}`
     * (regular 400 · medium 500 · semibold 600 · bold 700 · extrabold 800 · black 900).
     * When unset, the component keeps its theme font-weight.
     */
    fontWeight?: TFontWeight
    /**
     * Line-height token. Maps to `--origam-font__lineHeight---{lineHeight}`
     * (none 1 · tight 1.25 · snug 1.375 · normal 1.5 · relaxed 1.625 · loose 2).
     * When unset, the component keeps its theme line-height.
     */
    lineHeight?: TLineHeight
    /**
     * Letter-spacing token. Maps to `--origam-font__letterSpacing---{letterSpacing}`
     * (tight -0.025em · normal 0em · wide 0.0094em · wider 0.0125em · widest 0.0893em).
     * When unset, the component keeps its theme letter-spacing.
     */
    letterSpacing?: TLetterSpacing
}

entry.name: IUseAudioProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for the low-level useAudio composable — wires a frequency analyser and a play-on-flag mechanism. Renamed from IAudioProps to avoid collision with the OrigamAudio component interface.

props.name: audio
props.description_fallback: URL or data-URI of the audio source to load into the analyser.

props.name: playAudio
props.description_fallback: Reactive flag — when toggled to true the composable starts playback; false pauses it.

examples.title_fallback: Consuming useAudio with typed props

entry.name: IUseCalendarOptions
entry.description_fallback: Options consumed by the useCalendar composable. All fields are thunks (getter functions) so the composable stays reactive without owning the prop reference directly.
entry.definition:
export interface IUseCalendarOptions {
    events: () => Array<IEvent>
    view: () => TCalendarView
    currentDate: () => Date
    firstDayOfWeek: () => number
    slotDuration: () => number
    dayStartHour: () => number
    dayEndHour: () => number
    minDate?: () => Date | null
    maxDate?: () => Date | null
    locale?: () => string
}

props.name: events
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the current event array. Recurring events are expanded internally by the composable.

props.name: view
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the active view mode (month / week / day / agenda).

props.name: currentDate
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the anchor date for the currently rendered range.

props.name: firstDayOfWeek
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the first weekday column index (0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday).

props.name: slotDuration
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the minutes per time slot row in week/day views (15, 30, or 60).

props.name: dayStartHour
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the first rendered hour in week/day views (0–23).

props.name: dayEndHour
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the last rendered hour in week/day views (1–24).

props.name: minDate
props.description_fallback: Optional getter returning the lower navigation bound. Prev navigation is disabled before this date.

props.name: maxDate
props.description_fallback: Optional getter returning the upper navigation bound. Next navigation is disabled after this date.

props.name: locale
props.description_fallback: Optional getter returning the BCP 47 locale tag for weekday/month name formatting.

examples.title_fallback: Passing options to useCalendar
examples.code:
import { useCalendar } from 'origam'
import type { IUseCalendarOptions } from 'origam'

const opts: IUseCalendarOptions = {
    events: () => myEvents.value,
    view: () => activeView.value,
    currentDate: () => today.value,
    firstDayOfWeek: () => 1,
    slotDuration: () => 30,
    dayStartHour: () => 8,
    dayEndHour: () => 20,
}

const { weeks, timeSlots } = useCalendar(opts)

entry.name: IUseChartGaugeOptions
entry.description_fallback: Strict options consumed by useChartGauge. All getters are thunks so the composable can be reactively driven by props, a store, or computed sources without re-instantiation.

props.name: value
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the current gauge value (un-clamped). The composable clamps to [min..max] internally.

props.name: min
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the lower bound of the gauge range.

props.name: max
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the upper bound of the gauge range.

props.name: width
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the SVG viewBox width in pixels.

props.name: height
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the SVG viewBox height in pixels.

props.name: padding
props.type_label: () => { top: number, right: number, bottom: number, left: number }
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the SVG inner padding that frees room for endpoint labels.

props.name: thickness
props.description_fallback: Optional getter overriding the arc stroke thickness in SVG pixels.

props.name: startAngle
props.description_fallback: Optional getter overriding the arc start angle in radians. Default is -3pi/4.

props.name: endAngle
props.description_fallback: Optional getter overriding the arc end angle in radians. Default is +3pi/4.

examples.title_fallback: Driving useChartGauge from component props
examples.code:
import { useChartGauge } from 'origam'
import type { IUseChartGaugeOptions } from 'origam'

const opts: IUseChartGaugeOptions = {
    value: () => props.value,
    min: () => props.gaugeMin ?? 0,
    max: () => props.gaugeMax ?? 100,
    width: () => svgWidth.value,
    height: () => svgHeight.value,
    padding: () => ({ top: 20, right: 20, bottom: 30, left: 20 }),
}

const { trackPath, valuePath, ratio } = useChartGauge(opts)

entry.name: IUseChartOptions
entry.description_fallback: Strict, defaulted options consumed by useChart. All fields are getter thunks so the composable remains reactive without owning the prop reference. Drives path computation for all eight chart primitives.
entry.definition:
export interface IUseChartOptions {
    type: () => TChartType
    series: () => Array<IChartSeries>
    categories: () => Array<string>
    stacked: () => boolean
    /**
     * Stacking mode when `stacked()` is `true`. Optional — defaults
     * to `'normal'` so callers that don't expose the prop yet are
     * unaffected.
     */
    stacking?: () => TChartStacking
    donutHoleSize: () => number
    colorScheme: () => Array<TIntent | string>
    smoothing: () => TChartSmoothing
    yMin: () => number | undefined
    yMax: () => number | undefined
    width: () => number
    height: () => number
    padding: () => { top: number, right: number, bottom: number, left: number }
    /**
     * Per-label hidden set. Used by the single-pie / donut path to
     * skip slices whose category label was toggled OFF in the
     * legend. Cartesian / radar / gauge / multi-pie use `series.visible`
     * for the same purpose; this option exists for the pie/donut
     * "category as slice" mode where there's no per-slice series to
     * attach visibility to. Optional — defaults to an empty Set.
     */
    hiddenLabels?: () => Set<string>
    /**
     * Secondary (right-hand) Y axis configuration. Optional — when
     * absent the engine ignores `series.yAxis === 1` assignments and
     * plots everything against the primary left axis.
     */
    secondaryYAxis?: () => IChartSecondaryYAxis | undefined
}

props.name: type
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the chart primitive type (line, area, bar, column, pie, donut, radar, gauge).

props.name: series
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the data series array. An empty array renders the #empty slot.

props.name: categories
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the X-axis category labels for cartesian charts.

props.name: stacked
props.description_fallback: Getter returning whether bar/column series are stacked.

props.name: stacking
props.description_fallback: Optional getter returning the stacking mode (normal or percent) when stacked is true.

props.name: donutHoleSize
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the inner-radius proportion for donut charts (0..1).

props.name: colorScheme
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the palette cycled when a series has no explicit color.

props.name: smoothing
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the line/area smoothing strategy (none or curve).

props.name: yMin
props.description_fallback: Getter returning an optional override for the auto-computed Y-axis minimum.

props.name: yMax
props.description_fallback: Getter returning an optional override for the auto-computed Y-axis maximum.

props.name: width
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the SVG viewBox width in pixels.

props.name: height
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the SVG viewBox height in pixels.

props.name: padding
props.type_label: () => { top: number, right: number, bottom: number, left: number }
props.description_fallback: Getter returning the SVG inner padding that reserves space for axes and labels.

props.name: hiddenLabels
props.description_fallback: Optional getter returning the set of pie/donut category labels toggled off via the legend.

props.name: secondaryYAxis
props.description_fallback: Optional getter for right-hand secondary Y-axis configuration. When absent series with yAxis===1 plot against the primary axis.

examples.title_fallback: Passing options to useChart
examples.code:
import { useChart } from 'origam'
import type { IUseChartOptions } from 'origam'

const opts: IUseChartOptions = {
    type: () => 'line',
    series: () => props.series,
    categories: () => props.categories ?? [],
    stacked: () => false,
    donutHoleSize: () => 0.6,
    colorScheme: () => ['primary', 'success', 'danger'],
    smoothing: () => 'curve',
    yMin: () => undefined,
    yMax: () => undefined,
    width: () => svgWidth.value,
    height: () => svgHeight.value,
    padding: () => ({ top: 20, right: 20, bottom: 40, left: 50 }),
}

const { paths, ticks } = useChart(opts)

entry.name: IUseClipboardOptions
entry.description_fallback: Options accepted by useClipboard. Controls the duration the `copied` flag stays true after a successful write.

props.name: feedbackDuration
props.description_fallback: Duration (ms) the returned `copied` ref stays true after a successful write before auto-resetting.

examples.title_fallback: Using useClipboard with custom feedback duration
examples.code: const { copy, copied } = useClipboard({ feedbackDuration: 3000 })

entry.name: IUseCodeReturn
entry.description_fallback: Public surface of the useCode composable. Lazily instantiates a shiki highlighter on first call and reuses it across every OrigamCode instance for the lifetime of the page.
entry.definition:
export interface IUseCodeReturn {
    /**
     * Render `code` to a pre-tokenised HTML string. Resolves once the
     * underlying shiki highlighter has loaded the requested language /
     * theme (lazy; ~200 ms cold, instant on subsequent calls).
     */
    highlight (code: string, lang: TCodeLang): Promise<string>
    /**
     * Force the highlighter to load. Useful for stories / tests that want
     * to warm the cache before mounting consumers.
     */
    prime (): Promise<void>
    /**
     * Whether the singleton highlighter is ready. `false` on first paint,
     * flips to `true` once the dynamic import finishes.
     */
    isReady (): boolean
    /**
     * Wipe the LRU cache. Test-only — production code never needs this.
     */
    resetCacheForTesting (): void
}

examples.title_fallback: Consuming useCode in a component
examples.code:
const { highlight, isReady } = useCode()
const html = await highlight('const x = 1', 'typescript')

entry.name: IUseInlineEditOptions
entry.description_fallback: Options accepted by useInlineEdit. Controls validation rules, trim behaviour, and lifecycle callbacks for the edit-in-place state machine.

props.name: rules
props.description_fallback: Array of validation rules — evaluated in order before validate. First failure blocks the commit and surfaces the error message.

props.name: validate
props.description_fallback: Sync or async validator. Only runs if all rules pass.

props.name: trim
props.description_fallback: Strip surrounding whitespace before running the validator and emitting the new value.

props.name: onConfirm
props.description_fallback: Called when the draft is committed. Use this when the composable is consumed outside the SFC emit pipeline.

props.name: onCancel
props.description_fallback: Called when the draft is discarded.

props.name: onError
props.description_fallback: Called when a validator returns an error message.

examples.title_fallback: Using useInlineEdit with validation

entry.name: IUseLink
entry.description_fallback: Return type of the useLink composable — extends vue-router's useLink return (minus the raw href) with two additional computed flags: isLink and isClickable.

props.name: isLink
props.description_fallback: True when the component renders as an anchor (href or to prop is present).

props.name: isClickable
props.description_fallback: True when the component is interactive (isLink or a click handler is bound).

props.name: href
props.description_fallback: Resolved href string — undefined when no navigation target is set.

examples.title_fallback: Reading isLink to conditionally render an <a> or <button>

entry.name: IUseMediaPlayerOptions
entry.description_fallback: Options accepted by useMediaPlayer. The composable is media-agnostic — mediaRef accepts any HTMLMediaElement (audio OR video).

props.name: autoplay
props.description_fallback: Suppress autoplay when the user has requested reduced motion.

props.name: muted
props.description_fallback: Initial muted state.

props.name: loop
props.description_fallback: Loop on ended.

props.name: preload
props.description_fallback: Buffering hint passed to the HTMLMediaElement.

props.name: mediaRef
props.description_fallback: Resolves to the HTMLMediaElement to drive. When omitted, the composable creates an empty ref and expects the consumer to bind it on a <video ref> / <audio ref> themselves.

examples.title_fallback: Passing options to useMediaPlayer

entry.name: IUseNumberFormatOptions
entry.description_fallback: Options accepted by useNumberFormat. Mirrors the public props of OrigamNumberFormat minus the wiring concerns (tag, class, style), forwarded directly to Intl.NumberFormat.

props.name: format
props.description_fallback: High-level format dialect: decimal, currency, unit, percent, compact, scientific, engineering.

props.name: locale
props.description_fallback: BCP-47 locale tag (or array for fallback chains). The literal string "auto" triggers runtime resolution.

props.name: currency
props.description_fallback: ISO 4217 currency code. Required when format === "currency".

props.name: currencyDisplay
props.description_fallback: Currency-symbol rendering: symbol, narrowSymbol, code, or name.

props.name: unit
props.description_fallback: ECMAScript-sanctioned unit identifier. Required when format === "unit".

props.name: unitDisplay
props.description_fallback: Unit-label rendering: short, long, or narrow.

props.name: notation
props.description_fallback: Raw notation override: standard, scientific, engineering, compact.

props.name: compactDisplay
props.description_fallback: Compact-notation rendering: short or long. Only honoured when notation === "compact".

props.name: minimumFractionDigits
props.description_fallback: Minimum digits after the decimal point.

props.name: maximumFractionDigits
props.description_fallback: Maximum digits after the decimal point.

props.name: minimumSignificantDigits
props.description_fallback: Minimum significant digits (mutually exclusive with fraction digits).

props.name: maximumSignificantDigits
props.description_fallback: Maximum significant digits (mutually exclusive with fraction digits).

props.name: useGrouping
props.description_fallback: Thousands-separator behaviour: true/auto, false, always, min2.

props.name: signDisplay
props.description_fallback: Sign-display strategy: auto, always, except-zero, negative, never.

examples.title_fallback: Formatting a currency value with useNumberFormat

entry.name: IUseOrigamAudioPlayerOptions
entry.description_fallback: Options accepted by the Audio-namespace useAudioPlayer composable. Named distinctly from the legacy IUseAudioPlayerOptions in the Sound namespace so both can coexist in the public barrel.

props.name: autoplay
props.description_fallback: Suppress autoplay when the user has requested reduced motion.

props.name: muted
props.description_fallback: Initial muted state.

props.name: loop
props.description_fallback: Loop on ended.

props.name: preload
props.description_fallback: Buffering hint.

props.name: audioRef
props.description_fallback: Resolves to the HTMLAudioElement to drive. When omitted, the composable creates an empty ref and expects the consumer to bind it on a <audio ref> themselves.

examples.title_fallback: Wiring useAudioPlayer to an <audio> element

entry.name: IUseQrCodeOptions
entry.description_fallback: Lower-level options accepted by useQrCode. Unlike IQrCodeProps, the composable does not speak the canonical DS contracts — it only owns the raw SVG string. The wrapper component is responsible for mapping color/bgColor/rounded down to the primitive foreground/background/cornerRadius keys here.

props.name: errorCorrectionLevel
props.description_fallback: Reed-Solomon redundancy level — same semantics as IQrCodeProps.

props.name: foreground
props.description_fallback: Raw CSS colour painted on dark modules.

props.name: background
props.description_fallback: Raw CSS colour painted behind the matrix (quiet zone).

props.name: margin
props.description_fallback: Quiet-zone padding in module units.

props.name: cornerRadius
props.description_fallback: Per-module corner radius in module units (0 = square, 0.5 = circle). The component layer derives this from the public qrCodeProps.rounded prop.

props.name: logo
props.description_fallback: Optional logo overlay rendered inside the SVG via <image>. Public consumers should use the image prop on OrigamQrCode.

examples.title_fallback: Generating a QR code SVG directly with useQrCode

entry.name: IUseTextareaRichOptions
entry.description_fallback: Returned shape of the useTextareaRich composable. Components consume the public surface (refs + handlers) to render the contenteditable editor and bind the toolbar.

props.name: disabled

entry.name: IUseWatermarkOptions

props.description_fallback: Installs a MutationObserver that re-injects the layer when it is removed. Dissuasive, not a security feature.

entry.name: IUseWaveformOptions
entry.description_fallback:
Options accepted by `useWaveform()` to configure how the audio file
is fetched and decoded into waveform peak data.
entry.definition:
export interface IUseWaveformOptions {
    /**
     * Number of amplitude buckets (bars) to downsample the decoded
     * audio into. A higher value produces a finer-grained waveform but
     * costs more processing time.
     *
     * @default 200
     */
    bins?: number
    /**
     * CORS credentials mode forwarded to `fetch()`.
     *
     * - `'use-credentials'` — include cookies / auth headers.
     * - any other value (or `undefined`) — `'same-origin'` semantics.
     */
    crossOrigin?: string
}

props.name: bins
props.description_fallback:
Number of amplitude buckets (bars) to downsample the decoded
audio into. A higher value produces a finer-grained waveform but
costs more processing time.

props.name: crossOrigin
props.description_fallback:
CORS credentials mode forwarded to `fetch()`.

- `'use-credentials'` — include cookies / auth headers.
- any other value (or `undefined`) — `'same-origin'` semantics.

props.name: id

entry.name: IValidationProps
entry.description_fallback: Complete validation surface for form-control components — extends IFocusProps with rules, error state, error messages, disabled/readonly flags, label and the validateOn trigger.
entry.definition:
export interface IValidationProps extends IFocusProps {
    disabled?: boolean
    /** Field-level error flag. Accepts a `boolean` (error on/off) or a `string`
     *  (error message rendered inline by consumers such as `FileField`). */
    error?: string | boolean
    errorMessages?: Array<string> | string
    maxErrors?: number | string
    name?: string
    label?: string
    readonly?: boolean
    rules?: Array<any>
    modelValue?: any
    validateOn?: TValidateOn
    validationValue?: any
}

props.name: disabled
props.description_fallback: Disables the field — prevents interaction and validation.

props.name: error
props.description_fallback: Forces the field into an error state regardless of rule results.

props.name: errorMessages
props.description_fallback: Static error message(s) shown alongside rule-produced errors.

props.name: maxErrors
props.description_fallback: Maximum number of error messages to display simultaneously.

props.name: name
props.description_fallback: HTML name attribute and validation identifier for the field.

props.name: label
props.description_fallback: Label text displayed above or inside the field.

props.name: readonly
props.description_fallback: Makes the field non-editable while keeping it focusable and validatable.

props.name: rules
props.description_fallback: Array of validation rule functions. Each receives the current value and returns true (valid) or an error string.

props.name: modelValue
props.description_fallback: The v-model value of the field, forwarded to rule functions during validation.

props.name: validateOn
props.description_fallback: When validation runs — e.g. "input", "blur", "submit", or combinations thereof.

props.name: validationValue
props.description_fallback: Override the value passed to rule functions (useful when the internal representation differs from modelValue).

props.description_fallback: Visual rendering style — action variants (text, flat, elevated, tonal, outlined, plain) or input variants (solo, filled, underlined, solo-filled, solo-inverted).

entry.name: IVideoEmits
entry.description_fallback: Emits surfaced by OrigamVideo. The event names mirror the native DOM events one-to-one so consumers can move between the wrapper and a vanilla <video> without re-wiring listeners. Extends IMediaPlayerEmits with video-specific events (fullscreen, pip, skip, quality-change, download).
entry.definition:
export interface IVideoEmits extends IMediaPlayerEmits {
    (e: 'enterfullscreen'): void
    (e: 'exitfullscreen'): void
    (e: 'enterpip'): void
    (e: 'exitpip'): void
    /** Skip event — positive seconds = forward, negative = backward.
     *  Fired by the centered skip buttons and by the double-tap gesture. */
    (e: 'skip', seconds: number): void
    /** Fired when the viewer picks a different quality from the
     *  settings menu. Payload is the new `quality` identifier (e.g.
     *  "1080p"). Consumers can persist this in localStorage to remember
     *  the choice across sessions. */
    (e: 'quality-change', quality: string): void
    /** Fired when the viewer clicks the "Download" row in the settings
     *  menu. Payload is the URL of the file being downloaded so the
     *  consumer can log analytics or block the download by listening
     *  with `.prevent` if needed. */
    (e: 'download', url: string): void
}

examples.title_fallback: Listening to video-specific emits
examples.code:
<OrigamVideo
    src="/video.mp4"
    @enterfullscreen="onFullscreen"
    @skip="onSkip"
    @quality-change="onQuality"
/>

entry.name: IVideoProps
entry.description_fallback: Props for OrigamVideo. Extends the standard DS visual interfaces (color, dimension, rounded, border, margin, padding, elevation) with media-specific controls: src, poster, tracks, autoplay, loop, aspect-ratio, fullscreen, PIP, skip, quality switching, and download.
entry.definition:
export interface IVideoProps extends ICommonsComponentProps, IColorProps, IBgColorProps, IDimensionProps, IRoundedProps, IBorderProps, IMarginProps, IPaddingProps, IElevationProps, ITypographyProps {
    /**
     * Media URL — either a single string for `<video src>`, or an array
     * of `<source>` descriptors when multiple formats are provided. The
     * browser walks the list top-down and picks the first source whose
     * `type` it can decode; pass the most preferred codec first.
     */
    src: string | Array<IVideoSource>
    /**
     * Image rendered before playback starts. Maps to the native
     * `poster` attribute when the `#poster` slot is not used.
     */
    poster?: string
    /**
     * Captions / subtitles / chapter tracks attached to the player.
     * Empty array (the default) renders no `<track>` element.
     *
     * @default []
     */
    tracks?: Array<IVideoTrack>
    /**
     * Starts playback as soon as `loadedmetadata` fires. Browsers
     * usually require `muted` to be true alongside `autoplay`; the
     * component forces `muted=true` when the user passes `autoplay`
     * without an explicit `muted` value.
     *
     * **Accessibility:** `autoplay` is suppressed when the user has
     * requested reduced motion (`prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`).
     *
     * @default false
     */
    autoplay?: boolean
    /**
     * Starts the player muted. Setting `muted=true` together with
     * `autoplay=true` is required for most browsers to allow
     * unattended playback.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    muted?: boolean
    /**
     * Loops playback. The player restarts at `0` when `ended` fires.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    loop?: boolean
    /**
     * Which control surface to render. `'custom'` paints the in-house
     * toolbar; `'native'` exposes the browser's built-in `controls`
     * attribute; `'none'` renders neither so the consumer can drive
     * playback through the `#controls` slot or programmatically.
     *
     * @default 'custom'
     */
    controls?: TVideoControls
    /**
     * Mirrors the native `playsinline` attribute. Required on iOS to
     * keep the player inside the page (otherwise iOS hijacks the
     * playback into the system video viewer). Defaults to `true`
     * because that's the in-page UX we want 99 % of the time.
     *
     * @default true
     */
    playsInline?: boolean
    /**
     * Buffering hint. `'metadata'` (default) loads just enough to
     * compute the duration; `'none'` defers everything until the user
     * presses play; `'auto'` lets the browser pre-buffer as much as it
     * wants.
     *
     * @default 'metadata'
     */
    preload?: 'none' | 'metadata' | 'auto'
    /**
     * CSS `aspect-ratio` value applied to the wrapper. Accepts the
     * presets (`'16/9'`, `'4/3'`, `'1/1'`, `'21/9'`, `'9/16'`) or any
     * raw value (`'2 / 1'`, `'1.85'`, …). The wrapper enforces the
     * ratio via `aspect-ratio` so the player never re-flows the page
     * after metadata loads.
     *
     * @default '16/9'
     */
    aspectRatio?: string
    /**
     * Mirrors the native `crossorigin` attribute. Required when the
     * media or its tracks are served from a CORS-enabled origin and
     * the consumer needs canvas / WebAudio access to the decoded
     * frames. Affects how cookies are sent on the request.
     */
    crossorigin?: 'anonymous' | 'use-credentials'
    /**
     * Disables the picture-in-picture button (and the matching
     * keyboard shortcut). Useful for content where PIP would defeat
     * the experience (e.g. a synced multi-player layout).
     *
     * @default false
     */
    disablePictureInPicture?: boolean
    /**
     * Seconds skipped by the ±skip buttons and by the double-tap
     * gesture on touch devices. Pass `0` to hide the skip buttons.
     *
     * @default 30
     */
    skipSeconds?: number
    /**
     * Show the centered ±skip buttons + play/pause overlay on hover
     * (and always while paused). When `false`, only the bottom
     * toolbar handles play/pause.
     *
     * @default true
     */
    showCenterControls?: boolean
    /**
     * Available playback rates exposed via the config menu.
     *
     * @default [0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75, 2]
     */
    playbackRates?: ReadonlyArray<number>
    /**
     * Initial playback rate. The runtime value is exposed via the
     * `update:playbackRate` emit and the `playbackRate` scoped-slot
     * binding.
     *
     * @default 1
     */
    playbackRate?: number
    /**
     * Layout mode for the bottom toolbar :
     *   - `true` — controls overlaid on the video bottom edge,
     *     auto-hide while playing once the cursor leaves.
     *   - `false` — controls always painted, no auto-hide.
     *
     * @default true
     */
    inset?: boolean
    /**
     * Enable the cast / AirPlay button (Remote Playback API). The
     * button only renders when the browser exposes the API AND at
     * least one cast-capable device is detected. No 3rd-party plugin.
     *
     * @default true
     */
    allowRemotePlayback?: boolean
    /**
     * Enable double-tap gestures on touch devices: tap-tap on the
     * left half = skip backward by `skipSeconds`, on the right half =
     * skip forward.
     *
     * @default true
     */
    doubleTapToSkip?: boolean
    /**
     * Adds a "Download" row to the settings (cog) menu that lets the
     * viewer download the currently-playing video file. The browser's
     * standard download dialog opens — no analytics, no XHR, no server
     * call. Works on every modern browser (uses the native `download`
     * attribute on a hidden `<a>` element).
     *
     * Set to `false` (default) to hide the row, e.g. for licensed
     * content that must stay streamed.
     *
     * @default false
     */
    downloadable?: boolean
    /**
     * Override the downloaded file name. Defaults to the trailing
     * segment of the source URL. Useful when the URL is opaque
     * (e.g. signed-S3 with a hash basename) and you want the file
     * to land in the user's Downloads folder with a clean name.
     */
    downloadFilename?: string
}
